### The Sheep Detectives

What if sheep are secretly smarter than you think? In The Sheep Detectives, life on the farm seems quiet, until a mystery turns everything upside down. While shepherd George unsuspectingly reads aloud from detective books, it turns out his sheep have been listening all along. So, it's time for action: they take the investigation into their own hands and dive into an adventure full of clues, suspicious characters, and clever discoveries.   
Showing at: Filmhallen, Pathé Cinemas, Het Ketelhuis, VUE // , Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé Cinemas  
Genre: Family, Comedy  
On view from: May 7, 2026

### In the Gray [...] ### Wasteman

A minor offense, but major consequences. Wasteman follows the life of Taylor, who is serving a long prison sentence and tries to survive primarily by remaining inconspicuous. He fills his days with small routines and memories of his son. But when early release suddenly comes within reach, a new, charismatic cellmate puts everything on edge.  
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, The Movies, Kriterion, LAB111, Rialto, Studio/K, FC Hyena   
Genre: Drama  
On view from: May 21, 2026

## Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026: more films to see in May

### The Devil Wears Prada 2 [...] ### In the Gray

A billion dollars, a ruthless adversary, and a mission that goes anything but according to plan. In the Grey follows a secret team of elite agents who are brought out of the shadows to right a massive heist. What begins as a tightly organized operation quickly turns into a dangerous game of deception, strategy, and survival.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, VUE // Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Action, Drama, Thriller  
On view from: May 14, 2026

### Merrily We Roll Along

#### Danique

# Amsterdam Film Agenda: 14 films to see in the cinema in May

Going to the cinema in Amsterdam? These are the best films (according to the Your Little Black Book team) to see in the cinema this month.

##### YLBB Notes

## Fancy a trip to the cinema in Amsterdam? Read on for the new film schedule for Amsterdam.

A movie afternoon or evening is always a good idea! Especially when there is a fun movie playing. In our new Amsterdam cinema schedule, you can discover the latest films to watch in the cinema in May. It's fun to do with girlfriends or as a date with your loved one. Order a nice big bucket of popcorn to go with it and your movie date can begin. Many films are premiering again this month, from comedies to thrillers and touching movies. Whatever your mood, there is bound to be a film playing to your taste! View the Amsterdam Cinema Schedule for the month of May here! And did you know that you can find last month's films via [Pathé Home](https://www.awin1.com/cread.php?awinmid=17314&awinaffid=373571&campaign=filmagenda+amsterdam) You can also just watch it from the comfort of your own home? Bonus tip :-).

Just back from Catalonia and I thought: I'll share some tips right away.  
  
During our road trip, we stayed two nights in the Ebro Delta. An incredibly fun stop for families and foodies, where there is plenty to do.  
  
• A boat trip to the Musclarium is a must! Here you eat oysters and mussels straight from the farm, in the middle of the sea.  
  
 · Admire the flamingos and black ibises that can be found here all year round.  
  
 · Rice is grown in the Ebro Delta all year round. For paella, but Kensho also makes sake and miso from it! You can visit them during a tasting.  
  
 Cycling along the rice fields, canoeing on the Ebro River, and visiting various beaches. It was still a bit too cold right now, but there is plenty to do.  
  
 • The towns are very low-key and not that exciting, but you can find fantastic local food there. From the Michelin-awarded Les Moles\* to local eateries like Vermuteria Bonmarisc and Lo Tinglado. We ate a lot of fish, but also anemone fritters (a first!).  
  
Now back to work, and I’m going to create an extensive travel guide of this road trip through Catalonia, but you’ll want to save these tips for a future adventure.  
  
✖️✖️✖️  
🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck   
  
{ In this post, I share tips for the Ebro Delta in Spain (Delta de l'Ebre). More tips for Catalonia can be found at www.yourlittleblackbook.me }

PUUR YONNIE, an ode to Jonnie Boer.  
  
Today I had the honor of being at @delibrijezwolle for the book launch of @jonnie\_en\_therese.  
I spent the whole evening reading and flipping through the pages.   
  
WHAT A BOOK.   
What stories.  
WHAT AN ODE.  
  
Love, Nature, Pioneer, Fatherhood, Music, Jonnie's Foundation, Friendship, Suppliers, Local products, Relaxation, Entrepreneurship, Humor & The pure human being.  
  
Everything passes by, beautifully written by @joel.broekaert.  
  
With contributions from, among others, @nelsontanate, @rekkebus, @ronblaauwtje, @jorisbijdendijk, @sidney\_schutte & @hvw043.  
  
Thanks for the lovely afternoon @jonnie\_en\_therese 💚  
  
PURE JONNIE,  
From 14 April   
at your local bookstore   
(and online)  
  
🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck

A cookbook from Grandma. Three Michelin stars. And a beautiful 70s villa in a quiet neighborhood in Bangkok, where you are welcomed as if you were at the chefs' home.  
  
The Sühring brothers do not cook based on trends; they cook from memories. Of their grandmother's farm, of the flavors of their youth in Germany, and of techniques passed down through generations.  
  
@relaischateaux invited me to experience Sühring during a solo lunch, and this is truly my ultimate definition of culinary self-care.  
  
This was a surprising afternoon in Bangkok, where I ate the best German food I have ever tasted. And that in a city where I normally avoid European restaurants. Well, for @restaurant\_suhring, you really want to make that exception.  
  
Signature dishes that I still think back to regularly?  
🍴 Labskaus with caviar  
🍴 Enleta waffle with Pfalz vinegar  
🍴 Game pie  
🍴 Dry aged Duck  
  
TIP: If you go to Sühring, reserve the seats at the kitchen bar so you have a view of the chefs at work!  
  
Follow @yourlbb for more tips you can trust.  
  
✖️✖️✖️  
🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck  
  
#prinvited #bangkok #michelin #relaischateaux

This is one of the nicest free outings in the spring! Did you know that you can gather (or pick, for the connoisseurs 😉) oysters yourself in various places in the Netherlands? I’m in Zeeland for a bit this weekend, and if we can, we’ll go again.  
  
📍In this video, I show where you can fill your bucket.   
  
Comment oester for the comprehensive guide with all details about all 8 locations + handy tips and rules.  
  
Important to know:  
 – Max. 10 kilos per person (≈ 1 bucket)  
 – For personal use only  
 – Do not scoop or scrape the soil  
 – Go at low tide  
  
Handy to take with you:  
Bucket, screwdriver, boots, work gloves, tea towel, clean clothes and garbage bags (for the oysters and dirty clothes).  
  
And of course: also bring an oyster knife, lemon and champagne 🍋🥂Because you want to taste something right away!   
  
The oyster season runs from September to about April / early May, so now that the water isn't so cold, this is the perfect outing.   
  
In the full guide, I share exactly where you can easily find them without much effort. Leave a comment and I'll send it to you right away!  
  
Who do you want to go oyster collecting with soon?   
  
✖️✖️✖️  
 🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck

## Watching movies with a twist: these are the best movie nights in Amsterdam

### Cinair Europe

Watching movies under the starry sky, but next level. At Cinair Europe, well-known films are screened at unique outdoor locations, such as historic buildings, gardens, or hidden spots you wouldn't normally discover. With a drink in your hand and headphones on (silent cinema style), it feels like stepping into another world for a moment. The exact locations and dates vary by edition. [so keep an eye on the website >>](https://www.cinair.eu/en)

### Movie night at the Supperclub

If you're in the mood for a movie night with just that little bit extra, then you have to be at [Supper](https://supper.nl/movienights/) are. Every Tuesday evening, they transform the restaurant into an intimate cinema with great classics on the screen, while you sink into one of their iconic white lounge beds. Think: cozy atmosphere, tasty bar bites, and drinks within reach. Book early, because these movie nights are popular!   
Order date:  
– April 28 – Focus  
– May 12 – The Tourist

### Cinema Culinary

Some films aren't just meant to be seen, they're meant to be tasted. At Cinema Culinair, you don't just watch films like Chef, Ratatouille, or The Menu; you eat exactly what you see on the screen or what is inspired by it! Served precisely to the second, in the form of a brunch or a 7-course dinner. Think: the scene starts, the dish arrives. Enjoyment with all your senses. Perfect for an original brunch date or a culinary evening out. [View the full Cinema Culinair film schedule here >>](https://cinemaculinair.nl)

## Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026: 5 X New in May

### A Useful Ghost

Sometimes love returns in the most unexpected forms. In A Useful Ghost, March is inconsolable after the loss of his wife, until she suddenly reappears… as a ghost inside a vacuum cleaner. Sounds absurd? It is, but in a charming way. While his family grapples with this bizarre situation, Nat turns out to be surprisingly handy and even helps the family business out of trouble.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Filmhallen, Kriterion, Rialto, FC Hyena, The Movies  
Genre: Drama, Comedy  
On view from: May 7, 2026

### The Sheep Detectives

What if sheep are secretly smarter than you think? In The Sheep Detectives, life on the farm seems quiet, until a mystery turns everything upside down. While shepherd George unsuspectingly reads aloud from detective books, it turns out his sheep have been listening all along. So, it's time for action: they take the investigation into their own hands and dive into an adventure full of clues, suspicious characters, and clever discoveries.   
Showing at: Filmhallen, Pathé Cinemas, Het Ketelhuis, VUE // , Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé Cinemas  
Genre: Family, Comedy  
On view from: May 7, 2026

### In the Gray

A billion dollars, a ruthless adversary, and a mission that goes anything but according to plan. In the Grey follows a secret team of elite agents who are brought out of the shadows to right a massive heist. What begins as a tightly organized operation quickly turns into a dangerous game of deception, strategy, and survival.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, VUE // Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Action, Drama, Thriller  
On view from: May 14, 2026

### Merrily We Roll Along

What happens to friendship when success and ambition get in the way? Merrily We Roll Along follows the bond between composer Franklin and his two best friends, writer Charley and Mary, over a period of thirty years. What once began as a close friendship slowly falls out of balance due to choices, career, and life itself.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, VUE // Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Music, Drama, Thriller  
On view from: May 14, 2026

### Wasteman

A minor offense, but major consequences. Wasteman follows the life of Taylor, who is serving a long prison sentence and tries to survive primarily by remaining inconspicuous. He fills his days with small routines and memories of his son. But when early release suddenly comes within reach, a new, charismatic cellmate puts everything on edge.  
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, The Movies, Kriterion, LAB111, Rialto, Studio/K, FC Hyena   
Genre: Drama  
On view from: May 21, 2026

## Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026: more films to see in May

### The Devil Wears Prada 2

Okay, so this is one I'm really looking forward to. Twenty years after the first part, Miranda Priestly, Andy, and the rest are back in a completely revamped fashion world where everything is faster, more digital, and more ruthless than ever. How they hold their own? You'll want to be there. Tip: watch part 1 again on [Disney +](https://www.disneyplus.com/en-pt/browse/entity-35ee3632-230a-4e4b-8520-46063f9282a3) of [Prime Video](https://www.primevideo.com/detail/The-Devil-Wears-Prada/0TZUGTVNJUZ5PN2X7WHS6I15H5) For the ultimate throwback. One thing is certain: this will be \*the\* film for fashion lovers and fans from the very beginning.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Drama, comedy  
On view from: April 30, 2026

### Michael

Michael Jackson may be gone, but his influence is still felt everywhere. In the new film Michael, you dive into the life of the King of Pop: from his breakthrough with the Jackson Five to his legendary solo career. The film showcases not only his greatest hits but also the man behind the music. With impressive performances and iconic moments, you get the feeling as if you have a front-row seat to his story. A must-see for fans of Michael's music and his legacy.   
Showing at: VUE Amsterdam, The Movies, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Biography, drama

## Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026: more films to see in May

### The Drama

What should have been a dream week leading up to your wedding changes completely when a big secret from the past comes to light. In The Drama, we follow Charlie and Emma. Their relationship is suddenly put under immense strain as the big day draws ever closer. Starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, you get an intense and uncomfortably honest love story about trust, doubt, and the question: how well do you really know someone?   
Showing at: VUE Amsterdam, The Movies, Studio/K, De Filmhallen, EYE, FC Hyena, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Romance, comedy and drama

### A Family

In A Family, we follow teenagers Eli and Nina, who each try to cope in their own way with their parents' (Carice van Houten and Pieter Embrechts) acrimonious divorce. As they are torn between loyalty, anger, and grief, they both search for stability. A poignant and realistic family drama that shows what happens when love comes under pressure.   
Showing at: VUE Amsterdam, Rialto, Kriterion, Het Ketelhuis, De Filmhallen, EYE, FC Hyena, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Drama

## Amsterdam Cinema Agenda May 2026: going to the movies with the kids

### The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

Is your mini-me going through a Mario phase too? Then you definitely want to add this one to your list. In The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, Mario trades the Mushroom Kingdom for an adventure in space, full of new worlds, challenges, and, of course, familiar faces. Following the success of the previous film, this sequel promises to be just as fun and action-packed.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Studio/K, Het Ketelhuis, De Filmhallen  
Genre: Adventure, animation

### Tafiti – Across the Desert

A meerkat and an aardvark as best friends? According to Grandpa, that’s simply impossible. But when Tafiti’s grandpa is in mortal danger, he simply \*must\* head into the desert in search of a rare flower. Against his will, he is joined by the cheerful and slightly clumsy Borstel. What follows is an exciting and funny adventure in which they discover that differences sometimes make for the best friendships.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Het Ketelhuis  
Genre: Animation, kids

An incredibly fun stop for families and foodies alike, where there is plenty to do. · A boat trip to the Musclarium is a must! Here you eat oysters and mussels straight from the farm, in the middle of the sea. · Admire the flamingos and black ibises that can be found here all year round. · Rice is grown in the Ebro Delta all year round. For paella, but Kensho also makes sake and miso from it! You can visit them during a tasting. · Cycling along the rice fields, canoeing on the Ebro River, and visiting various beaches. It was still a bit too cold now, but there is plenty to do. · The towns are very low-key and not that exciting, but you can find fantastic local food there. From the Michelin-awarded Les Moles* to local eateries like Vermuteria Bonmarisc and Lo Tinglado. We ate a lot of fish, but also anemone fritters (a first!). Prices are very reasonable here! 📍 Eetcafé de Reiger Jordaan, Amsterdam City Center Save this spot for when you're craving warm Dutch traditional dishes in the heart of Amsterdam. ![A grandma's cookbook. Three Michelin stars. And a beautiful 70s villa in a quiet neighborhood in Bangkok, where you are welcomed as if you were at the chefs' home. The Sühring brothers don't cook based on trends; they cook based on memories. Of their grandmother's farm, of the flavors of their youth in Germany, and of techniques passed down through generations. @relaischateaux invited me to experience Sühring during a solo lunch, and this is truly my ultimate definition of culinary self-care. This was a surprising afternoon in Bangkok, where I ate the best German food I have ever tasted. And that in a city where I normally avoid European restaurants. Well, for @restaurant_suhring, you really want to make that exception. Signature dishes that I still think back to regularly? 🍴 Labskaus with caviar 🍴 Enleta waffle with Pfalz vinegar 🍴 Game pâté 🍴 Dry-aged Duck TIP: When you go to Sühring, reserve seats at the kitchen bar so you have a view of the chefs at work! Follow @yourlbb for more tips you can trust. ✖️✖️✖️ 🖊️ @anne_de_buck #prinvited #bangkok #michelin #relaischateaux In this reels, I take you along for fine dining in Bangkok at the three-Michelin-starred restaurant Sühring.](https://www.yourlittleblackbook.me/wp-content/plugins/instagram-feed-pro/img/placeholder.png)

[yourlbb](https://www.instagram.com/yourlbb/)

![This is one of the best free outings in the spring! Did you know that you can gather (or pick, for the connoisseurs 😉) oysters yourself at various locations in the Netherlands? I’m in Zeeland for a bit this weekend, and if we can make it, we’ll go again. 📍In this video, I show you where you can fill your bucket. Comment *oester* for the comprehensive guide with all the details about all 8 locations + handy tips and rules. Important to know: – Max. 10 kilos per person (≈ 1 bucket) – For personal use only – Do not scoop or scrape the seabed – Go at low tide Handy things to bring: Bucket, screwdriver, boots, work gloves, tea towel, clean clothes, and garbage bags (for the oysters *and* dirty clothes). And of course: bring an oyster knife, lemon, and champagne too 🍋🥂 Because you’ll want to taste something right away, won’t you! The oyster season runs from September to about April / early May, so now that the water isn't so cold, this is the perfect outing. In the full guide, I share exactly where you can easily find them, without much effort. Comment oyster and I'll send it to you right away! Who do you want to go oyster gathering with soon? ✖️✖️✖️ 🖊️ @anne_de_buck](https://www.yourlittleblackbook.me/wp-content/plugins/instagram-feed-pro/img/placeholder.png)

hits.title: Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026 // Your Little Black Book
hits.snippet:
### The Sheep Detectives

What if sheep are secretly smarter than you think? In The Sheep Detectives, life on the farm seems quiet, until a mystery turns everything upside down. While shepherd George unsuspectingly reads aloud from detective books, it turns out his sheep have been listening all along. So, it's time for action: they take the investigation into their own hands and dive into an adventure full of clues, suspicious characters, and clever discoveries.   
Showing at: Filmhallen, Pathé Cinemas, Het Ketelhuis, VUE // , Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé Cinemas  
Genre: Family, Comedy  
On view from: May 7, 2026

### In the Gray [...] ### Wasteman

A minor offense, but major consequences. Wasteman follows the life of Taylor, who is serving a long prison sentence and tries to survive primarily by remaining inconspicuous. He fills his days with small routines and memories of his son. But when early release suddenly comes within reach, a new, charismatic cellmate puts everything on edge.  
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, The Movies, Kriterion, LAB111, Rialto, Studio/K, FC Hyena   
Genre: Drama  
On view from: May 21, 2026

## Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026: more films to see in May

### The Devil Wears Prada 2 [...] ### In the Gray

A billion dollars, a ruthless adversary, and a mission that goes anything but according to plan. In the Grey follows a secret team of elite agents who are brought out of the shadows to right a massive heist. What begins as a tightly organized operation quickly turns into a dangerous game of deception, strategy, and survival.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, VUE // Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Action, Drama, Thriller  
On view from: May 14, 2026

### Merrily We Roll Along

### Seek and find

#### Danique

# Amsterdam Film Agenda: 14 films to see in the cinema in May

Going to the cinema in Amsterdam? These are the best films (according to the Your Little Black Book team) to see in the cinema this month.

##### YLBB Notes

## Fancy a trip to the cinema in Amsterdam? Read on for the new film schedule for Amsterdam.

A movie afternoon or evening is always a good idea! Especially when there is a fun movie playing. In our new Amsterdam cinema schedule, you can discover the latest films to watch in the cinema in May. It's fun to do with girlfriends or as a date with your loved one. Order a nice big bucket of popcorn to go with it and your movie date can begin. Many films are premiering again this month, from comedies to thrillers and touching movies. Whatever your mood, there is bound to be a film playing to your taste! View the Amsterdam Cinema Schedule for the month of May here! And did you know that you can find last month's films via [Pathé Home](https://www.awin1.com/cread.php?awinmid=17314&awinaffid=373571&campaign=filmagenda+amsterdam) You can also just watch it from the comfort of your own home? Bonus tip :-).

#### Follow @yourlbb on Instagram

From a brand-new rooftop beach in Oost to vintage treasure hunting at IJ-Hallen, Italian market mornings, new restaurants and lots of May holiday plans for families.

And yes, market season is fully back. Sunday Market, IJ-Hallen, NoordOogst and Zi Camì Market… where to start?

Just back from Catalonia and I thought: I'll share some tips right away.  
  
During our road trip, we stayed two nights in the Ebro Delta. An incredibly fun stop for families and foodies, where there is plenty to do.  
  
• A boat trip to the Musclarium is a must! Here you eat oysters and mussels straight from the farm, in the middle of the sea.  
  
 · Admire the flamingos and black ibises that can be found here all year round.  
  
 · Rice is grown in the Ebro Delta all year round. For paella, but Kensho also makes sake and miso from it! You can visit them during a tasting.  
  
 Cycling along the rice fields, canoeing on the Ebro River, and visiting various beaches. It was still a bit too cold right now, but there is plenty to do.  
  
 • The towns are very low-key and not that exciting, but you can find fantastic local food there. From the Michelin-awarded Les Moles\* to local eateries like Vermuteria Bonmarisc and Lo Tinglado. We ate a lot of fish, but also anemone fritters (a first!).  
  
Now back to work, and I’m going to create an extensive travel guide of this road trip through Catalonia, but you’ll want to save these tips for a future adventure.  
  
✖️✖️✖️  
🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck

A cookbook from Grandma. Three Michelin stars. And a beautiful 70s villa in a quiet neighborhood in Bangkok, where you are welcomed as if you were at the chefs' home.  
  
The Sühring brothers do not cook based on trends; they cook from memories. Of their grandmother's farm, of the flavors of their youth in Germany, and of techniques passed down through generations.  
  
@relaischateaux invited me to experience Sühring during a solo lunch, and this is truly my ultimate definition of culinary self-care.  
  
This was a surprising afternoon in Bangkok, where I ate the best German food I have ever tasted. And that in a city where I normally avoid European restaurants. Well, for @restaurant\_suhring, you really want to make that exception.  
  
Signature dishes that I still think back to regularly?  
🍴 Labskaus with caviar  
🍴 Enleta waffle with Pfalz vinegar  
🍴 Game pie  
🍴 Dry aged Duck  
  
TIP: If you go to Sühring, reserve the seats at the kitchen bar so you have a view of the chefs at work!  
  
Follow @yourlbb for more tips you can trust.  
  
✖️✖️✖️  
🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck

This is one of the nicest free outings in the spring! Did you know that you can gather (or pick, for the connoisseurs 😉) oysters yourself in various places in the Netherlands? I’m in Zeeland for a bit this weekend, and if we can, we’ll go again.  
  
📍In this video, I show where you can fill your bucket.   
  
Comment oester for the comprehensive guide with all details about all 8 locations + handy tips and rules.  
  
Important to know:  
 – Max. 10 kilos per person (≈ 1 bucket)  
 – For personal use only  
 – Do not scoop or scrape the soil  
 – Go at low tide  
  
Handy to take with you:  
Bucket, screwdriver, boots, work gloves, tea towel, clean clothes and garbage bags (for the oysters and dirty clothes).  
  
And of course: also bring an oyster knife, lemon and champagne 🍋🥂Because you want to taste something right away!   
  
The oyster season runs from September to about April / early May, so now that the water isn't so cold, this is the perfect outing.   
  
In the full guide, I share exactly where you can easily find them without much effort. Leave a comment and I'll send it to you right away!

## Watching movies with a twist: these are the best movie nights in Amsterdam

### Cinair Europe

Watching movies under the starry sky, but next level. At Cinair Europe, well-known films are screened at unique outdoor locations, such as historic buildings, gardens, or hidden spots you wouldn't normally discover. With a drink in your hand and headphones on (silent cinema style), it feels like stepping into another world for a moment. The exact locations and dates vary by edition. [so keep an eye on the website >>](https://www.cinair.eu/en)

### Movie night at the Supperclub

If you're in the mood for a movie night with just that little bit extra, then you have to be at [Supper](https://supper.nl/movienights/) are. Every Tuesday evening, they transform the restaurant into an intimate cinema with great classics on the screen, while you sink into one of their iconic white lounge beds. Think: cozy atmosphere, tasty bar bites, and drinks within reach. Book early, because these movie nights are popular!   
Order date:  
– April 28 – Focus  
– May 12 – The Tourist

### Cinema Culinary

Some films aren't just meant to be seen, they're meant to be tasted. At Cinema Culinair, you don't just watch films like Chef, Ratatouille, or The Menu; you eat exactly what you see on the screen or what is inspired by it! Served precisely to the second, in the form of a brunch or a 7-course dinner. Think: the scene starts, the dish arrives. Enjoyment with all your senses. Perfect for an original brunch date or a culinary evening out. [View the full Cinema Culinair film schedule here >>](https://cinemaculinair.nl)

![Amsterdam cinema agenda ](https://www.yourlittleblackbook.me/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/bioscoopagenda-amsterdam-cinema-culinair-1.jpg)

Cinema Culinary © Your Little Black Book

## Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026: 5 X New in May

### A Useful Ghost

Sometimes love returns in the most unexpected forms. In A Useful Ghost, March is inconsolable after the loss of his wife, until she suddenly reappears… as a ghost inside a vacuum cleaner. Sounds absurd? It is, but in a charming way. While his family grapples with this bizarre situation, Nat turns out to be surprisingly handy and even helps the family business out of trouble.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Filmhallen, Kriterion, Rialto, FC Hyena, The Movies  
Genre: Drama, Comedy  
On view from: May 7, 2026

### The Sheep Detectives

What if sheep are secretly smarter than you think? In The Sheep Detectives, life on the farm seems quiet, until a mystery turns everything upside down. While shepherd George unsuspectingly reads aloud from detective books, it turns out his sheep have been listening all along. So, it's time for action: they take the investigation into their own hands and dive into an adventure full of clues, suspicious characters, and clever discoveries.   
Showing at: Filmhallen, Pathé Cinemas, Het Ketelhuis, VUE // , Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé Cinemas  
Genre: Family, Comedy  
On view from: May 7, 2026

### In the Gray

A billion dollars, a ruthless adversary, and a mission that goes anything but according to plan. In the Grey follows a secret team of elite agents who are brought out of the shadows to right a massive heist. What begins as a tightly organized operation quickly turns into a dangerous game of deception, strategy, and survival.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, VUE // Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Action, Drama, Thriller  
On view from: May 14, 2026

### Merrily We Roll Along

What happens to friendship when success and ambition get in the way? Merrily We Roll Along follows the bond between composer Franklin and his two best friends, writer Charley and Mary, over a period of thirty years. What once began as a close friendship slowly falls out of balance due to choices, career, and life itself.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, VUE // Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Music, Drama, Thriller  
On view from: May 14, 2026

### Wasteman

A minor offense, but major consequences. Wasteman follows the life of Taylor, who is serving a long prison sentence and tries to survive primarily by remaining inconspicuous. He fills his days with small routines and memories of his son. But when early release suddenly comes within reach, a new, charismatic cellmate puts everything on edge.  
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, The Movies, Kriterion, LAB111, Rialto, Studio/K, FC Hyena   
Genre: Drama  
On view from: May 21, 2026

## Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026: more films to see in May

### The Devil Wears Prada 2

Okay, so this is one I'm really looking forward to. Twenty years after the first part, Miranda Priestly, Andy, and the rest are back in a completely revamped fashion world where everything is faster, more digital, and more ruthless than ever. How they hold their own? You'll want to be there. Tip: watch part 1 again on [Disney +](https://www.disneyplus.com/en-pt/browse/entity-35ee3632-230a-4e4b-8520-46063f9282a3) of [Prime Video](https://www.primevideo.com/detail/The-Devil-Wears-Prada/0TZUGTVNJUZ5PN2X7WHS6I15H5) For the ultimate throwback. One thing is certain: this will be \*the\* film for fashion lovers and fans from the very beginning.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Drama, comedy  
On view from: April 30, 2026

### Michael

Michael Jackson may be gone, but his influence is still felt everywhere. In the new film Michael, you dive into the life of the King of Pop: from his breakthrough with the Jackson Five to his legendary solo career. The film showcases not only his greatest hits but also the man behind the music. With impressive performances and iconic moments, you get the feeling as if you have a front-row seat to his story. A must-see for fans of Michael's music and his legacy.   
Showing at: VUE Amsterdam, The Movies, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Biography, drama

## Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026: more films to see in May

### The Drama

What should have been a dream week leading up to your wedding changes completely when a big secret from the past comes to light. In The Drama, we follow Charlie and Emma. Their relationship is suddenly put under immense strain as the big day draws ever closer. Starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, you get an intense and uncomfortably honest love story about trust, doubt, and the question: how well do you really know someone?   
Showing at: VUE Amsterdam, The Movies, Studio/K, De Filmhallen, EYE, FC Hyena, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Romance, comedy and drama

### A Family

In A Family, we follow teenagers Eli and Nina, who each try to cope in their own way with their parents' (Carice van Houten and Pieter Embrechts) acrimonious divorce. As they are torn between loyalty, anger, and grief, they both search for stability. A poignant and realistic family drama that shows what happens when love comes under pressure.   
Showing at: VUE Amsterdam, Rialto, Kriterion, Het Ketelhuis, De Filmhallen, EYE, FC Hyena, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Drama

## Amsterdam Cinema Agenda May 2026: going to the movies with the kids

### The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

Is your mini-me going through a Mario phase too? Then you definitely want to add this one to your list. In The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, Mario trades the Mushroom Kingdom for an adventure in space, full of new worlds, challenges, and, of course, familiar faces. Following the success of the previous film, this sequel promises to be just as fun and action-packed.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Studio/K, Het Ketelhuis, De Filmhallen  
Genre: Adventure, animation

### Tafiti – Across the Desert

A meerkat and an aardvark as best friends? According to Grandpa, that’s simply impossible. But when Tafiti’s grandpa is in mortal danger, he simply \*must\* head into the desert in search of a rare flower. Against his will, he is joined by the cheerful and slightly clumsy Borstel. What follows is an exciting and funny adventure in which they discover that differences sometimes make for the best friendships.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Het Ketelhuis  
Genre: Animation, kids

![Just back from Catalonia and I thought: I'll share some tips right away. During our road trip, we stayed two nights in the Ebro Delta. An incredibly fun stop for families and foodies alike, where there is plenty to do. · A boat trip to the Musclarium is a must! Here you eat oysters and mussels straight from the farm, in the middle of the sea. · Admire the flamingos and black ibises that can be found here all year round. · Rice is grown in the Ebro Delta all year round. For paella, but Kensho also makes sake and miso from it! You can visit them during a tasting. · Cycling along the rice fields, canoeing on the Ebro River, and visiting various beaches. It was still a bit too cold now, but there is plenty to do. · The towns are very low-key and not that exciting, but you can find fantastic local food there. From the Michelin-awarded Les Moles* to local eateries like Vermuteria Bonmarisc and Lo Tinglado. We ate a lot of fish, but also anemone fritters (a first!). Now back to work, and I’m going to create an extensive travel guide of this road trip through Catalonia, but you’ll want to save these tips for a future adventure. ✖️✖️✖️ 🖊️ @anne_de_buck { In this post, I share tips for the Ebro Delta in Spain (Delta de l'Ebre). ![A grandma's cookbook. Three Michelin stars. And a beautiful 70s villa in a quiet neighborhood in Bangkok, where you are welcomed as if you were at the chefs' home. The Sühring brothers don't cook based on trends; they cook based on memories. Of their grandmother's farm, of the flavors of their youth in Germany, and of techniques passed down through generations. @relaischateaux invited me to experience Sühring during a solo lunch, and this is truly my ultimate definition of culinary self-care. This was a surprising afternoon in Bangkok, where I ate the best German food I have ever tasted. And that in a city where I normally avoid European restaurants. Well, for @restaurant_suhring, you really want to make that exception. Signature dishes that I still think back to regularly? 🍴 Labskaus with caviar 🍴 Enleta waffle with Pfalz vinegar 🍴 Game pâté 🍴 Dry-aged Duck TIP: When you go to Sühring, reserve seats at the kitchen bar so you have a view of the chefs at work! ![This is one of the best free outings in the spring! Did you know that you can gather (or pick, for the connoisseurs 😉) oysters yourself at various locations in the Netherlands? I’m in Zeeland for a bit this weekend, and if we can make it, we’ll go again. 📍In this video, I show you where you can fill your bucket. Comment *oester* for the comprehensive guide with all the details about all 8 locations + handy tips and rules. Important to know: – Max. 10 kilos per person (≈ 1 bucket) – For personal use only – Do not scoop or scrape the seabed – Go at low tide Handy things to bring: Bucket, screwdriver, boots, work gloves, tea towel, clean clothes, and garbage bags (for the oysters *and* dirty clothes). And of course: bring an oyster knife, lemon, and champagne too 🍋🥂 Because you’ll want to taste something right away, won’t you! The oyster season runs from September to about April / early May, so now that the water isn't so cold, this is the perfect outing. In the full guide, I share exactly where you can easily find them, without much effort. Comment oyster and I'll send it to you right away! Who do you want to go oyster gathering with soon? ✖️✖️✖️ 🖊️ @anne_de_buck](https://www.yourlittleblackbook.me/wp-content/plugins/instagram-feed-pro/img/placeholder.png)

This week's guide is packed because of Easter weekend and I saved the best tips for you in the guide on 𝗬𝗼𝘂𝗿𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘁𝗹𝗲𝗯𝗹𝗮𝗰𝗸𝗯𝗼𝗼𝗸.𝗺𝗲. 💬 Comment 𝗪𝗘𝗘𝗞𝗘𝗡𝗗 and I'll send you the full Amsterdam Weekend Guide with all 79 tips straight to your DMs. What's your vibe this weekend? Markets, Easter lunches, or a festival? ✖️✖️✖️ 🖊️ @anne_de_buck](https://www.yourlittleblackbook.me/wp-content/plugins/instagram-feed-pro/img/placeholder.png)

![This is the time to make plans for Easter in Amsterdam 👀🐣🐰 Are you team lunch or dinner? ✖️✖️✖️ 🖊️ @anne_de_buck Ps: More tips for Easter in Amsterdam, from restaurants to markets, on WWW.YOURLITTLEBLACKBOOK.ME](https://www.yourlittleblackbook.me/wp-content/plugins/instagram-feed-pro/img/placeholder.png)

Think: delicious food, nice hotels, coffee shops for your matcha ;-) and wine bars you actually don't want to leave. During our last trip, we added a few new favorites to the Antwerp Little Black Book. Save this guide for your next city trip to Antwerp. Have fun! Coffee to-go in hand, and just head out as soon as the sun shows up. 📍 Bloesempark – Amsterdamse Bos (the classic, yes it gets busy, but totally worth it) 📍 Rijksmuseum 📍 Fruit Garden of West 📍 Westerpark 📍 Jozef Israëlskade 📍 WG site 📍 Nelson Mandelapark 📍 Bos en Lommerplantsoen 📍Amstelpark By mid-April most of the sakura will already be gone, so if you want to catch it: this is your moment. From a free event to try new Limited Subs at @ijveramsterdam to slow mornings, a market stroll (or two), maybe finally ticking off that new restaurant that's been on your list for weeks… and ending somewhere with a good glass of wine while telling yourself “just one more” 😉 𝗡𝗲𝘄-𝗶𝘀𝗵 𝗶𝗻 𝗔𝗺𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗱𝗮𝗺: 📍 @caferestaurant.meyer 📍 @kamer.bar 📍 @rondo.restaurant 📍 @weinlokalstern 📍 @enotecadeolyphant 📍 @tonton.noord 📍 @yusu.coffee (centre) 📍 @eetcafe.vanbeeren 📍 @kidamsterdam 💬 Comment 𝗪𝗘𝗘𝗞𝗘𝗡𝗗 and I'll send you the full Amsterdam Weekend Guide with 47 tips straight to your DMs. What's your vibe this weekend… markets, cocktails or a new hotspot? ✖️✖️✖️ 🖊️ @anne_de_buck](https://www.yourlittleblackbook.me/wp-content/plugins/instagram-feed-pro/img/placeholder.png)

I have massive spring fever, so it's time for a mini-escape: everything is within walking distance, there are lovely coffee shops, boutiques for shopping, and enough gems to discover in 48 hours ✨ Which city is at the top of your list for a spring getaway? ## Rialto

### A Useful Ghost

After her husband’s death, Suman inherits a factory whose appliances are haunted by ghosts, including her late employee Tok and her daughter-in-law Nat, who now lives inside a vacuum cleaner. This comedy-drama, the debut of director Ratchapoom Boonbunchachoke, combines imaginative storylines with dark humor, won the Grand Prix at Cannes, and is reminiscent of the style of Yorgos Lanthimos.

Address: Ceintuurbaan 338 (Google Maps)  
Info & tickets: www.rialtofilm.nl

## LAB111

### Obsession [...] ## LAB111

### Obsession

In ‘Obsession’, hopeless romantic Bear turns to dark magic to win back the love of his childhood friend Nikki, with unexpected and grim consequences. Director Curry Barker shows how obsessive love can turn into horror, as the film balances between black humor and a dark nightmare.

Address: Arie Biemondstraat 111 (Google Maps)  
Info & tickets: www.lab111.nl

## FilmHallen

### The Tasters

In the fall of 1943, Rosa moves to the countryside, where she discovers that the village near Hitler’s Wolf’s Lair is still dangerous. Along with other women, she is forced to perform the dangerous task of tasting Hitler’s meals to prevent him from being poisoned. The story is based on true events. [...] Address: Hannie Dankbaarpassage 12 (Google Maps)  
Info & tickets: www.filmhallen.nl

## Het Ketelhuis

### Tre Ciotole

Marta, a gym teacher in Rome, is trying to get her life back on track after a sudden breakup with her partner, Antonio. While she finds support in her work and a colleague, she discovers that her loss of appetite hides a deeper secret, which changes her outlook on life.

Location: Pazzanistraat 4 (Google Maps)  
Info & tickets: www.ketelhuis.nl

## The Movies

### The Devil Wears Prada 2

Twenty years after their iconic roles, Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt, and Stanley Tucci return in ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’. In this highly anticipated sequel, their characters once again find themselves facing the rapidly changing world of fashion and media.

# Film agenda may 2026 Amsterdam: special movies

![Film agenda may 2026 Amsterdam: special movies](https://amsterdamspotted.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/IMG_0339-optimized.jpeg)

Discover the film program for Amsterdam for May 2026, featuring a carefully curated selection of special and exclusive films. From classics to popular blockbusters, discover ***[unique cinemas](https://amsterdamspotted.com/amsterdams-most-extraordinary-cinemas/)*** in Amsterdam. Stay up to date with the latest films so you don’t miss this month’s most notable film highlights in Amsterdam!

## Eye Filmmuseum

### Films, Talks & Events 2001 A Time Capsule

#### Until 14 June

With the film program *2001 – A Time Capsule*, Eye Filmmuseum celebrates the 25th anniversary of the year 2001 with a touch of nostalgia, featuring 25 films released that year.

**Address**: IJpromenade 1 ([Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/TYxMPzxusVt68Zxn8))  
**Info & tickets**: [www.eyefilm.nl](https://www.eyefilm.nl/programma/2001-a-time-capsule/1600279)

## Rialto

### A Useful Ghost

After her husband’s death, Suman inherits a factory whose appliances are haunted by ghosts, including her late employee Tok and her daughter-in-law Nat, who now lives inside a vacuum cleaner. This comedy-drama, the debut of director Ratchapoom Boonbunchachoke, combines imaginative storylines with dark humor, won the Grand Prix at Cannes, and is reminiscent of the style of Yorgos Lanthimos.

**Address**: Ceintuurbaan 338 ([Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/qYsjcN29K487gLFA8))  
**Info & tickets**: [www.rialtofilm.nl](https://rialtofilm.nl/nl/films/2711/a-useful-ghost-eng-subs)

## LAB111

### Obsession

In ‘Obsession’, hopeless romantic Bear turns to dark magic to win back the love of his childhood friend Nikki, with unexpected and grim consequences. Director Curry Barker shows how obsessive love can turn into horror, as the film balances between black humor and a dark nightmare.

**Address**: Arie Biemondstraat 111 ([Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/dx68kjQqhf7CRSfj9))  
**Info & tickets**: [www.lab111.nl](https://www.lab111.nl/movie/obsession/)

## FilmHallen

### The Tasters

In the fall of 1943, Rosa moves to the countryside, where she discovers that the village near Hitler’s Wolf’s Lair is still dangerous. Along with other women, she is forced to perform the dangerous task of tasting Hitler’s meals to prevent him from being poisoned. The story is based on true events.

**Address**: Hannie Dankbaarpassage 12 ([Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/T9WFB8mnicZXUyKk6))  
**Info & tickets**: [www.filmhallen.nl](https://filmhallen.nl/films/the-tasters/)

## Het Ketelhuis

### Tre Ciotole

Marta, a gym teacher in Rome, is trying to get her life back on track after a sudden breakup with her partner, Antonio. While she finds support in her work and a colleague, she discovers that her loss of appetite hides a deeper secret, which changes her outlook on life.

**Location**: Pazzanistraat 4 ([Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/K4kmmHLLVVc5htEf7))  
**Info & tickets**: [www.ketelhuis.nl](https://www.ketelhuis.nl/films/tre-ciotole/)

## The Movies

### The Devil Wears Prada 2

Twenty years after their iconic roles, Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt, and Stanley Tucci return in ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’. In this highly anticipated sequel, their characters once again find themselves facing the rapidly changing world of fashion and media.

**Address**: Haarlemmerdijk 161 ([Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/sAAPBAhXX2BBHTgm8))  
**Info & tickets**: [www.themovies.nl](https://themovies.nl/films/the-devil-wears-prada-2/)

## Kriterion

### URCHIN

In his directorial debut, Harris Dickinson follows the life of Mike, a homeless drug addict who tries to get his life back on track after prison. Despite a job, a roof over his head, and new love, London does not offer him the peace he seeks. Urchin is a raw and compelling character study that portrays the harsh life on the streets and the failing system in London.

**Address**: Roetersstraat 170 ([Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/Bcd4rEpnDrgQpLbS9))  
**Info & tickets**: [www.kriterion.nl](https://www.kriterion.nl/films/urchin)

## Cinecenter

#### Eng Subs: Sentimental Value

Sisters Nora and Agnes are reunited with their estranged father, director Gustav, who offers Nora a role in his comeback film. After she turns down the role and it is given to a young Hollywood star, the sisters must try to salvage their complicated relationship with their father, while also dealing with a new addition to their family drama.

**Address**: Korte Leidsedwarsstraat 10 ([Google Maps](https://maps.app.goo.gl/xPcYueBZteMsKc8f8))  
**Info & tickets**: [www.cinecenter.nl](https://cinecenter.nl/film/eng-subs-sentimental-value/)

Our festival in Amsterdam may have come to an end, but CinemAsia’s journey continues. **CinemAsia On Tour** brings some of the festival favourites, as well as, two newly programmed films to different cities in the Netherlands. Join us in May and June at **Filmhuis Den Haag**, **Kino Rotterdam**, **Lumière Maastricht** and **Forum Groningen** to celebrate Asian Cinema. Let’s see, connect and discover through Asian voices!

hits.title: What to Do in Amsterdam in 2026: A Guide to Main Events ...
hits.snippet:
Dance Valley  — 8 AugustDance Valley is a large electronic music festival held near Amsterdam. Known for its long history, it attracts international DJs and thousands of visitors each year.  
  
Grachtenfestival – 7-16 August, city centreThe Grachtenfestival is a classical and contemporary music festival held at unique locations, including canal-side venues and boats. It aims to make classical music accessible to wider audiences.  
  
Open Air Film Festival Pluk de Nacht – Date TBAPluk de Nacht is an open-air film festival showing independent and lesser-known films. Screenings take place outdoors, often in informal settings, and are free to attend.

### Events in Amsterdam in September 2026 [...] IDFA – 12-22 November, various locationsIDFA, the International Documentary Film Festival Amsterdam, is one of the world’s leading documentary festivals and a highlight of the city’s cultural calendar. Each November, venues across Amsterdam screen hundreds of documentaries from around the globe, covering social, political, and personal stories. In addition to screenings, the festival offers workshops, debates, and networking events for filmmakers and audiences alike. For locals and visitors, IDFA is a chance to explore compelling real-life stories and discover new perspectives on the world.  
  
PAN Amsterdam – 1-8 November at RAIPAN Amsterdam is an art, antiques, and design fair showcasing a wide range of periods and styles, from classical art to modern design. [...] Amsterdam_Marathon_2014

Photo Wikipedia

Cinekid Festival – 10-25 October, various locationsCinekid is one of the world’s largest children’s film and media festivals. It includes films, workshops, and interactive installations.  
  
Amsterdam Dance Event (ADE) – 21-25 October, various locationsADE is the world’s leading electronic music conference and festival. It combines industry events with hundreds of performances across the city.

### Events in Amsterdam in November 2026

Museum Night — 7 NovemberMuseum Night allows visitors to explore museums late into the night with special programmes, performances, and talks. It’s particularly popular with younger audiences. You can use your Museum Card if you have one.
