# What to Do in Amsterdam in 2026: A Guide to Main Events Throughout the Year

Posted by [Dana Marin](https://amsterdamian.com/author/dana/ "Posts by Dana Marin") | Jan 9, 2026 | [All Photos](https://amsterdamian.com/category/see/photos/), [Amsterdam](https://amsterdamian.com/category/see/amsterdam/), [Culture and Events](https://amsterdamian.com/category/see/cultural-amsterdam/) | [0](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/what-to-do-in-amsterdam-a-guide-to-main-events-throughout-the-year/#comments)

Amsterdam has a very particular rhythm. It’s shaped by seasons, light, weather, and, just as much, by the events that return year after year. Some take over the streets completely, others stay in neighbourhoods, museums, or parks. Together, they create the feeling of the city changing mood as the year unfolds. Amsterdam is one of the Dutch cities with the most events organised throughout the year, and the schedule of the main events is fairly consistent, so locals know what to expect each month.

This guide brings together the main events in Amsterdam that happen every year. I’ve grouped them by month, to make it easier for you to plan your agenda. We don’t have the dates yet for some of them, but I’ll update the article as they become available.

## Main Events in Amsterdam 2026

These yearly events in Amsterdam give structure to the calendar, but they’re only part of the story. This list is here to help you plan, get inspired, and understand how the city moves through the year. There are, of course, a lot of smaller events that happen in between, so keep an eye for them. If you want a curated cultural agenda for each month, you can [sign up for my newsletter](https://amsterdamian.com/newsletter/).

[Save the monthly guide as a PDF](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Yearly-events-2026.pdf).

### Events in Amsterdam in January 2026

**[Amsterdam Light Festival](https://amsterdamlightfestival.com/en) – until 18 January**[The Amsterdam Light Festival](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/a-guide-to-the-amsterdam-light-festival/) that starts in the previous year in November continues into January, lighting up the city during its darkest weeks. Light installations by international artists are displayed along the canals and streets, inviting evening walks or boat rides. For many locals, the January part of the festival feels quieter, after the holiday crowds have faded, making it one of the best moments to experience it. Read here about my [boat ride to the Light Festival](https://amsterdamian.com/see/amsterdam/canal-adventures-renting-a-boat-in-amsterdam/) in 2025-2026.

**[National Tulip Day](https://tulpentijd.nl/en/) – 17 January at Museumplein**  
[National Tulip Day](https://amsterdamian.com/see/amsterdam/national-tulip-day-2016/) marks the unofficial start of [tulip season in the Netherlands](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/dutch-tulip-fields-a-complete-guide/). Each January, a large temporary tulip garden appears in Amsterdam (in Dam Square or Museumplein), inviting people to pick their own bouquet for free (the tulips have the bulbs as well, and you can plant them in your garden to enjoy them for many years). It’s a colourful and very Dutch moment in the middle of winter, when the city is still grey and quiet. Locals often stop by briefly, while visitors are queuing for a few moments inside the ”garden”.

**[Jumping Amsterdam](https://www.jumpingamsterdam.nl/) — 22 – 25 January at RAI**  
Jumping Amsterdam is an international equestrian event that brings top show jumpers and dressage riders to the city. Held indoors, it attracts both dedicated fans of the sport and curious spectators who are experiencing it for the first time. For Amsterdam, it’s a slightly unexpected event but it has become a recurring winter fixture over the years. In 2026, the event celebrates its 65th edition.  
  
**[The Maker Market](https://www.themakermarket.nl/) — 24, 25 January and then each month**  
This market brings together local designers, artisans, and small-scale producers, offering handmade goods ranging from ceramics and jewellery to textiles, prints, and food products. Rather than focusing on mass production, the market highlights craftsmanship and personal stories behind the objects. For many visitors, it’s a relaxed way to discover Amsterdam-based makers and support creative work on a human scale.

**[Museum Market](https://www.museummarket.nl/) — each third Sunday of the Month**   
This is a monthly design and maker market held on Museumplein, bringing together artists, designers, and creative entrepreneurs from the city and beyond. Stalls feature handmade objects, illustrations, jewellery, prints, and small design pieces, often created by the makers themselves. With the major museums as a backdrop, the market has a relaxed, open-air atmosphere and is as much about browsing and conversation as it is about buying, making it a pleasant stop during a walk through the museum district.

**[IJ-Hallen Flea Market](https://ijhallen.nl/en/) — 24-25 January at NDSM-Plein – and then each month throughout the year**  
The IJ-Hallen flea market is one of the largest [flea markets](https://amsterdamian.com/see/amsterdam/waterlooplein-flea-market/) in Europe and a favourite among locals who enjoy slow browsing and unexpected finds. Held in a large industrial space in Amsterdam Noord, it offers everything from vintage clothes and books to household items and curiosities. The atmosphere is informal and practical rather than trendy, and many Amsterdammers treat it as a regular weekend outing rather than a special event. The market is held every month, so check out their website for the entire calendar.

### Events in Amsterdam in February 2026

**Chinese New Year in Amsterdam – 17 February 2026, Nieuwmarkt, Zeedijk and Dam Square.**  
Chinese New Year is celebrated each year in Amsterdam’s Chinatown, around the Zeedijk area. The festivities usually include traditional lion and dragon dances, firecrackers, and cultural performances, drawing both locals and visitors. While smaller in scale than in some other cities, the celebration has a strong community feel and offers a glimpse into the multicultural layers that have long been part of Amsterdam’s identity.

**[Chocolate Festival Amsterdam](https://www.chocoladefestival.amsterdam/english)** — 21-22 February, **Beurs van Berlage**.  
This is the ultimate celebration of cacao and chocolate in the Netherlands, a two-day flavour-packed experience that invites visitors to dive deep into the delicious world of chocolate at the historic **Beurs van Berlage** in Amsterdam.

**[Remembrance 1941 February Strike](https://februaristaking.nl/) – 25 February, Jonas Daniël Meijerplein.**  
The February Strike of 1941 is commemorated every year in Amsterdam as a powerful act of resistance against the persecution of Jewish citizens during the Nazi occupation. The remembrance takes place at the statue of the Dockworker on Jonas Daniël Meijerplein and is marked by speeches and wreath-laying. It is a sober and reflective moment in the city’s calendar, reminding residents and visitors alike of Amsterdam’s wartime history and the courage of those who stood up in solidarity.

### Events in Amsterdam in March 2026

**[Opera Forward Festival](https://www.operaballet.nl/en/opera/dutch-national-opera/opera-forward-festival) – 6-15 March, Nationale Opera & Ballet, Muziekgebouw aan ’t IJ and Frascati.**The Opera Forward Festival is organised by Dutch National Opera and focuses on contemporary opera and new musical forms. The festival explores experimentation, social themes, and collaborations with modern composers and artists. It attracts a mixed audience of opera lovers and newcomers curious about how the art form is evolving. This year marks the 10th anniversary of the festival.  
  
**[Stille Omgang](https://www.stille-omgang.nl/the-route/) – 21, 22 March**The Stille Omgang is a silent night-time procession through Amsterdam’s city centre, rooted in a centuries-old Catholic tradition. Participants walk together in silence, commemorating a medieval miracle associated with the city. Despite taking place in busy streets, the event remains quiet and reflective, creating a striking contrast with Amsterdam’s usual nightlife. Each year, the festival week of the Miracle begins on the Wednesday after the 12th of March. From Wednesday through Saturday, holy masses of devotion are celebrated in the Beguinage. But the big yearly manifestation is still the Silent Procession, held on Saturday night through Sunday morning.  
  
**[Roze Filmdagen (Pink Film Days](https://rozefilmdagen.nl/nl)) – 18-29 March at Ketelhuis**Pink Film Days is an LGBTQ+ film festival that showcases international queer cinema, including feature films, documentaries, and shorts. Screenings take place in various cinemas across the city and often include discussions with filmmakers. The festival is known for its inclusive atmosphere and focus on storytelling rather than spectacle. Note that you can use your Cineville pass for tickets, but only when you buy at the counter (can’t reserve the tickets when using the pass).  
  
**[Cinedans](https://cinedans.nl/) — 25 – 29 March in Eye Filmmuseum Amsterdam, WEB and Tolhuistuin.**Cinedans is a film festival dedicated to dance on screen, bringing together choreography and cinema. It presents films, performances, and installations that explore movement in both traditional and experimental ways. The festival attracts a niche but international audience and fits well within Amsterdam’s broader performing arts scene.

**[Amsterdam Wine Festival](https://www.amsterdamwinefestival.nl/) — 26-29 March at Kromhouthal**The Amsterdam Wine Festival brings together winemakers, importers, and wine lovers for tastings and masterclasses. Usually held in a large indoor venue, it balances education with a relaxed atmosphere. It attracts both casual visitors and those with a deeper interest in wine culture.  
  
**[Keukenhof Gardens](https://keukenhof.nl/nl/) — 19 March – 10 May, in Lisse**Although not located in Amsterdam itself, [Keukenhof Gardens](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/keukenhof-most-beautiful-garden-in-europe/) are closely associated with a spring visit to the city. Open only for a limited period each year, the gardens showcase millions of flowering bulbs, including tulips, daffodils, and hyacinths. Many visitors combine Keukenhof with a stay in Amsterdam, making it a seasonal highlight.

### Events in Amsterdam April in 2026

**[Tulp Festival](https://www.tulpfestival.com/) (*Tulip Festival*) – Entire month, throughout the city**[The Tulp Festival](https://amsterdamian.com/see/amsterdam/amsterdam-tulp-festival-2016/) celebrates Amsterdam’s connection to the flower by placing tulips in public spaces across the city. The festival invites visitors to explore different neighbourhoods, and it’s a reminder that you can see even more flowers at Keukenhof. This year marks the 12th edition of the Tulp Festival Amsterdam, which will feature organic tulips for the first time. The Nieuw-West neighbourhood will lead the way, with organic tulips appearing at various locations, while in Vondelpark they will float on rafts on the pond.

**[CinemAsia Film Festival](https://cinemasia.nl/en/) – 8-12 April at EYE, Studio/K, Rialto de Pijp**CinemAsia is dedicated to Asian cinema and Asian-European storytelling. The festival screens films from across Asia as well as works by diaspora filmmakers, often accompanied by talks and cultural events. It plays an important role in highlighting underrepresented voices within Amsterdam’s film landscape.

**[Amsterdam Spanish Film Festiva](https://amsterdamspanishfilmfestival.com/)l – 10-19 April, various venues**The Amsterdam Spanish Film Festival focuses on contemporary Spanish cinema, showcasing films from Spain and Spanish-speaking regions. Screenings are often accompanied by introductions or discussions, making it a favourite among film enthusiasts interested in European cinema beyond the mainstream.

**[Amsterdam Coffee Festival](https://www.amsterdamcoffeefestival.com/) — 16–18 April, NDSM-Loods**

Three days, dozens of Amsterdam’s best roasters and baristas, and more single-origin espresso than you can reasonably drink in a weekend. The Amsterdam Coffee Festival is a well-established fixture on the city’s food calendar, with workshops, tastings, latte art competitions, and guest roasters from around the world.   
  
**[King’s Day](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/kings-day-2015/) (Koningsdag) – 27 April**King’s Day is one of the most recognisable yearly events in Amsterdam. Celebrated on the King’s birthday, the city (and the entire country) turns orange as streets fill with flea markets, music, and informal parties. The party starts on the evening of 26, with the Koningsavond, and continues the next day. The flea markets are organised throughout the city, and it’s the chance for everyone to sell their stuff and for kids to make extra money by selling toys or showcasing their talents.

**[World Press Photo Exhibition](https://www.nieuwekerk.nl/en/exhibitions/) – 24 April – 27 September**The World Press Photo Exhibition presents the winning images of the annual World Press Photo Contest. Shown in Amsterdam each year, the exhibition offers a powerful overview of global events through photojournalism. It’s one of the city’s most consistently impactful exhibitions.

**[KunstRAI](https://kunstrai.nl/en/) – 22-26 April at RAI**KunstRAI is one of the Netherlands’ longest-running contemporary art fairs, bringing together around 100 galleries from the Netherlands and abroad. Held annually in Amsterdam, it offers an accessible overview of modern and contemporary art, appealing to both collectors and casual visitors. The offer consists of an inspiring selection of contemporary painting, photography, (vintage) design, applied arts, post-war art, and specialist art books.

### Events in Amsterdam in May 2026

**[Remembrance Day](https://www.4en5mei.nl/herdenken/nationale-herdenking-op-de-dam) — 4 May, Dam Square**[Remembrance Day](https://amsterdamian.com/see/the-netherlands/remembrance-day/) commemorates Dutch victims of war and violence. In Amsterdam, the main ceremony takes place on Dam Square and includes two minutes of silence at 8 p.m. The atmosphere is solemn, and much of the city comes to a standstill.

**[Liberation Day](https://4en5meiamsterdam.nl/) — 5 May**[Liberation Day](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/a-sunny-liberation-day/) marks the end of the Nazi occupation of the Netherlands in 1945. While it’s a national celebration, Amsterdam hosts concerts and events focused on freedom and democracy. Unlike Remembrance Day, the tone is celebratory. One of the main events is the one on Museumplein, with music, poetry and speeches.

**[National Mill Day (Nationale Molendag)](https://www.molens.nl/ontdek-molens/molendagen) — 9-10 May**On National Mill Day, [windmills across the Netherlands](https://amsterdamian.com/see/the-netherlands/meet-the-windmills-at-zaanse-schans/) open their doors to the public, including several in and around Amsterdam. Visitors can learn about milling, engineering, and the role windmills played in Dutch history. It’s a family-friendly and distinctly local event.

**[Amsterdam Art Week](https://amsterdamart.com/) – date TBA**Amsterdam Art Week connects galleries, museums, and project spaces across the city. It offers exhibitions, talks, and guided tours, highlighting both established and emerging artists. The event encourages visitors to move beyond major institutions.  
  
**[Rollende Keukens](https://rollendekeukens.amsterdam/) — 13-17 May, Westerpark**[Rollende Keukens](https://amsterdamian.com/see/shopping-and-lifestyle/rollende-keukens-2015/) is a food truck festival held in Westerpark. For several days, the park fills with mobile kitchens, long communal tables, and live music. Many locals treat it as a casual outdoor gathering rather than a formal event. Free entry, but the food is usually overpriced to compensate.

[Vondelpark Open Air Theatre](https://www.openluchttheater.nl/) — May–September in [Vondelpark](https://amsterdamian.com/see/nature/skating-natural-ice-vondelpark/)  
Throughout summer, the Vondelpark Open Air Theatre hosts free performances including music, dance, and theatre. Events take place in an informal setting and are popular with locals who combine them with picnics or evening walks.

### Events in Amsterdam in June 2026

**[Red Light Jazz](https://redlightjazz.com/en/) — Date TBA, [Red Light District](https://amsterdamian.com/see/amsterdam/culture-in-the-red-light-district-straat-vol-kunst/)**Red Light Jazz brings jazz performances to venues in and around Amsterdam’s Red Light District. The festival highlights the area’s historical connection to jazz and live music, offering an alternative perspective on a much-visited neighbourhood.

**[Open Tower Day (Open Toren Dag)](https://www.opentorendag.nl/) – Date TBA, various locations**On Open Tower Day, church towers and other historic towers across Amsterdam open to the public. Visitors are rewarded with panoramic views of the city, often from places normally closed off.  
  
**[Open Garden Days (Open Tuinen Dagen)](https://www.opentuinendagen.nl/) — 19-21 June, city centre**During [Open Garden Days](https://amsterdamian.com/see/nature/the-green-city-open-garden-days/), private gardens along Amsterdam’s canals open their gates to visitors. Many are hidden behind historic canal houses and rarely accessible, offering a glimpse into a quieter, greener side of the city. It’s a unique opportunity to visit these amazing gardens and to discover a part of the city that you don’t normally see.

**[Bacchus Wine Festival](https://www.bacchuswijnfestival.nl/) — 5-6-7, 12-13-14, 19, 20, 21 June in Amsterdamse Bos**Bacchus Wine Festival is an outdoor wine event combining tastings with food and music. It’s informal and relaxed, and attracts a mix of locals and visitors.

**[Holland Festival](https://www.hollandfestival.nl/en) — 3-28 June, various locations**The Holland Festival is one of the Netherlands’ most important performing arts festivals. Taking place in Amsterdam and other cities, it features theatre, music, dance, tours, and multidisciplinary performances from international artists.

**[Amsterdam Roots Festival](https://www.amsterdamroots.nl/) – 28 June**Amsterdam Roots Festival showcases music, dance, and storytelling from cultures around the world. It highlights global traditions while connecting them to contemporary themes.

**[Jardin Rouge](https://jardinrouge.nl/jardin-rouge-amsterdam/) — 18-20 June**Jardin Rouge Festival is an outdoor music festival held in Ruigoord, the artist village on the western edge of Amsterdam. Set among trees, old industrial buildings, and art installations, the festival focuses on electronic music, with DJs and live acts performing across several stages. The setting and relaxed pace make it feel less like a large-scale event and more like a day spent wandering between music, nature, and art, in keeping with Ruigoord’s long-standing creative spirit.

**[Zuidas Run](https://truetribezuidasrun.nl/) — 12 June, Zuidas**This is a yearly running event through Amsterdam’s Zuidas district, combining sport with the city’s modern business area. With several race distances, it attracts both experienced runners and casual participants, offering a different view of Amsterdam away from the historic centre.

### Events in Amsterdam in July 2026

**[Keti Koti](https://ketikotiamsterdam.nl/) — 1 July**[Keti Koti](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/keti-koti-amsterdam-stories-events-and-meaning/) commemorates the abolition of slavery in the former Dutch colonies. In Amsterdam, it is marked with ceremonies, educational events, and celebrations, particularly in Oosterpark. It’s an important moment of remembrance and reflection.  
  
**[Julidans](https://julidans.nl/nl/) –** 2-14 July  
Julidans is an international contemporary dance festival held across multiple venues in Amsterdam. It focuses on innovative choreography and global perspectives on dance.  
  
**[Milkshake Festival](https://www.milkshakefestival.com/) — 25-26 July, Westergas**Milkshake Festival is an inclusive, queer-friendly music festival celebrating diversity and self-expression. Held in Westerpark, it blends music, fashion, and performance with a strong emphasis on openness and acceptance.

**[Kwaku Summer Festival](https://kwakufestival.nl/) – 11 July – 2 August**The Kwaku Summer Festival takes place over several weekends in Amsterdam-Zuidoost. It combines food, music, sports, and community events, with strong roots in Surinamese and Afro-Caribbean culture.

**[De Parade](https://deparade.nl/) — 24 July – 9 August**De Parade is the world’s only traveling theater festival, that sets up a temporary tent village in parks, in various cities like Rotterdam, The Hague, Utrecht, and Amsterdam. Short performances, food stalls, and an informal atmosphere define the experience.

**[Landjuweel](https://ruigoord.nl/evenement/het-landjuweel-2026/) — 23-26 July, Ruigoord**An annual open-air festival held in Ruigoord, a small artist village near Amsterdam, featuring music, theatre, visual art, and other forms of creative expression, often with a free-spirited, bohemian vibe.

[**Festival Trek**](https://www.festival-trek.nl/) **— 3-5 July Amstelpark**

Festival TREK is a lively traveling food festival that turns city parks into open-air restaurants, with food trucks, live music, and a relaxed picnic atmosphere.  
  
**[WorldPride](https://pride.amsterdam/) — 25 July- 8 August, various locations**[Amsterdam Pride](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/romanian-boat-amsterdam-pride-canal-parade/) is a city-wide celebration of LGBTQ+ identity, visibility, and rights, spread over several days and neighbourhoods. Pride Week usually begins in late July, with talks, exhibitions, sports events, and community gatherings, before building up to the larger public celebrations in early August. While the Canal Parade is the most visible moment, Amsterdam Pride as a whole is rooted in activism, history, and the city’s long-standing tradition of openness and diversity. In 2026 Amsterdam will host **World Pride**, which is a global event that promotes visibility and awareness of LGBTQIA+ issues internationally

### Events in Amsterdam in August 2026

**WorldPride and [Canal Parade](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/pride-2018-amsterdam-the-canal-parade/) — Canal Parade on 1 August**  
Amsterdam WorldPride culminates in the Canal Parade, where decorated boats move through the city’s canals. The event celebrates LGBTQ+ visibility and rights and is both festive and political in nature.

**[Dance Valley](https://dancevalley.com/)  — 8 August**Dance Valley is a large electronic music festival held near Amsterdam. Known for its long history, it attracts international DJs and thousands of visitors each year.  
  
**[Grachtenfestival](https://www.grachtenfestival.nl/) – 7-16 August, city centre**The Grachtenfestival is a classical and contemporary music festival held at unique locations, including canal-side venues and boats. It aims to make classical music accessible to wider audiences.  
  
**[Open Air Film Festival Pluk de Nacht](https://plukdenacht.nl/) – Date TBA**Pluk de Nacht is an open-air film festival showing independent and lesser-known films. Screenings take place outdoors, often in informal settings, and are free to attend.

### Events in Amsterdam in September 2026

**[Open Monumentendag](https://www.openmonumentendag.nl/) — 12-13 September**During Open Monumentendag, historic buildings across Amsterdam open to the public free of charge. It’s an opportunity to explore churches, schools, and private buildings usually closed to visitors.

**[Amsterdam Fashion Week](https://amsterdamfashionweek.nl/) — 1-5 September**Amsterdam Fashion Week highlights Dutch designers through runway shows and presentations. It reflects the city’s experimental and conceptual approach to fashion.  
  
**[Amsterdam City Swim](https://www.amsterdamcityswim.nl/) – 6 September**The Amsterdam City Swim is a charity swimming event in the city’s waterways, raising money for ALS research. Participants swim a set route, watched by spectators along the canals.

**[Dutch Theatre Festival](https://tf.nl/) (TF) – 3-13 September, various locations**The Dutch Theatre Festival officially kicks off the new theatre season each year, presenting the most notable theatre productions of the previous season. It acts as a showcase of national performing arts. In addition to the performances, it also features meetings, conversations, public programs.  
  
**[Amsterdam Fringe Festival](https://amsterdamfringefestival.nl/en/home-eng/) – 3-13 September**Running alongside the Dutch Theatre Festival, the Amsterdam Fringe Festival focuses on experimental and emerging theatre makers. Performances take place in unconventional locations.

**[Dam tot Dam Run](https://www.nndamloop.com/) – 19-20 September**The Dam tot Dam Run is a popular road race from Amsterdam to Zaandam. Thousands of runners participate, and large crowds line the route.  
  
**[Ambacht in Beeld Festival](https://ambachtinbeeldfestival.nl/en/) (Craft in Focus Festival) – 26 -27 September, NDSM Loods**The Ambacht in Beeld Festival is a hands-on celebration of craftsmanship held each autumn in the NDSM Loods in Amsterdam-Noord. For a weekend, the huge industrial space becomes an interactive workshop where more than a hundred master artisans from the Netherlands and abroad demonstrate their skills and lead workshops, masterclasses, talks, and demonstrations.

### Events in Amsterdam in October 2026

**[Children’s Book Week (Kinderboekenweek)](https://www.kinderboeken.nl/) – Date TBA, various locations**Kinderboekenweek celebrates children’s literature across the Netherlands. In Amsterdam, bookshops, libraries, and schools host readings and activities.  
  
**[De Amsterdamse Grachtenrace](https://www.grachtenrace.amsterdam/) — 10 October, Amsterdam canals**De Amsterdamse Grachtenrace (Canal Race) is a long-standing rowing event in which teams of rowers compete in traditional sloops along a course that winds through Amsterdam’s canals, usually covering around 21–25 kilometres of waterway.

**[Afrovibes Festival](https://www.afrovibes.nl/) – Date TBA, various locations**Afrovibes Festival presents contemporary performing arts from Africa and the African diaspora. The programme often addresses social and political themes.

**[Amsterdam Marathon](https://www.tcsamsterdammarathon.eu/) – 17-18 October**The Amsterdam Marathon is an annual marathon in Amsterdam in the Netherlands since 1975. The road race has a Platinum Label from World Athletics. During the event, there are also a half marathon race and an 8 km race in the program. The marathon starts and ends in the Amsterdam Olympic Stadium, a stadium built for the Olympic Games that were held in Amsterdam in 1928.

[![Amsterdam_Marathon_2014](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Amsterdam_Marathon_2014_-_09-1024x768.jpeg)](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Amsterdam_Marathon_2014_-_09-scaled.jpeg)

Photo Wikipedia

**[Cinekid Festival](https://cinekid.nl/en/festival) – 10-25 October, various locations**Cinekid is one of the world’s largest children’s film and media festivals. It includes films, workshops, and interactive installations.  
  
**[Amsterdam Dance Event (ADE)](https://www.amsterdam-dance-event.nl/en/live/) – 21-25 October, various locations**ADE is the world’s leading electronic music conference and festival. It combines industry events with hundreds of performances across the city.

### Events in Amsterdam in November 2026

**[Museum Night](https://museumnacht.amsterdam/) — 7 November**Museum Night allows visitors to explore museums late into the night with special programmes, performances, and talks. It’s particularly popular with younger audiences. You can use your [Museum Card](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/how-to-explore-amsterdam-the-best-city-passes-for-tourists-and-locals/) if you have one.

**[The Amsterdam Storytelling Festival](https://storytellingfestival.nl/) – Date TBA, Podium Mozaiek**This festival celebrates storytelling in its many forms, from spoken word to performance. It often focuses on personal and social narratives.  
  
**[IDFA](https://www.idfa.nl/en/) – 12-22 November, various locations**IDFA, the International Documentary Film Festival Amsterdam, is one of the world’s leading documentary festivals and a highlight of the city’s cultural calendar. Each November, venues across Amsterdam screen hundreds of documentaries from around the globe, covering social, political, and personal stories. In addition to screenings, the festival offers workshops, debates, and networking events for filmmakers and audiences alike. For locals and visitors, IDFA is a chance to explore compelling real-life stories and discover new perspectives on the world.  
  
**[PAN Amsterdam](https://www.pan.nl/en/) – 1-8 November at RAI**PAN Amsterdam is an art, antiques, and design fair showcasing a wide range of periods and styles, from classical art to modern design.

**[Affordable Art Fair Amsterdam](https://affordableartfair.com/fairs/amsterdam/) – 25-29 November, De Kromhouthal**The Affordable Art Fair focuses on accessible contemporary art, with works priced below a set maximum. It attracts first-time buyers as well as regular collectors.  
  
**Sinterklaas Arrival & Parade – Date TBA**The arrival of Sinterklaas marks the beginning of the festive season. In Amsterdam, the event includes a parade and activities for children.

**Amsterdam Light Festival – Date TBA**One of the most awaited festivals of the year starts at the end of November.

### Events in Amsterdam in December 2026

**[Amsterdam Light Festival](https://amsterdamlightfestival.com/en) – Date TBA**The Amsterdam Light Festival runs through winter, featuring light installations along the canals and streets. The artworks can be viewed on foot or by boat and bring warmth and reflection to the darkest months of the year.

**New Year’s Eve — 31 December**Celebrate New Year’s Eve with various parties and fireworks.

### A few tips before you plan

If you’re visiting during one of the main events in Amsterdam, it helps to know a few things:  
• The most popular events get busy quickly, especially in spring and summer. Booking ahead is often necessary, even for things that seem casual.  
• Book accommodation early for visits during King’s Day and Pride, as the city fills up fast.  
• Not every event is city-wide. Many are neighbourhood-based, which can be a lovely way to see a different side of Amsterdam.  
• Make sure to check the weather and pack accordingly. As a general rule, Amsterdam is the place for layers, no matter the season.  
• Amsterdam doesn’t demand constant activity. Even during major yearly events, there’s always space to step aside, walk along the canals, or sit somewhere quietly and let the city pass by.

---

Stay tuned for more and follow Amsterdamian on [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/theamsterdamian/) and [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/amsterdamian.page) for more stories about life in the Netherlands. Please share this post if you liked it!

**Check out my photo book: [Amsterdam Through the Seasons](https://www.blurb.com/b/10882891-amsterdam-through-the-seasons)!**

Love what you’re reading? [Support my work with a small donation](https://ko-fi.com/amsterdamian).

[Subscribe to the newsletter](https://amsterdamian.com/newsletter/)!

## About

Amsterdamian is a digital magazine exploring life in Amsterdam and the Netherlands and everything that makes this place unique. From everyday moments to cultural deep-dives, hidden corners to personal stories, it’s an invitation to discover not just the country, but the people who give it soul.

hits.title: Pluk de Nacht Film Festival | I amsterdam
hits.snippet:
PLUK de NACHT Amsterdam
PLUK de NACHT Amsterdam
PLUK de NACHT - undiscovered cinema

For over 20 years, you can enjoy the open-air film festival at the Stenen Hoofd in Amsterdam. This festival guarantees balmy summer evenings with the best films on the big screen, beach chairs, warm blankets, a campfire, and cocktails! ![PLUK de NACHT - undiscovered cinema](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fapp.thefeedfactory.nl%2Fapi%2Fassets%2F60b4e58a65b7c97410ee2102%2FPLUK%2520de%2520NACHT%2520-%2520undiscovered%2520cinema%2520CREDIT%2520Jurriaan%2520Esmeijer%25201800x1020.webp&w=3840&q=75)

For over 20 years, you can enjoy the open-air film festival at the Stenen Hoofd in Amsterdam. This festival guarantees balmy summer evenings with the best films on the big screen, beach chairs, warm blankets, a campfire, and cocktails! In 2026, you can once again curl up in a beach chair for the best films. Please note: The dates and start and end times listed for 2026 are subject to change.

## Pluk de Nacht Film Festival

### Dates

### Location

### Contact

### Follow

## Amsterdam open-air cinema

A giant screen showcases national and international feature films, documentaries, short films, and animations that have not yet been released in Dutch **cinemas** . The secret to Pluk de Nacht's success lies in its initiators: a group of friends with a deep passion for **film** . They select gems from various [film festivals](https://www.iamsterdam.com/nl/uit/festivals/filmfestivals) that have not yet been released in the Netherlands or have only reached a small audience.

## From your lazy beach chair

At the Stenen Hoofd city beach (a stone's throw from Central Station) you can watch films with a fantastic view of the IJ.

The festival grounds open for the evening films at 7:00 PM. On weekends (and in warm weather), it might open a little earlier; keep an eye on social media. The films start at 9:00 PM.

## Seize the Night Mini

The ninth edition of Pluk MINI, like all previous editions, is free for children and their parents, babysitters, caregivers, and grandparents. You are welcome at [Pluk MINI](https://plukdenacht.nl/2025/06/29/popeye-op-pluk-mini/) on Sunday, August 24th, from 2:00 PM to 5:30 PM. Tickets are required for the film nights in the circus tent (August 22nd, 23rd, 29th, and 30th). These film nights cost €4.50 plus a €1.50 entertainment tax.

The titles for these evenings are now available , [check the program](https://plukdenacht.nl/en/filmprogramma/)[.](https://plukdenacht.nl/2025/07/12/films-in-de-pluktent-2025/) Reserve your ticket now; you can find out how on the film's page. Parents are welcome to enjoy themselves outside, at the bar or by the campfire.

## Other information

Tickets cost €9.50. In Amsterdam, there's a €1.50 entertainment tax on top of that, for a total of €11.00. With a ticket like this, you can pick up a beach chair, see a fantastic film, enjoy a fantastic art program, and dance the night away at De Stille Nachtclub.

With your ticket you can pick up a beach chair, watch a fantastic film, enjoy a wonderful art program and afterward you can dance in De Stille Nachtclub.

Of course, you can also relax on the terrace, but you won't get the same movie experience as you do from a beach chair right in front of the container screen. You're also welcome to bring your own chair!

Good to know: both the Plukbar and the blanket rental service accept only **debit cards**. **Note:** movie tickets for the same evening can be purchased online until 8:30 PM, after which they can only be purchased on-site. Visit the [Pluk de Nacht website](https://plukdenacht.nl/) for more information.

![Film Fest Report](https://film-fest-report.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Logo-Film-Fest-Report-115x115-1.png)

# 2026 Film Festival Dates

![Photo of Mehdi Balamissa](https://film-fest-report.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/cropped-Mehdi-Balamissa-Film-Fest-Report-140x140.jpg)
![](https://i0.wp.com/film-fest-report.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/20210708_201206.jpg?resize=780%2C470&ssl=1)

## Film Fest Report provides you with insights to anticipate the 2026 film festival season. Explore the dates of the world’s major film festivals including Cannes, Berlin, Sundance, Venice, Locarno, Hot Docs, IDFA, and many more.

As the Film Fest Report team pursues its mission to promote **independent cinema** and report from the **film festival circuit** to our engaged audience of filmmakers, film professionals, and film enthusiasts, we compiled **one of the most extensive lists of film festivals** taking place in 2026 as possible.

Feel free to [reach out to us](https://film-fest-report.com/about/) to highlight your festival so we can include it in our listing. Also, explore how we can **promote your film festival** by taking a look at our coverage opportunities (on-site coverage, interviews, etc.).

 

**January**

### Public lecture series

# This is Film! 2026

## Film Heritage in Practice

4 March — 13 May 2026

scroll down

![still from Coração Materno (Haroldo Barradas, BR 1974)](https://assets.eyefilm.nl/images/production/_560x407_crop_center-center_none/still_Coração-Materno-Haroldo-Barradas-BR-1974.jpg)
![still from Coração Materno (Haroldo Barradas, BR 1974)](https://assets.eyefilm.nl/images/production/_560x407_crop_center-center_none/still_Coração-Materno-Haroldo-Barradas-BR-1974.jpg)

still *Coração Materno* (Haroldo Barradas, BR 1974)

This year, the overarching theme of the series is ‘The Future of Film Programming’, in line with the [Eye International Conference 2026](https://www.eyefilm.nl/en/programme/eye-international-conference-2026/1537328).

This is Film! Film Heritage in Practice is an annual public lecture series in collaboration with the University of Amsterdam devoted to notable projects in the fields of film restoration and film heritage, with international guest speakers and film screenings.

![still from Histerias (Inês Castilho, BR 1985)](https://assets.eyefilm.nl/images/production/_560x407_crop_center-center_none/still_Histerias-Inês-Castilho-BR-1985.jpg)
![still from Histerias (Inês Castilho, BR 1985)](https://assets.eyefilm.nl/images/production/_560x407_crop_center-center_none/still_Histerias-Inês-Castilho-BR-1985.jpg)

still *Histerias* (Inês Castilho, BR 1985)

The film collections preserved in archives trace an inclusive global history of cinema, encompassing not only celebrated canonical films but also lesser-known treasures. Rather than remaining locked away in vaults, these collections find a new life when shared and reinterpreted. Through diverse approaches to programming, memories of the past are revealed, influencing how we understand the world we inhabit today.  
  
Across six sessions, this year's programme brings together research projects and archival practices from around the globe that offer innovative approaches to programming collections. Together with archivists, researchers and film professionals, we explore topics such as programming Palestinian cinema as a form of resistance, digitising films at the margins of Brazilian film history, rediscovering a 1960s independent film festival in Istanbul, and activating Catalan film heritage.

Rather than remaining locked away in vaults, these collections find a new life when shared and reinterpreted. Through diverse approaches to programming, memories of the past are revealed, influencing how we understand the world we inhabit today.  
  
Across six sessions, this year's programme brings together research projects and archival practices from around the globe that offer innovative approaches to programming collections. Together with archivists, researchers and film professionals, we explore topics such as programming Palestinian cinema as a form of resistance, digitising films at the margins of Brazilian film history, rediscovering a 1960s independent film festival in Istanbul, and activating Catalan film heritage.

# International Documentary Film Festival Amsterdam 2026

Photo: www.idfa.nl

The International documentary film festival Amsterdam is held, yearly, in end November. More than 150 films are shown in a number of venues around the city. Contests in three categories of films and video are held:

The event is carried out since 1998 and attracts thousands of visitors, documentary film’s lovers and professionals. The producers, directors and film distributors that are gather for professional meetings, are those who choose the ten favorite films.

The festival’s program is present in the festival’s link website. See details below, under “Event Related Information” section.

# International Documentary Film Festival Amsterdam 2026

![](https://www.carnifest.com/wp-content/themes/herald-child/assets/images/misc/commentIcon.png)

Add Comment

![Photo: www.idfa.nl](https://www.carnifest.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/International_Documentary_Film_Festival_Amsterdam-Photo-www.idfa_.nl_.jpg "Photo: www.idfa.nl")

The International documentary film festival Amsterdam is held, yearly, in end November. More than 150 films are shown in a number of venues around the city. Contests in three categories of films and video are held:

The event is carried out since 1998 and attracts thousands of visitors, documentary film’s lovers and professionals. The producers, directors and film distributors that are gather for professional meetings, are those who choose the ten favorite films.

The festival’s program is present in the festival’s link website. See details below, under “Event Related Information” section.

Culture

# 10x the Best Film Festivals in the Netherlands in 2026

28 January 2026

Have you always wanted to enjoy a great movie in the most stunning settings? The Netherlands is *the* place for film festivals you won’t want to miss in 2026! From major international festivals in Rotterdam to cozy movie nights under the stars in Apeldoorn, there’s something for every film enthusiast. So grab your popcorn and mark these dates in your calendar!

## Planning a Movie + Hostel Stay?

Since most festivals last several days, you’ll have plenty of time to enjoy both the films and the surroundings. Lucky for you, we also have some wonderful hostels nearby for the perfect overnight stay!

## 1. Silent Film Festival, 16 - 18 Jan in Eindhoven

![Silent film festival in Eindhoven](/system/bloxy/images/images/000/001/245/large/silent_film_festival_eindhoven.jpg?1732300233)

The [Netherlands Silent Film Festival](https://nsff.nl/en/) (NSFF) is the largest film festival in the Netherlands dedicated to silent films. Here, you’ll experience cinema in its purest form: no words, but brimming with emotion, expression, and live music. Unique, right? The NSFF not only brings stunning silent films to the big screen but also makes this heritage accessible through workshops, lectures, and educational projects. From iconic audience favorites to recently restored hidden gems, everything is accompanied by performances from world-class musicians.

#### Spend the night in

## Eindhoven

And after such an impressive film experience, you can enjoy a good night’s sleep at [Stayokay Eindhoven](/en/hostel/eindhoven), our newest hostel located in the trendy Strijp-S district. This hostel is housed in the iconic Klokgebouw and has retained its industrial charm!

![](/system/bloxy/quotes/image_icons/000/001/515/original/icon-blank.png?1757340660)

## 2. IFFR, 29 jan - 8 feb in Rotterdam

![](/system/bloxy/images/images/000/001/244/large/49446643001_49ce3dd627_o-blog.jpg?1732711507 "Image by Hans Splinter on Flickr")

Image by Hans Splinter on Flickr

Welcome to the city where film thrives year-round: Rotterdam! The [International Film Festival Rotterdam](https://iffr.com/en/) (IFFR) is the perfect way to kick off your festival year. This massive event (think: thousands of films!) offers everything from cutting-edge avant-garde cinema to the very best international productions. What makes IFFR so special? Its focus on films that push boundaries, both in content and form. Come for the movies you might not encounter at other festivals!

#### Spend the night in

## Rotterdam

[Stayokay Rotterdam](/en/hostel/rotterdam) is currently undergoing a complete renovation! From April 2026, you will once again be able to stay in the iconic Cube Houses in a brand-new room. See you then?

![](/system/bloxy/quotes/image_icons/000/001/514/original/icon-blank.png?1757340660)

## 3. Roze Filmdagen, 18 - 29 March in Amsterdam

![](/system/bloxy/images/images/000/001/246/large/4733140096_374a94c839_o-blog.jpg?1732714197 "Image by 'philippe leroyer' on Flickr")

Image by 'philippe leroyer' on Flickr

The [Roze Filmdagen](https://rozefilmdagen.nl/en) in Amsterdam is one of the largest and most beloved LGBTQI+ film festivals in the Netherlands. The festival showcases films that tell stories from the queer community, going beyond the mainstream. From documentaries to dramas, the program is diverse, featuring beautiful, bold, and sometimes thought-provoking films.

#### Spend the night in

## Amsterdam Vondelpark

Stay close by at [Stayokay Amsterdam Vondelpark](/en/hostel/amsterdam-vondelpark), where you can relax with a drink in hand at the trendy, green bar after a day of films. And the location? Right in the heart of Vondelpark and the vibrant city center of Amsterdam. What more could you want?

![](/system/bloxy/quotes/image_icons/000/001/516/original/icon-blank.png?1757340660)

## 4. Movies that Matter, 20 - 28 March in Den Haag

![](/system/bloxy/images/images/000/001/247/large/pexels-jibarofoto-2774556-blog.jpg?1732714318)

If you love films with a message, then [Movies that Matter](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/) is the festival for you! These films make you think about important societal issues such as human rights, gender equality, and the environment. Expect heartwarming, emotional, and sometimes hard-hitting documentaries and films that will change your perspective on the world.

#### Spend the night in

## Den Haag

The great thing is, [Stayokay The Hague](/en/hostel/den-haag) is super centrally located near Holland Spoor station. So after a day filled with thought-provoking films, you can unwind and chat in the cozy lounge of the hostel. A perfect combination of culture and comfort!

![](/system/bloxy/quotes/image_icons/000/001/517/original/icon-blank.png?1757340660)

## 5. TOF, 7 - 16 Aug on Terschelling

![Terschelling Openlucht film festival](/system/bloxy/images/images/000/001/248/large/terschelling_openlucht_festival-bewerkt.jpg?1732714561 "Photographer: Berbe Rinders")

Photographer: Berbe Rinders

The [Terschelling Open Air Festival](https://tof.nu/) (TOF) is the ultimate festival for film and nature lovers. The Wadden island itself provides the perfect setting for a festival: the natural surroundings and open space create a unique experience. Rent a bike and enjoy 10 evenings of magical film locations, one after another. This is the ultimate summer evening: nature, coziness, and films under the starry sky!

#### Spend the night on

## Terschelling

And where will you stay? [Stayokay Terschelling](/en/hostel/terschelling) is located in West-Terschelling and offers the most beautiful view on the island (it’s almost cinematic!). Here, you can wake up each morning with a coffee on the terrace, and it’s the perfect starting point for the most scenic bike rides and walks.

![](/system/bloxy/quotes/image_icons/000/001/518/original/icon-blank.png?1757340660)

## 6. Vue Open Air Film Festival, Aug in Apeldoorn

![](/system/bloxy/images/images/000/001/249/large/pexels-son-tung-tran-17375059-6521574-blog.jpg?1732713375)

Want to watch the best blockbuster films on a 200 m² screen with hundreds or even thousands of other film lovers? The [Vue Open Air Film Festival](https://www.openluchtfilmfestival.nl/) in Apeldoorn is the perfect opportunity to enjoy the latest films and classics outdoors. And all of this takes place in Berg en Bos city park, right in the heart of nature.

#### Spend the night in

## Apeldoorn

A hostel right in the heart of nature? For a wonderful overnight stay, head to [Stayokay Apeldoorn](/en/hostel/apeldoorn), located close to the Hoge Veluwe. So, if you combine the festival with a walk in the forest, this is the perfect getaway!

![](/system/bloxy/quotes/image_icons/000/001/519/original/icon-blank.png?1757340660)

## 7. Film by the Sea, 11 - 20 Sept in Vlissingen

![](/system/bloxy/images/images/000/001/251/large/De_leukste_festivals_in_Nederland.jpg?1732713134)

[Film by the Sea](https://www.filmbythesea.nl/) is the jewel of the province Zeeland and one of the largest film festivals in the Netherlands. Here, you can enjoy over 100 films from all corners of the world, from book adaptations and French-language hits to stories from Zeeland. With a Panorama section full of new high-quality films and a fun side program (think Q&As, workshops, and performances), this festival is a hit for film enthusiasts and culture seekers alike.

#### Spend the night in

## Domburg

And after a day full of films? [Stayokay Domburg](/en/hostel/domburg) welcomes you with open arms in a 13th-century castle, recently fully renovated. Whether you're coming with the whole family, for a romantic getaway, or with friends, we always have the perfect (private or shared) room for you.

![](/system/bloxy/quotes/image_icons/000/001/521/original/icon-blank.png?1757340661)

## 8. Nederlands Film Festival, 25 Sept - 2 Oct in Utrecht

![](/system/bloxy/images/images/000/001/250/large/Neude-NFF24__Foto-Wilke-Geurds-blog.jpg?1732712328 "Source: NFF")

Source: NFF

In September, the city of Utrecht becomes the film paradise of the Netherlands thanks to the [Netherlands Film Festival](https://www.filmfestival.nl/en) (NFF). Here, the latest gems of the Dutch film industry are showcased, from indie films to big feature films. And what about the Golden Calf awards ceremony? It all happens here! From talented new filmmakers to established names, the NFF is the stage for the films you’ll see everywhere in cinemas after the festival.

#### Spend the night in

## Utrecht

[Stayokay Utrecht Centrum](/en/hostel/utrecht-centrum) is located right in the heart of the famous 't Neude square, exactly where the festival takes place! This cozy hostel has everything for a great stay: a trendy interior, a lovely café, and a perfect location for a day out in the city. After a busy day filled with films, this is the ideal spot to unwind!

![](/system/bloxy/quotes/image_icons/000/001/520/original/icon-blank.png?1757340661)

## 9. Cinekid Film Festival, 10 - 25 October, across the country

![](/system/bloxy/images/images/000/001/252/large/48935915051_30eab73159_o-blog.jpg?1732706698 "Image by Guido van Nispen on Flickr")

Image by Guido van Nispen on Flickr

[Cinekid](https://cinekid.nl/en/festival) is the largest children's film and media festival in the world, and it's right here in the Netherlands! At more than 40 locations across the country, kids can enjoy a diverse range of films, workshops, and other activities. Instead of just sitting in cinema seats, children also have the chance to get creative with film, animation, and digital art.

## 10. IDFA, 12 - 22 November in Amsterdam

![](/system/bloxy/images/images/000/001/253/large/22845265430_66312b71e6_o-blog.jpg?1732713779 "Image by Arjen de Vries on Flickr")

Image by Arjen de Vries on Flickr

[IDFA](https://www.idfa.nl/en/) (International Documentary Film Festival Amsterdam) is the largest documentary festival in the world. In November, you can enjoy the best (inter)­national documentaries in our capital. What makes this festival so special? It's not just a place for films, but also for debate. You can talk to filmmakers, attend panel discussions, and dive into the stories behind the camera.

#### Spend the night in

## Amsterdam East

[Stayokay Amsterdam Oost](/en/hostel/amsterdam-oost) is a great base for IDFA. This modern hostel is located in a trendy neighborhood, and you’re just 15 minutes from the city center. After a busy day of films and debates, you can relax in your own room or unwind in the cozy lounge.

![](/system/bloxy/quotes/image_icons/000/001/522/original/icon-blank.png?1757340661)

## Open air cinema's in the Netherlands

In addition to film festivals, you can also enjoy smaller events on warm summer evenings in the Netherlands. In every region, including your own, there are sure to be outdoor movie nights organized. Just bring your chair, a blanket, and let the films roll!

For example, how about:

* Watching a film with a breathtaking view at the [Rooftop Cinema](https://www.pathe.nl/rooftopcinema) in Utrecht?
* Enjoying a movie in the Kloostertuin in Dordrecht at [Brasserie De Witt](https://www.dewittdordrecht.nl/filmtheater/)?
* Or watching films all summer long on the roof of the [Focus](https://www.focusarnhem.nl/) cinema in Arnhem!

## Already feeling the Festival vibe?

Do you also plan to explore more festivals in 2026? No matter the season, there’s always something to do in every region. Because: the Netherlands is a festival country! And if you need more festival inspiration, check out our other blog:

[The best (music) festivals in the Netherlands](/en/blog/festivals-the-netherlands "The best (music) festivals in the Netherlands")

[Go to main content](#main-content)

[20 – 28 March 2026](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en "Movies that Matter")

# Specials

In addition to film screenings, the Movies that Matter Festival offers a range of specials that deepen and broaden the festival experience. From in-depth talks and panel discussions to performances, art installations and quizes.

Check the special events from the Movies that Matter Festival 2026 below.

[![Opening Night](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/MAIN_americandoctor1-460x260.jpg)

Friday 20 March

### Opening Night

*American Doctor* by Poh Si Teng opens the Movies that Matter Festival 2026 in The Hague.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/opening-night/) [![Pop-up exhibition Woman, Life, Freedom](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/Vrouwen-in-Teheran-zwaaien-met-hun-hoofddoek-en-roepen-leuzen-tijdens-een-protest-in-2022-foto-GettyImages-460x260.jpg)

Friday 20 March to Saturday 28 March

### Pop-up exhibition Woman, Life, Freedom

We honour the Iranian protest movement Woman, Life, Freedom with a pop-up exhibition that, in collaboration with the Resistance Museum, will be on display throughout the festival in the foyer of Theater aan het Spui.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/pop-up-exhibition-woman-life-freedom/) [![Book Swap Circle & Film](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/bookswap-460x260.jpg)

Saturday 21 March

### Book Swap Circle & Film

Book lovers, take note! Discover new stories during the Movies that Matter Book Swap and give your bookshelves a fresh start.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/book-swap-circle-and-film/) [![Selected by Trouw](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/Trouw-middag-2025-460x260.jpeg)

Saturday 21 March

### Selected by Trouw

Trouw presents two programmes around films selected by Trouw editor Remke de Lange: *Derek vs Derek* and *Silenced*.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/trouw/) [![(S)exposed](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/08.Shari_de_Boer_Sexposed-e1770731453276-460x260.jpg)

Saturday 21 March

### (S)exposed

An art installation that makes street harassment tangible for those who (fortunately) don’t experience it daily. Through an audio experience, you step into the shoes of someone being harassed.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/sexposed/) [![Party: Space Age DJ Collective](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/03/Space-Age-DJ-Collective-e1773244325385-460x260.jpg)

Saturday 21 March

### Party: Space Age DJ Collective

Kick off the festival weekend with the Space Age DJ Collective at Filmhuis Den Haag!](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/party-space-age-dj-collective/) [![Earth at Stake](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/MELT_Still_4_CNGF-460x260.jpg)

Sunday 22 March

### Earth at Stake

Two films that shine a light on ice loss (*MELT*) and climate displacement (*Black Water*), presented by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/earth-at-stake/) [![Meet me at the Bingo – Speed Dating](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/Cutting-Through-Rocks_Still-01-460x260.jpeg)

Sunday 22 March

### Meet me at the Bingo – Speed Dating

A special edition of Meet me at the Bingo together with @BallenBingo. During the speed dating rounds, you might just meet your perfect film match!](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/meet-me-at-the-bingo/) [![Selected by De Groene Amsterdammer](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/2-Noam-Shuster-Elisassi-Protest-Credit-Philippe-Bellaishe-Coexistence-My-Ass-460x260.jpeg)

Sunday 22 March

### Selected by De Groene Amsterdammer

De Groene Amsterdammer presents a special programme around the films *Steal This Story, Please* and *Coexistence, My Ass!*](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/de-groene/) [![De Grote Podcastlas Pubquiz](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/MTM25-EC-6455-1-460x260.jpg)

Sunday 22 March  
Wednesday 25 March

### De Grote Podcastlas Pubquiz

Do you want to test your world knowledge? During the festival, we’re teaming up with De Grote Podcastlas for a special pub quiz!](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/de-grote-podcastlas-pubquiz/) [![Teachers’ Day](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2024/11/Movies-that-Matter-2024-26-maart-2024-gemaakt-door-Wilke-Geurds-lage-resolutie-64-van-95-e1732626893586-460x260.jpg)

Monday 23 March

### Teachers’ Day

An inspiring teachers’ day for secondary education, vocational education (MBO), and ISK teachers, full of insights, tools, and practical takeaways to help discuss social issues in and beyond the classroom.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/education/teachers-day/) [![European Commission: Deep Rising](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2023/02/deeprising_still_40_credit_schmidt_ocean_institute-460x260.jpg)

Monday 23 March

### European Commission: Deep Rising

A free screening of *Deep Rising*, a documentary that takes an in-depth look at the world of deep-sea mining.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/european-commission-deep-rising/) [![Dutch Focus Night](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/truly-naked_still_graded_no-grain-460x260.jpeg)

Monday 23 March

### Dutch Focus Night

A special evening in honour of the Dutch Focus competition, full of personal, poignant and unique films. During the evening, we will screen the much-discussed feature film *Truly Naked*.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/dutch-focus-night/) [![Activist Night](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/03/Activist-Night-2025-460x260.jpeg)

Tuesday 24 March

### Activist Night

Face to face with today's heroes! On the festive Activist Night, all activists and directors from the Activist Lens competition will be honoured and put in the spotlight.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/activist-night/) [![Award Night](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2023/03/Awards_1-460x260.jpg)

Wednesday 25 March

### Award Night

Curious to see which films take home an award? Join us for the Award Night!](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/award-night/) [![Justice Frame Day](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2025/02/JaJo00001-460x260.jpg)

Thursday 26 March

### Justice Frame Day

A full-day deep dive into film and global justice, presented together with the Asser Institute.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/justice-frame-day/) [![Virtual Reality: Revival Roadshow](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/03/Revival-Roadshow_Anne-Fehres-and-Luke-Conroy-4-460x260.jpg)

Thursday 26 March - Saturday 28 March

### Virtual Reality: Revival Roadshow

In the 17-minute VR experience *Revival Roadshow*, explorer Abel Tasman confronts colonial legacies, climate crisis and digital culture in a speculative future.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/virtual-reality-revival-roadshow/) [![Stichting Vluchteling presents: Lost Land](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/03/Image-1-LOST-LAND-460x260.png)

Thursday 26 March

### Stichting Vluchteling presents: Lost Land

In collaboration with Stichting Vluchteling, we are screening *Lost Land*, the first feature film made entirely in the Rohingya language.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/stichting-vluchteling-presents-lost-land/) [![Our Colonial Past](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/03/Ons-Koloniaal-Verleden-Uma-Memoria-Emplumada-460x260.jpg)

Friday 27 March

### Our Colonial Past

Two films that shed light on the Dutch colonial history, followed by talks.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/our-colonial-past/) [![Queer Night](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2025/12/Queer-Night-MtMF25-460x260.jpeg)

Friday 27 March

### Queer Night

Queer Night is back! Featuring *La Petite Derniere*, *The Mysterious Gaze of the Flamingo*, and of course a party with DJ Calixta.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/queer-night/) [![NPO Doc presents: MARIINKA](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/03/MARIINKA.jpeg)

Friday 27 march

### NPO Doc presents: MARIINKA

NPO Doc presents the documentary *MARIINKA*, followed by a conversation with director Pieter-Jan De Pue.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/npodoc/) [![Sakharov Special](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/Mr-Nobody-Against-Putin_st_5_jpg_sd-low-460x260.jpg)

Friday 27 March

### Sakharov Special

A special evening dedicated to the Sakharov Prize with a free screening of the Oscar-nominated *Mr. Nobody Against Putin*.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/sakharov/) [![Selected by HUMAN](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/02/Still_02_Bagasi_Emma-Lesuis-460x260.jpg)

Saturday 28 March

### Selected by HUMAN

HUMAN presents two specially curated documentaries: *Cutting Through Rocks* and the premiere of *Bagasi – what we carry*, followed by a post-screening discussion.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/human/) [![Closing Night](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/content/uploads/2026/03/Divine-Comedy_st_1_jpg_sd-high-460x260.jpg)

Saturday 28 March

### Closing Night

Celebrate the festival’s grand finale with us! Featuring the announcement of the NPO Audience Award winner, a performance by violinist Azadeh Maghsoodi, the closing film *Divine Comedy*, and a DJ set by INDIEGLORIA.](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/festival/specials/closing-party/)

### Newsletter

Do you want to stay up to date with all Movies that Matter news, promotions and special screenings? Sign up for our newsletter.

Subscribe to our Industry newsletter or Educational newsletter (in Dutch) [here](https://moviesthatmatter.nl/en/newsletter/)

### FID Marseille

Marseilles, France

Sicily, Italy

hits.title: The Best Places to See Independent Film in Amsterdam - Lift-Off Global Network
hits.snippet:
###### Contact Info

A: Lift-Off Global Network, 47 Club Admin, Pinewood Studios, Iver Heath, Iver SL0 0HN   
[E: [email protected]](#)

Copyright © 2026 Lift-Off Global Network

### Festival Event Registration

Registration for all events happens via the Network Hub (the digital home for participants)

You should have received a free registration link during your onboarding process. For support, reach out to (/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)

### You're on the list!

Please check your inbox to confirm your email address and complete TKT registration.

If the email doesn’t arrive, check your spam or email (/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection) [...] # The Best Places to See Independent Film in Amsterdam

The international Lift-Off Film Festival returns to Amsterdam this week to celebrate the best that the independent film community in the Netherlands and across the rest of the world has to offer.

Taking place at quirky cult cinema LAB11, Amsterdam Lift-Off will be showcasing inspiring and thought provoking shorts, features, and animations.

View the full programme here.

Tickets to the event can be found here.

You can also keep up to date with Amsterdam Lift-Off  here.  

In honour of the return of the festival, carry on reading below to find out where else you can watch and discover the best independent and stimulating content in Amsterdam. [...] Whether you prefer foreign features in a cosy arthouse venue, or Hollywood decade dramas alongside the harbour, take a look at these venues across the capital for your ultimate film fix.

1. LAB111

Arie Biemondstraat

111, 1054 PD

Amsterdam, Netherlands

A quirky cult cinema located in a monumental old pathological anatomical laboratory, LAB111 showcases everything from in-depth documentaries, modern classics, and small public films to unconventional genre films. (And of course Amsterdam Lift-Off Film Festival 2018!)

2. Cinecenter

Cinecenter

Lijnbaansgracht 236

1017 PH Amsterdam

An independent arthouse cinema with an intimate atmosphere located in the centre of Amsterdam, Cinecenter showcases the latest independent films from France, Italy, Spain, the UK, and South America.

# The Best Places to See Independent Film in Amsterdam

The international Lift-Off Film Festival returns to Amsterdam this week to celebrate the best that the independent film community in the Netherlands and across the rest of the world has to offer.

Taking place at quirky cult cinema LAB11, Amsterdam Lift-Off will be showcasing inspiring and thought provoking shorts, features, and animations.

View the full programme **[here](http://liftoffnet.wpengine.com/amsterdam-lift-off-film-festival-2018/).**

Tickets to the event can be found **[here](https://www.eventbrite.co.uk/e/amsterdam-lift-off-film-festival-2018-tickets-50163287744).**

You can also keep up to date with Amsterdam Lift-Off  **[here](https://www.facebook.com/AmsterdamLiftOff/).**  

In honour of the return of the festival, carry on reading below to find out where else you can watch and discover the best independent and stimulating content in Amsterdam. 

Whether you prefer foreign features in a cosy arthouse venue, or Hollywood decade dramas alongside the harbour, take a look at these venues across the capital for your ultimate film fix.

**1. LAB111**

Arie Biemondstraat

111, 1054 PD

Amsterdam, Netherlands

https://www.lab111.nl/

A quirky cult cinema located in a monumental old pathological anatomical laboratory, LAB111 showcases everything from in-depth documentaries, modern classics, and small public films to unconventional genre films. (And of course Amsterdam Lift-Off Film Festival 2018!)

**2. Cinecenter**

Cinecenter

Lijnbaansgracht 236

1017 PH Amsterdam

<http://www.cinecenter.nl/>

An independent arthouse cinema with an intimate atmosphere located in the centre of Amsterdam, Cinecenter showcases the latest independent films from France, Italy, Spain, the UK, and South America.

**3. Filmhuis Cavia**

Filmhuis Cavia

Van Hallstraat 52-I

1051 HH Amsterdam

<http://www.filmhuiscavia.nl/>

A non-profit organisation, Filmhuis Cavia has been showing films for more than 30 years. Located in the former Princess Beatrix boarding school, the theatre is made up of 40 seats, making it the smallest cinema in Amsterdam.

**4. Filmhallen**

De FilmHallen

Hannie Dankbaarpassage 12

1053 RT Amsterdam

<http://www.filmhallen.nl/>

With nine theatres, Filmhallen is one of the biggest cinemas in Amsterdam, offering a wide variety of screenings, from documentaries to family-friendly films.

**5. De Uitkijk**

De Omval

Ouddiemerlaan 104

1111 HL Diemen

<http://www.uitkijk.nl/>

Located in the heart of Amsterdam on the Prinsengracht, De Uitkijk is is the oldest movie theatre in the Netherlands, with its origins dating back to 1912.

**6. Filmhuis Griffioen**

Filmhuis Griffioen

Uilenstede 106

1183 AM Amstelveen

<http://www.griffioen.vu.nl/>

Showcasing regular screenings of recent hits, classics, documentaries and forgotten pearls of cinema, Filmhuis Griffioen is a main part of a culture centre connected to the Vrije Universiteit.

*Showings are on Tuesdays, Thursdays and Fridays.*

**7. EYE**

EYE

IJpromenade 1

1031 KT Amsterdam

https://www.eyefilm.nl/en

Housed in the impressive white building on Amsterdam’s IJ harbour in Amsterdam Noord, EYE showcases more than 40, 000 films of all genres.

### A Useful Ghost

Soms komt liefde in de meest onverwachte vormen terug. In A Useful Ghost is March ontroostbaar na het verlies van zijn vrouw, tot ze ineens weer opduikt… als geest in een stofzuiger. Klinkt absurd? Dat is het ook maar op een charmante manier. Terwijl zijn familie worstelt met deze bizarre situatie, blijkt Nat juist verrassend handig en helpt ze zelfs de familiezaak uit de problemen.   
 Te zien bij: Pathé-bioscopen, Filmhallen, Kriterion, Rialto, FC Hyena, The Movies  
 Genre: Drama, Komedie  
 Te zien vanaf: 7 mei 2026

### The Sheep Detectives [...] ### Wasteman

Een klein vergrijp, maar een groot gevolg. In Wasteman volgt het leven van Taylor, die een lange gevangenisstraf uitzit en vooral probeert te overleven door onopvallend te blijven. Zijn dagen vult hij met kleine routines en herinneringen aan zijn zoon. Maar wanneer vervroegde vrijlating ineens binnen handbereik komt, zet een nieuwe, charismatische celgenoot alles op scherp.  
 Te zien bij: Pathé-bioscopen, The Movies, Kriterion, LAB111, Rialto, Studio/K, FC Hyena  
 Genre: Drama  
 Te zien vanaf:21mei 2026

## Bioscoop- en filmagenda Amsterdam 2026: meer films om te zien in mei

### The Devil Wears Prada 2 [...] ### The Sheep Detectives

Wat als schapen stiekem slimmer zijn dan je denkt? In The Sheep Detectives lijkt het leven op de boerderij rustig, tot een mysterie alles op z’n kop zet. Terwijl herder George nietsvermoedend voorleest uit detectiveboeken, blijken zijn schapen altijd al mee hebben geluisterd. Tijd voor actie dus: ze nemen zelf het onderzoek in handen en duiken in een avontuur vol aanwijzingen, verdachte types en slimme vondsten.   
 Te zien bij: Filmhallen, Pathé-bioscopen, Het Ketelhuis, VUE // , Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé-bioscopen  
 Genre: Familie, Komedie  
 Te zien vanaf: 7 mei 2026

# Amsterdam Film Agenda: 14 films to see in the cinema in May

Bioscoopje pakken in Amsterdam? Dit zijn de leukste films (volgens team Your Little Black Book) om deze maand in de bioscoop te kijken.

##### YLBB Notes

## Zin om naar de bioscoop te gaan in Amsterdam? Lees snel verder voor de nieuwe filmagenda voor Amsterdam

Een **filmmiddag** of -avond is altijd wel een goed idee! En zeker als er een leuke film draait. In onze nieuwe **bioscoopagenda van Amsterdam** ontdek je **de nieuwste films om in mei in de bioscoop te kijken**. Leuk om te doen met vriendinnen of als date met je geliefde. Bestel er een lekkere grote bak popcorn bij en je filmdate kan beginnen. Ook deze maand gaan er weer veel films in première, van **komedies** tot **thrillers** en **ontroerende films**. In welke stemming je ook bent, er draait vast wel een film naar jouw smaak! Bekijk hier de **Bioscoopagenda Amsterdam** voor de maand **mei**! En wist je dat je de films van afgelopen maand via [**Pathé Thuis**](https://www.awin1.com/cread.php?awinmid=17314&awinaffid=373571&campaign=filmagenda+amsterdam) ook gewoon **vanuit huis op de bank** kunt kijken? Bonustipje :-).

[#### Volg @yourlbb op Instagram](https://bit.ly/instafollowhome)

Nét terug uit Catalonië en ik dacht: ik ga direct wat tips delen.  
  
Tijdens onze roadtrip bleven we twee nachtjes in de Ebrodelta. Een ontzettend leuke stop voor families én foodies, waar van alles te doen is.  
  
· Een boottochtje naar het Musclarium is een must! Hier eet je oesters en mosselen zo van de farm, midden op zee.  
  
 · Bewonder de flamingo’s en zwarte ibissen die hier het hele jaar door te vinden zijn.  
  
 · In de Ebrodelta wordt het hele jaar door rijst verbouwd. Voor paella, maar Kensho maakt er ook sake en miso van! Je kunt hen bezoeken tijdens een tasting.  
  
 · Fietsen langs de rijstvelden, kanoën op de Ebro-rivier en naar verschillende stranden. Het was nu nog net te koud, maar er is van alles te doen.  
  
 · De stadjes zijn heel low key en niet zo spannend, maar je kunt er wel fantastisch lekker lokaal eten. Van het door Michelin bekroonde Les Moles\* tot lokale eethuisjes als Vermuteria Bonmarisc en Lo Tinglado. We aten heel veel vis, maar ook fritters van anemoon (een first!).  
  
Nu back to work en ga ik een uitgebreide travel guide maken van deze roadtrip door Catalonië, maar deze tips wil je alvast bewaren voor een volgend avontuur.  
  
✖️✖️✖️  
🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck   
  
{ In deze post deel ik tips voor de Ebrodelta in Spanje (Delta de l’Ebre). Meer tips voor Catalunya zijn te vinden op www.yourlittleblackbook.me }

𝗕𝗲𝗸𝗶𝗷𝗸 𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗲 𝘁𝗶𝗽𝘀 𝘃𝗶𝗮 𝗱𝗲 𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗸 𝗶𝗻 𝗺𝗶𝗷𝗻 𝗯𝗶𝗼. 𝗩𝗲𝗲𝗹 𝗽𝗹𝗲𝘇𝗶𝗲𝗿!

Ik ben benieuwd: wat staat er bij jullie op de planning deze vakantie?

PS: wij maken dit jaar een roadtrip van Barcelona naar Valencia en je kunt meereizen via stories.

Een kookschrift van oma. Drie Michelinsterren. En een prachtige jaren ’70-villa in een rustige wijk in Bangkok, waar je wordt verwelkomd alsof je bij de chefs thuis bent.  
  
De Sühring brothers koken niet vanuit trends, ze koken vanuit herinneringen. Aan de boerderij van hun grootmoeder, aan de smaken van hun jeugd in Duitsland en aan technieken die generaties lang zijn doorgegeven.  
  
@relaischateaux nodigde mij uit om Sühring tijdens een sololunch te ervaren en dit is toch wel mijn ultieme definitie van culinaire self-care.  
  
Dit was een verrassende middag in Bangkok, waar ik het beste Duitse eten at dat ik ooit heb geproefd. En dat in een stad waar ik normaal gesproken de Europese restaurants juist mijd. Nou, voor @restaurant\_suhring wil je écht die uitzondering maken.  
  
Signature dishes waar ik nog regelmatig aan terugdenk?  
🍴 Labskaus met kaviaar  
🍴 Enleta wafeltje met Pfalz vinegar  
🍴 Wildpastete  
🍴 Dry aged Duck  
  
TIP: reserveer als je naar Sühring gaat de seats bij de keukenbar, zodat je uitzicht hebt op de chefs die aan het werk zijn!  
  
Volg @yourlbb voor meer tips waarop je kunt vertrouwen.  
  
✖️✖️✖️  
🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck  
  
#prinvited #bangkok #michelin #relaischateaux   
In this reels I take you along for fine dining in Bangkok at the three Michelin starred restaurant Sühring.

Dit is een van de leukste gratis uitjes in de lente! Wist je dat je in Nederland op verschillende plekken zelf oesters kunt rapen (of plukken, voor de kenners 😉)? Dit weekend ben ik even in Zeeland en als het lukt gaan we weer.  
  
📍In deze video laat ik zien waar je je emmer kunt vullen.   
  
Comment oester voor de uitgebreide guide met alle details over alle 8 locaties + handige tips en regels.  
  
Belangrijk om te weten:  
 – Max. 10 kilo per persoon (≈ 1 emmer)  
 – Alleen voor eigen gebruik  
 – Niet scheppen of schrapen in de bodem  
 – Ga bij laag water  
  
Handig om mee te nemen:  
Emmer, schroevendraaier, laarzen, werkhandschoenen, theedoek, schone kleren en vuilniszakken (voor de oesters én vieze kleding).  
  
En of course: neem ook een oestermes, citroen en champagne mee 🍋🥂Want je wilt toch het liefst direct iets proeven!   
  
Het oesterseizoen loopt van september tot ongeveer april / begin mei, dus nu het water niet niet zo koud is, is dit het perfecte uitje.   
  
In de full guide deel ik precies waar je ze makkelijk kunt vinden, zonder veel moeite. Comment oester en ik stuur hem je direct!  
  
Met wie wil jij binnenkort oesters rapen?   
  
✖️✖️✖️

 – Alleen voor eigen gebruik
 – Niet scheppen of schrapen in de bodem
 – Ga bij laag water

Handig om mee te nemen:
