## Films kijken met een twist: dit zijn de leukste filmavonden in Amsterdam

### Cinair Europe

Films kijken onder de sterrenhemel, maar dan next level. Bij Cinair Europe worden bekende films vertoond op bijzondere buitenlocaties, denk aan historische gebouwen, tuinen of verborgen spots die je normaal niet zomaar ontdekt. Met een drankje in je hand en een koptelefoon op (silent cinema style) voelt het alsof je even in een andere wereld stapt. De exacte locaties en data verschillen per editie, [*dus houd de website in de gaten >>*](https://www.cinair.eu/en)

### Movienight bij de Supperclub

Als je zin hebt in een filmavond met nét dat beetje extra, dan moet je bij [Supper](https://supper.nl/movienights/) zijn. Iedere dinsdagavond toveren ze het restaurant om tot een intieme bios met grote klassiekers op het scherm, terwijl jij onderuit zakt in een van hun iconische witte loungebedden. Denk: cozy sfeer, lekkere bar bites en drankjes binnen handbereik. Reserveer op tijd, want deze filmavonden zijn populair!   
 ***Data:*** *– 28 april – Focus  
 – 12**Mei – The Tourist*

### Cinema Culinair

Sommige films moet je niet alleen zien, die moet je proeven. Bij Cinema Culinair kijk je niet zomaar naar films als Chef, Ratatouille of The Menu, je eet exact wat je op het scherm ziet of wat erop geïnspireerd is! Op de seconde nauwkeurig geserveerd, in de vorm van een brunch óf een 7-gangendiner. Denk: de scène start, het gerecht komt. Genieten met álle zintuigen dus. Perfect voor een originele brunchdate of een culinaire avond uit. [*Bekijk de volledige filmagenda van Cinema Culinair hier >>*](https://cinemaculinair.nl)

![Bioscoopagenda amsterdam ](https://www.yourlittleblackbook.me/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/bioscoopagenda-amsterdam-cinema-culinair-1.jpg)

Cinema Culinair © Your Little Black Book

## Bioscoop- en filmagenda Amsterdam 2026:  5 X nieuw in mei

### A Useful Ghost

Soms komt liefde in de meest onverwachte vormen terug. In A Useful Ghost is March ontroostbaar na het verlies van zijn vrouw, tot ze ineens weer opduikt… als geest in een stofzuiger. Klinkt absurd? Dat is het ook maar op een charmante manier. Terwijl zijn familie worstelt met deze bizarre situatie, blijkt Nat juist verrassend handig en helpt ze zelfs de familiezaak uit de problemen.   
 **Te zien bij**: *Pathé-bioscopen, Filmhallen, Kriterion, Rialto, FC Hyena, The Movies*  
 **Genre**: *Drama, Komedie*  
 **Te zien vanaf:** 7 *mei 2026*

### The Sheep Detectives

Wat als schapen stiekem slimmer zijn dan je denkt? In **The Sheep Detectives** lijkt het leven op de boerderij rustig, tot een mysterie alles op z’n kop zet. Terwijl herder George nietsvermoedend voorleest uit detectiveboeken, blijken zijn schapen altijd al mee hebben geluisterd. Tijd voor actie dus: ze nemen zelf het onderzoek in handen en duiken in een avontuur vol aanwijzingen, verdachte types en slimme vondsten.   
 **Te zien bij**: *Filmhallen, Pathé-bioscopen, Het Ketelhuis, VUE* // *, Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé-bioscopen*  
 **Genre**: *Familie, Komedie*  
 **Te zien vanaf:** *7 mei 2026*

### In the Grey

Een miljard dollar, een meedogenloze tegenstander en een missie die allesbehalve volgens plan verloopt. In In the Grey volgt een geheim team van elite-agenten dat uit de schaduw wordt gehaald om een gigantische diefstal recht te zetten. Wat begint als een strak georganiseerde operatie, verandert al snel in een gevaarlijk spel vol misleiding, strategie en overleven.   
 **Te zien bij**: *Pathé-bioscopen, VUE // Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé-bioscopen*  
 **Genre**: *Actie, Drama, Thriller*  
 **Te zien vanaf:** *14**mei 2026*

### Merrily We Roll Along

Wat gebeurt er met vriendschap als succes en ambitie in de weg gaan staan? Merrily We Roll Along volgt over een periode van dertig jaar de band tussen componist Franklin en zijn twee beste vrienden, schrijver Charley en Mary. Wat ooit begon als een hechte vriendschap, raakt langzaam uit balans door keuzes, carrière en het leven zelf.   
 **Te zien bij**: *Pathé-bioscopen, VUE  // Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé-bioscopen*  
 **Genre**: *Muziek, Drama, Thriller*  
 **Te zien vanaf:** *14**mei 2026*

### Wasteman

Een klein vergrijp, maar een groot gevolg. In Wasteman volgt het leven van Taylor, die een lange gevangenisstraf uitzit en vooral probeert te overleven door onopvallend te blijven. Zijn dagen vult hij met kleine routines en herinneringen aan zijn zoon. Maar wanneer vervroegde vrijlating ineens binnen handbereik komt, zet een nieuwe, charismatische celgenoot alles op scherp.  
 **Te zien bij**: *Pathé-bioscopen, The Movies, Kriterion, LAB111, Rialto, Studio/K, FC Hyena*  
 **Genre**: *Drama*  
 **Te zien vanaf:***21**mei 2026*

## Bioscoop- en filmagenda Amsterdam 2026: meer films om te zien in mei

### The Devil Wears Prada 2

Oké, dit is er dus eentje waar ik écht naar uitkijk. Twintig jaar na het eerste deel zijn Miranda Priestly, Andy en de rest terug in een compleet vernieuwde modewereld waar alles sneller, digitaler en genadelozer is dan ooit. Hoe zij zich daarin staande houden? Daar wil je bij zijn. Tip: kijk deel 1 nog even terug op [**Disney+**](https://www.disneyplus.com/en-pt/browse/entity-35ee3632-230a-4e4b-8520-46063f9282a3) of [**Prime Video**](https://www.primevideo.com/detail/The-Devil-Wears-Prada/0TZUGTVNJUZ5PN2X7WHS6I15H5) voor de ultieme throwback. Eén ding is zeker: dit wordt dé film voor modeliefhebbers en fans van het eerste uur.   
 **Te zien bij**: *Pathé-bioscopen, Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé-bioscopen*  
 **Genre**: *Drama, komedie*  
 **Te zien vanaf:** *30 april 2026*

### Michael

Michael Jackson mag er dan niet meer zijn, zijn invloed voel je nog steeds overal. In de nieuwe film Michael duik je in het leven van de King of Pop: van zijn doorbraak met de Jackson Five tot zijn legendarische solocarrière. De film laat niet alleen zijn grootste successen zien, maar ook de mens achter de muziek. Met indrukwekkende performances en iconische momenten krijg je het gevoel alsof je front row zit bij zijn verhaal. Een must-see voor fans van Michaels muziek én zijn nalatenschap.   
 **Te zien bij**: *VUE Amsterdam, The Movies, Pathé-bioscopen*  
 **Genre**: *Biografie, drama*

## Bioscoop- en filmagenda Amsterdam 2026: meer films om te zien in mei

### The Drama

Wat een droomweek richting je bruiloft had moeten zijn, verandert compleet wanneer een groot geheim uit het verleden op tafel komt. In The Drama volgen we Charlie en Emma. Hun relatie komt ineens onder hoogspanning te staan terwijl de grote dag steeds dichterbij komt. Met Zendaya en Robert Pattinson in de hoofdrollen krijg je een intens en ongemakkelijk eerlijk liefdesverhaal over vertrouwen, twijfel en de vraag: hoe goed ken je iemand eigenlijk?   
 **Te zien bij**: *VUE Amsterdam, The Movies, Studio/K, De Filmhallen, EYE, FC Hyena, Pathé-bioscopen*  
 **Genre**: *Romantiek, komedie en drama*

### A Family

In A Family volgen we tieners Eli en Nina, die ieder op hun eigen manier proberen om te gaan met de vechtscheiding van hun ouders (Carice van Houten en Pieter Embrechts). Terwijl ze heen en weer worden geslingerd tussen loyaliteit, boosheid en verdriet, zoeken ze allebei naar houvast. Een indringend en realistisch familiedrama dat laat zien wat er gebeurt als liefde onder druk komt te staan.   
 **Te zien bij**: *VUE Amsterdam, Rialto, Kriterion, Het Ketelhuis, De Filmhallen, EYE, FC Hyena, Pathé-bioscopen*  
 **Genre**: *Drama*

## Bioscoopagenda Amsterdam mei 2026: naar de film met de kids

### The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

Heeft jouw mini-me ook een Mario-fase? Dan wil je deze sowieso op de lijst zetten. In The Super Mario Galaxy Movie verruilt Mario het Mushroom Kingdom voor een avontuur in de ruimte, vol nieuwe werelden, uitdagingen en natuurlijk bekende gezichten. Na het succes van de vorige film belooft dit vervolg weer net zo leuk en actievol te worden.   
 **Te zien bij:** *Pathé-bioscopen, Studio/K, Het Ketelhuis, De Filmhallen*  
 **Genre:** *Avontuur, animatie*

### Tafiti – Dwars door de Woestijn

Een stokstaartje en een aardvarken als beste vrienden? Volgens opa kan dat echt niet. Maar wanneer Tafiti’s opa in levensgevaar is, móét hij de woestijn in op zoek naar een zeldzame bloem. Tegen wil en dank krijgt hij gezelschap van de vrolijke en een tikkeltje onhandige Borstel. Wat volgt is een spannend en grappig avontuur waarin ze ontdekken dat verschillen soms juist voor de beste vriendschappen zorgen.   
 **Te zien bij:** *Pathé-bioscopen, Het Ketelhuis*  
 **Genre:** *Animatie, kids*

 [![Just back from Catalonia and I thought: I'll share some tips right away. During our road trip, we stayed two nights in the Ebro Delta. An incredibly fun stop for families and foodies alike, where there is plenty to do. · A boat trip to the Musclarium is a must! Here you eat oysters and mussels straight from the farm, in the middle of the sea. · Admire the flamingos and black ibises that can be found here all year round. · Rice is grown in the Ebro Delta all year round. For paella, but Kensho also makes sake and miso from it! You can visit them during a tasting. · Cycling along the rice fields, canoeing on the Ebro River, and visiting various beaches. It was still a bit too cold now, but there is plenty to do. · The towns are very low-key and not that exciting, but you can find fantastic local food there. From the Michelin-awarded Les Moles* to local eateries like Vermuteria Bonmarisc and Lo Tinglado. We ate a lot of fish, but also anemone fritters (a first!). Now back to work, and I’m going to create an extensive travel guide of this road trip through Catalonia, but you’ll want to save these tips for a future adventure. ✖️✖️✖️ 🖊️ @anne_de_buck { In this post, I share tips for the Ebro Delta in Spain (Delta de l'Ebre).  [![A grandma's cookbook. Three Michelin stars. And a beautiful 70s villa in a quiet neighborhood in Bangkok, where you are welcomed as if you were at the chefs' home. The Sühring brothers don't cook based on trends; they cook based on memories. Of their grandmother's farm, of the flavors of their youth in Germany, and of techniques passed down through generations. @relaischateaux invited me to experience Sühring during a solo lunch, and this is truly my ultimate definition of culinary self-care. This was a surprising afternoon in Bangkok, where I ate the best German food I have ever tasted. And that in a city where I normally avoid European restaurants. Well, for @restaurant_suhring, you really want to make that exception. Signature dishes that I still think back to regularly? 🍴 Labskaus with caviar 🍴 Enleta waffle with Pfalz vinegar 🍴 Game pté 🍴 Dry-aged Duck TIP: When you go to Sühring, reserve seats at the kitchen bar so you have a view of the chefs at work!  [![This is one of the best free outings in the spring! Did you know that you can gather (or pick, for the connoisseurs 😉) oysters yourself at various locations in the Netherlands? I’m in Zeeland for a bit this weekend, and if we can make it, we’ll go again. 📍In this video, I show you where you can fill your bucket. Comment *oester* for the comprehensive guide with all the details about all 8 locations + handy tips and rules. Important to know: – Max. 10 kilos per person (≈ 1 bucket) – For personal use only – Do not scoop or scrape the seabed – Go at low tide Handy things to bring: Bucket, screwdriver, boots, work gloves, tea towel, clean clothes, and garbage bags (for the oysters *and* dirty clothes). And of course: bring an oyster knife, lemon, and champagne too 🍋🥂 Because you’ll want to taste something right away, won’t you! The oyster season runs from September to about April / early May, so now that the water isn't so cold, this is the perfect outing. In the full guide, I share exactly where you can easily find them, without much effort. Comment oyster and I'll send it to you right away! [![](../img/logo.png)](../index.html)

[Cinemas](cinemas.html) [Clubs](clubs.html) [Late Night Bars](nachtcafes.html) [Music stages](music-stages.html) [Theatres](theatres.html)

# Cinemas in Amsterdam

### What's up in Amsterdam's movie theatres?

Heading to the movies and enjoying the latest film is still a popular pastime among Amsterdammers. And they are spoilt for choice when it comes to seeing a movie.

Amsterdam also has a great number of smaller theatres that mainly focus on non-Hollywood productions. The best cinemas are listed here.

#### Amsterdam's main movie theatres (filmhuizen) and their profile

## [Cinecenter](https://cinecenter.nl/)

For true film lovers and the cultural film connoisseur. Cosy cinema with small screening rooms and a nice bar where you can see a lot of arthouse films.

*place* [Leidseplein](../about/leidseplein.html)

[![de balie](img/debalie-cinema.jpg "Cinema De Balie, Amsterdam")](../eatdrink/debalie.html)

## [Cinema De Balie](../eatdrink/debalie.html)

Vibrant cultural centre with bar and restaurant, screening short retrospectives, thematic festivals and special import movies.

*place* [Leidseplein](../about/leidseplein.html)

## [Cinema De Vlugt](https://www.cinemadevlugt.nl/)

The only cinema in Nieuw-West neighbourhood. The programming is determined by the people living in this district and ranges from blockbusters to quirky international cinema.

*place* [Nieuw-West](../about/nieuw-west.html)

## [Cinema The Pulse](https://www.cinemathepulse.com/)

Newly opened cinema in The Pulse of Amsterdam building at the Zuidas business district.

*place* [Zuidas](../about/zuidas.html)

## [De Uitkijk](https://uitkijk.nl/)

A a cosy little theatre in a historic, distinguished building on the Prinsengracht, where popular but also lesser-known cult films are shown. Run by students and with table service directly to your seats.

*place* [Leidseplein](../about/leidseplein.html)

[![eye](../about/photos/modern/03a.jpg "EYE Film Institute, Amsterdam")](eye.html)

## [EYE Film Institute](eye.html)

The National center of cinematography, located opposite Amsterdam's Centraal Station on the banks of the River IJ.

*place* [Noord](../about/noord.html)

## [FC Hyena](https://fchyena.nl/films)

Relatively new cinema and restaurant in an old garage. No cinema seats here, but large orange couches. Perfect dinner & movie spot.

*place* [Noord](../about/noord.html)

[![filmhallen](img/filmhallen-s.jpg "Filmhallen, Amsterdam West")](https://www.filmhallen.nl/)

## [FilmHallen](https://www.filmhallen.nl/)

For better films from Hollywood as award-winning art house and documentary films from Europe and the rest of the world. Screening room 7 is the beautiful art-deco design interior decorations of the original Cinema Parisien (dating back to 1909) which is included in the UNESCO Memory of the World Register.

*place* [Oud-West](../about/oud-west.html)

## [Filmhuis Cavia](https://www.filmhuiscavia.nl/)

Founded in 1983 by the squatters’ movement in the Staatsliedenbuurt neighbourhood. It screens contemporary independent cinema from around the world, non-profit art films and historical films. With just 40 seats, it is the smallest cinema in Amsterdam.

*place* [West](../about/west.html)

## [Kriterion](https://www.kriterion.nl/)

Student-run film house where you can not only watch a movie, but also have a drink or a meal. Wide range of films, from forgotten documentaries to American blockbusters.

*place* [De Plantage](../about/plantage.html)

## [Lab 111](https://www.lab111.nl/#!/programma)

Alternative film house in a fascinating building with a relaxed restaurant. Screening of animation films, unconventional documentaries and films.

*place* [Oud-West](../about/oud-west.html)

## [Melkweg Cinema](melkweg.html)

For real music lovers. Music films and documentaries are shown on the 1st floor of this multifunctional cultural centre.

*place* [Leidseplein](../about/leidseplein.html)

## [Pathé Arena](https://en.pathe.nl/bioscoop/arena)

Large and modern cinema, located well outside the city centre of Amsterdam near the Johan Cruijff Stadium. Go there to see large Hollywood productions.

*place* [Zuidoost](../about/zuidoost.html)

## [Pathé De Munt](https://en.pathe.nl/bioscoop/demunt)

Modern cinema complex with large auditoriums, mixing mainstream releases with arty alternatives.

*place* [Rembrandtplein](../about/rembrandtplein.html)

## [Pathé City](https://en.pathe.nl/bioscoop/city)

Former City Theater cinema dating back to 1935. Popular among young people.

*place* [Leidseplein](../about/leidseplein.html)

## [Plein Theater](https://plein-theater.nl/en/agenda)

Idealistic neighbourhood theatre focusing on the different cultures of Amsterdam.

*place* [Oost](../about/oost.html)

## [Rialto](https://rialtofilm.nl/en/calendar)

Film theatre from 1921 consisting of 3 auditoriums and a film café. The screening focuses mainly on European and non-Western artistic film productions.

*place* [De Pijp](../about/depijp.html)

## [Studio/K](studio-k.html)

Small cinema, bar and restaurant run by a group of enthusiastic students.

*place* [Oost](../about/oost.html)

[![tuschinski](img/tuschinski-s.jpg "Pathé Tuschinski, Amsterdam")](tuschinski.html)

## [Theater Tuschinski](tuschinski.html)

A beautiful art-deco movie theatre located near Rembrandtplein. For a chic cinematic experience go to a film in the main auditorium and book one of the love seats. Often used for national premieres of large films.

*place* [Rembrandtplein](../about/rembrandtplein.html)

## [The Movies](https://www.themovies.nl/)

Historic art house movie theatre from 1912 with art deco interior, nice restaurant & screening rooms where you can watch mainly arthouse films, documentaries and the better Hollywood productions.

*place* [Jordaan](../about/jordaan.html)

## [Ventilator Cinema](https://amsterdamalternative.nl/ventilator)

Cinema and bar on the 2nd floor of the OT301 cultural centre with a unique, underground atmosphere.

*place* [Oud-West](../about/oud-west.html)

hits.content:
![Offscreen Tourist](https://almostginger.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Main-Logo_Transparent.png)
![Offscreen Tourist](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

# 14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

Offscreen Tourist posts contain affiliate links. If you make a purchase using one of these links, I may receive compensation at no extra cost to you. See the [disclaimer here](https://almostginger.com/terms/) for more information.

People who say “watching movies on holiday is a waste of time because you can do the same thing at home” haven’t visited one of the **many amazing independent cinemas in Amsterdam.**

It’s akin to saying “why would you eat pasta in Italy? You can just eat pasta at home.” And somewhere in the UK, at the most authentic Italian restaurant, there will be great pasta. And the pasta will be just as good as most restaurants in Italy. But it wouldn’t be the *same.*

Though Amsterdam isn’t known as being some famous film city, Amsterdammers do *love*their cinemas. **Watching an arthouse film in one of the many independent cinemas in Amsterdam is a truly unique, cultural experience.**

“Well, you can walk into a movie theatre in Amsterdam and buy a beer. And I don’t mean just like in no paper cup, I’m talking about a glass of beer.” – Vincent Vega, *Pulp Fiction*(1994)

Grab a Heineken and check out the best arthouse and independent cinemas in Amsterdam…

# 14 of the Best Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | Amsterdam Cinemas | Arthouse Cinemas in Amsterdam | Amsterdam film | filmtheater Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 1. Pathé Tuschinski

**Address:**Reguliersbreestraat 26-34, 1017 CN

The most beautifully ornate cinema in Amsterdam and quite possibly the world, Pathé Tuschinski has to be on any film fan’s short list of cinemas.

Though it’s not *technically*an independent cinema anymore as it screens mainly mainstream films, the Art Deco look and history of the place is what makes it a must-visit.

The three other Pathé cinemas in Amsterdam (Pathé Arena, Pathé City and Pathé De Munt) are also not what I would consider ‘Arthouse’ or Independent cinemas. So, sadly they won’t be on this list! There are lots of Pathé cinemas all over the Netherlands, it seems to be the main cinema chain in the country.

[Visit the Pathé Tuschinski website here.](https://www.pathe.nl/bioscoop/tuschinski)

[![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)](https://almostginger.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/10/PB210669.jpg)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 2. EYE Film Museum

**Address:**IJpromenade 1, 1031 KT Amsterdam

I’ve already written at great length about the wonderfulness that is the EYE Film Museum. [Check out my entire blog post on the EYE Film Museum here!](https://almostginger.com/eye-film-museum-amsterdam/)

[Visit the EYE Film Museum website here.](https://www.eyefilm.nl/)

[![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)](https://almostginger.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/10/PB200145.jpg)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 3. Rialto Film Theatre

**Address:**Ceintuurbaan 338, 1072 GN

One of the most authentic, arthouse, pre-war theatres in Amsterdam! For the finer film fan only.

[Visit the Rialto Film Theatre website here.](https://www.rialtofilm.nl/)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 4. Filmtheater De Fabriek

**Address:**Jan Sijbrandsteeg 12, 1502 BA Zaandam

Though this cinema is a *little*out of the way in the next town over, Zaandam, I’ll include it because it’s one of the most highly recommended cinemas in Amsterdam. Literally translating to ‘The Factory’, this cinema hosts a regular event called the ‘Men’s Evening’ which screens ‘man’ films and includes a Budweiser and a mini burger for a very reasonable €5.50.

However, ‘tough women’ are also welcome, so something tells me the name is just designed to grab your attention.

[Visit the Filmtheater De Fabrick website here.](http://www.de-fabriek.nl/)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 5. Filmhuis Cavia

**Address:**Van Hallstraat 52-1HG, 1051 HH

This is one of the most cult, underground, independent cinemas in Amsterdam. More like an ‘experiment’ or ‘project’ than a working cinema, Filmhuis Cavia screens whatever films they like. If you’re an artsy student, you might like what they’re screening!

[Visit the Filmhuis Cavia website here.](http://www.filmhuiscavia.nl/)

[![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)](https://almostginger.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/10/PB200436.jpg)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 6. LAB 111

**Address:**Arie Biemondstraat 111, 1054 PD

LAB 111 legit looks *amazing*and if I lived in Amsterdam I’d definitely be hitting up this place. Their film programming suits me to a T.

For starters, one of their programmes is ‘Brunch & Film.’ BRUNCH AND FILM, YOU GUYS!

Recent programmes also sound great, including Hitchcock’s Summer of Suspense, Expat Cinema and they even screened *Mean Girls*(2003) for Mean Girls Day on October 3rd.

[Visit the LAB 111 website here.](https://www.lab111.nl/)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 7. Filmhallen

**Address:**Hannie Dankbaarpassage 12, 1053 RT

This looks like a bloody gorgeous cinema. Urgh. I’m almost mad at how gorgeous it looks because I can’t visit any time soon. Filmhallen screens a lot of English indie films as well as some of the most, erm, mainstream world cinema, if that makes sense? And it’s got such pretty cinema screens.

It’s the biggest independent cinema in the Netherlands, no less!

[Visit the Filmhallen website here.](https://www.filmhallen.nl/)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 8. Cinecenter Amsterdam

**Address:**Lijnbaansgracht 236, 1017 PH

So far, all the independent cinemas in Amsterdam on this list are well-maintained, beautifully restored cinemas screening arthouse films in comfort and beautiful decor.

What about the grassroots, we’re-just-doing-our-best-here indie cinemas? If that’s the arthouse cinema in Amsterdam you’re yearning for, look no further than Cinecenter. They screen arthouse films in a building with padded walls and a classic 1970s-style carpet that explodes with pattern.

[Visit the Cinecenter Amsterdam website here.](https://cinecenter.nl/)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 9. The Movies Amsterdam

**Address:**Haarlemmerdijk 161, 1013 KH

Yet *another*cinema with classic Art Deco style. Honestly, I think that’s four cinemas now, it’s just too much. The decor inside the cinema screens is so beautiful, I imagine it would be quite distracting when watching films.

The Movies also boasts a fantastic restaurant and screens the most popular world cinema.

[Visit The Movies Amsterdam website here.](https://www.themovies.nl/)

[![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)](https://almostginger.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/10/PB200424.jpg)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 10. Filmtheater Kriterion

**Address:**Roetersstraat 170, 1018 WE

Most of the independent cinemas in Amsterdam on this list were built pre-war, including the last cinema which opened in 1912. However, the Kriterion was founded in 1945 by anti-Nazi students. To this day, it’s still mainly students that keep this place open.

Check out Kriterion for popular awards-worthy films and a weird Arthouse selection.

[Visit the Filmtheater Kriterion website here.](https://www.kriterion.nl/)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 11. Het Ketelhuis

**Address:**Pazzanistraat 4, 1014 DB

I visited this independent cinema in Amsterdam during the [IDFA 2016 Documentary Film Festival](https://almostginger.com/amsterdam-film-festival-2016/). It’s a cinema inside an old industrial building with a café/bar and I remember enjoying a glass of Erdinger beer during the film.

Yes, I have drunk a beer, in a glass (not a paper cup), in a cinema just as Vincent Vega from *Pulp Fiction*did before me. It really is an exciting and enviable life I lead.

[Visit the Het Ketelhuis website here.](https://www.ketelhuis.nl/)

[![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)](https://almostginger.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/10/IMAG0783.jpg)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 12. Studio/K

**Address:**Timorplein 62, 1094 CC

This place looks like a super chill place to hang on a Sunday morning. It’s a café/bar/club/art gallery/gig venue/cinema all rolled into one.

They tend to screen a few films over and over which I completely respect as it must be expensive to screen a bunch of different films only a handful of times. And actually, I wish some cinemas would do this more because independent films leave cinemas way too fast!

[Visit the Studio/K website here.](http://studio-k.nu/)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 13. Filmtheater De Uitkijk

**Address:**Prinsengracht 452, 1017 KE

Not just one of the oldest continually-running independent cinemas in Amsterdam, but in the whole of the Netherlands! This cinema has just the one screen with private balcony seating.

[Visit the De Uitkijk website here.](http://www.uitkijk.nl/)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

## 14. De Balie

**Address:**Kleine-Gartmanplantsoen 10, 1017 RR

Housed in a former courthouse, De Balie is more of a cultural centre than a straight cinema. They also host debates, theatre and sociopolitical talk shows as well as having a café.

[Visit the De Balie website here.](https://www.debalie.nl/home/)

![14 of the Best Arthouse/Independent Cinemas in Amsterdam | almostginger.com](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

**Those are the best independent cinemas in Amsterdam? Have you watched a film at a cinema in Amsterdam? Did you enjoy your experience? Let me know in the comments below!**

**Read next:**

[Hollywood Film Locations in Amsterdam: Ocean’s Twelve, Diamonds are Forever & More!](https://almostginger.com/hollywood-film-locations-amsterdam/)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)
![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

# Open Air Film Festival

Welcome to Pluk de Nacht – the most adventurous outdoor film festival in the Netherlands! In August 2025, we’re back in Amsterdam and Utrecht with a surprising [film program](https://plukdenacht.nl/en/filmprogramma/) under the stars.

Most of the program has been revealed and you can finally [reserve your deck chair](https://plukdenacht.nl/en/tickets/) for the film of your choice. We’ll be announcing a few more main films soon, and all the short films that screen before the features will be added to the schedule shortly.

If you purchased a presale ticket, you can now use your code to reserve one or more screenings – depending on how many presale tickets you bought. You can also split your credit across multiple screenings to enjoy different film nights. Want to check how much credit you have left? Simply [visit this page](https://plukdenacht.nl/voucher-checker/).

[Tickets](https://plukdenacht.nl/en/tickets/) are now available to everyone! Ready for a magical film night? Reserve your spot under the stars!

hits.content:
# Film theater Rialto, Amsterdam’s cinema

The theater in Amsterdam for arthouse and non-Western cinema



Rialto has been a household name for film enthusiasts in Amsterdam since 1921. Rialto is the perfect cinema in Amsterdam to see all kinds of films, such as premieres, classics, documentaries, short films, or arthouse cinema. At our two locations, in the heart of De Pijp and on De Boelelaan, you can enjoy the best new films from both the Netherlands and abroad. Discover everything from touching productions to works by emerging filmmakers, making Rialto a true gem among Amsterdam cinemas.

![](https://rialto.ams3.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/uploads/679a350e34e590cac9150ad0d3f91d829592dac5a86a5.jpg)

##### Amsterdam cinemas: what does Amsterdam offer?

Amsterdam cinemas offer something for everyone, and Rialto is no exception. Rialto leads the way with a unique selection of arthouse films, documentaries, classics, and additional specials. As a central part of the cinema Amsterdam landscape, Rialto operates two must-visit locations:

[**Rialto De Pijp**](nl/locaties/1/rialto-de-pijp)

Rialto De Pijp is an intimate film theater with a distinctive program, setting itself apart as a hub for arthouse cinema in Amsterdam. This is the place for lovers of special films, ranging from non-Western cinema to groundbreaking documentaries. Located at Ceintuurbaan 338, Rialto De Pijp offers a central spot with a warm atmosphere for film enthusiasts across Amsterdam-Oost, West, Zuid, the city center, and beyond.

[**Rialto VU**](nl/locaties/7/rialto-vu)

Rialto VU is located on the campus of the Vrije Universiteit, at De Boelelaan 1111. As the newest cinema in Amsterdam Zuid, this location features a diverse selection of films, from arthouse gems to major Hollywood productions. Catering to both students and local residents, Rialto VU delivers a contemporary take on the Amsterdam cinema experience.

##### What is the movie schedule in Amsterdam?

At Rialto, you can watch films daily, with the screening week always running from Thursday to Wednesday. The cinema times vary by location and film, so you can always find a showing that fits your schedule. Want to know which movies are playing and at what time? Check out our [Amsterdam movie schedule](nl/agenda) for an overview of all screenings.

[Cinema schedule in Rialto](/nl/agenda)

##### Buying tickets at Rialto

Buying tickets for films at Rialto is simple and fast. Whether you plan to visit Rialto De Pijp or Rialto VU, you can order your tickets online via our website to secure your spot. From the latest premieres to timeless classics, our film program has something for everyone. Prefer to buy in person? Tickets are also available at the box office at both locations. Rialto offers [various discount options](nl/tickets) to make your experience even more enjoyable.

##### Cineville at film theater Rialto

For Cineville pass holders, accessing beautiful cinema has never been easier. With a Cineville pass, you can watch all films at Rialto for free. This makes it the perfect way to enjoy unlimited screenings at one of the best cinemas in Amsterdam. Don’t have a Cineville subscription yet? Learn more about its benefits and how to get one via the Cineville website.

##### Kids movies at Rialto

Rialto is young at heart, which is why we also screen fun and exciting kids' movies suitable for children aged 3 to 12. Additionally, we organize activities for kids such as crafting sessions and LEGO workshops. Explore our full offering on the [kids' movies](nl/specials/2/films-voor-kinderen) page and find out why Rialto is a favorite among Amsterdam cinemas for family outings.

[Kids movies in Rialto](/nl/specials/2/films-voor-kinderen)

##### Why choose Rialto?

If you’re looking for a cinema in Amsterdam that offers more than the standard selection, Rialto is the place for you. With locations in De Pijp and Amsterdam-Zuid, we provide a unique experience for film lovers. Our intimate theaters, diverse screenings, and focus on non-Western cinema sets us apart from other Amsterdam movie theaters. Whether it’s the latest arthouse films, thrilling documentaries, or special screenings, Rialto platforms beautiful stories that deserve to be told.

At cinema Rialto, we don’t just show films—we create an experience you cannot miss out on. From the latest arthouse releases to timeless classics, Rialto is the Amsterdam cinema where every visit is special. Discover [our extensive program](nl/films), plan your visit to Rialto De Pijp or Rialto VU, and immerse yourself in the best of what Amsterdam cinemas have to offer.

[Films in Rialto](/nl/films)

Playing today at Rialto

[Go to the calendar](https://rialtofilm.nl/en/calendar)

## Over Koninklijk Theater Tuschinski

[Koninklijk Theater Tuschinski - Reguliersbreestraat 26-34 - 1017 CN Amsterdam](https://maps.google.com/maps?cid=6023732845231591430)

Welkom bij Koninklijk Theater Tuschinski! Op deze website vind je de actuele filmagenda en praktische informatie om jouw bioscoopbezoek compleet te maken.

6

Zalen

![Parkeren](https://media.pathe.nl/cinema/common/services/105583/parkeren-web.png)

Parkeren

![Ondersteuning](https://media.pathe.nl/cinema/common/services/105579/ondersteuning-web.png)

Ondersteuning

![Openingstijden](https://media.pathe.nl/cinema/common/services/105587/icon-openingstijden-web.png)

Openingstijden

Zalen

Neem plaats in een van onze bioscoopzalen en geniet van perfect beeld en geluid

Parkeren

**1. APCOA Prins en Keizer**   
 Het tarief is €6 per uur. Bij de bioscoop kun je een voordelige parkeerkaart kopen voor €13. Met deze parkeerkaart kun je drie uur parkeren. De kaarten zijn uitsluitend verkrijgbaar aan de kassa in de foyer van Koninklijk Theater Tuschinski. De kaarten zijn zogenaamde nasteekkaarten. Bij het afrekenen moet eerst de inrijkaart worden ingevoerd en vervolgens de kaart die Pathé verstrekt.  
 **Adres** Prinsengracht 927

**2. Q-Park Waterlooplein**   
 Reserveer je parkeerplaats met voordeel bij [Q-Park](https://www.q-park.nl/nl-nl/parkeren/amsterdam/ams-pathe-tuschinski/?utm_source=pathe&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=deal)en betaal na 18:00 uur slechts € 13,50 voor 4 uur parkeren.  
 **Adres** Valkenburgerstraat 238

**Gehandicaptenparkeerplaats** De dichtstbijzijnde gehandicaptenparkeerplaats vind je op de Amstel ter hoogte van nummer 30.

Ondersteuning

**Rolstoelmogelijkheden**  
 Alle zalen zijn rolstoeltoegankelijk en gelijkvloers (via lift) te bereiken. Rolstoelvriendelijk toilet aanwezig.  
   
 **Auditieve en visuele ondersteuning**  
 Door middel van Subcatch & Earcatch.  
   
 **Gevonden voorwerpen**  
 Binnen 48 uur worden gevonden voorwerpen op [ILost.co](https://ilost.co/nl/org/pathe?country=nl&location=1051) gezet.  
   
 **Overlast SMS code**  
 SMS *'TUSCH' 'zaalnummer' 'reden voor melding'*naar 3669.

Openingstijden

**Dagelijks** vanaf 15 minuten voor aanvang van de eerste voorstelling.

* Als je met het openbaar vervoer naar Koninklijk Theater Tuschinski komt, dan kun je (bijvoorbeeld via [www.9292.nl](http://www.9292.nl/)) jouw reis plannen naar Tram/bushalte Rembrandtplein of Metrostation Rokin (5 minuten lopen).

## Heb je een vraag?

Bezoek onze klantenservicepagina voor de meestgestelde vragen.

[Naar de klantenservice](https://www.pathe.nl/nl/help)

Voor de bereikbaarheid van onze bioscopen kun je [hier](https://www.pathe.nl/nl/info/parkeermogelijkheden) terecht. Maak je gebruik van OV? Neem dan een kijkje bij de praktische informatie van jouw bioscoop naar keuze.

De speelweken bij Pathé lopen van donderdag tot en met woensdag. Elke maandag maken wij het filmprogramma voor de nieuwe speelweek. Elke maandagavond na 18:00 uur wordt het nieuwe programma online gezet, dit gebeurd in de loop van de avond.

Enkele voorstellingen worden eerder al online gezet. Dit zijn hoofdzakelijk IMAX, 4DX, Dolby Cinema, Dolby Atmos en ScreenX voorstellingen. Na maandagavond is de programmatie compleet tot en met de woensdag een week later.

Al onze bioscopen zijn pin only. Hier kan **niet** met contant geld betaald worden.

In de bioscopen van Pathé kan betaald worden met:

- Bankpas (MAESTRO)

- Creditcard (VISA en Mastercard),

- Apple Pay

- Pathé Giftcard (tickets en Drinks & Bites)

- Nationale Bioscoopbon (alleen tickets)



Maak een account aan of log in om verder te gaan

InloggenEen account aanmaken

Log in en beheer gemakkelijk je:

* Tickets
* Favoriete bioscoop
* Club Pathé pas
* Bekeken films

hits.title: Indy Film Library finalises 2026 selection for two-day Amsterdam showpiece – Indy Film Library
hits.snippet:
Among the movies featured, cinema lovers will have a chance to witness breath-taking experimental, documentary and narrative shorts. These include May Bresser’s heartfelt coming of age drama Wat we achterlachten; Sando Heijnen’s unnerving experimental thriller Eindewerk; and Lisa Hukker‘s rip-roaring musical documentary Glock ’45: The Movie.

All films come with English subtitles for international viewers.

On the second evening of films, the event will move over to Amsterdam’s Supermercator venue, on Sunday May 3rd – for three more programmes of shorts, and an international documentary film. [...] Onderling de getoonde films hebben filmliefhebbers de kans om adembenemende experimentele, documentaires en verhalende shorts te zien. Daarbij zit May Bresser’s oprechte coming of age drama Wat we achterlachten; Sando Heijnen’s zenuwslopende experimentele thriller Eindewerk; en Lisa Hukker‘s spetterende muzikale documentaire Glock ’45: The Movie.

Alle films komen met Engelse ondertiteling voor internationaal publiek.

Op de tweede filmavond verhuist het evenement naar Supermercator in Amsterdam op Zondag 3 mei 2026. Drie nieuwe shortfilm programma’s en een internationale documentaire worden getoond. [...] We beginnen met Danny Mitchell en Ross Domoney’s Threads of a Revolution – een speelfilm documentaire over de democratische experimenten van een Koerdische gemeenschap in de regio Rojava – De avond sluit met briljant nieuw werk uit de Benelux. Inclusief Luuk Audenaerde’s moorddadige thriller Nevenschade, en Marlena Stam’s experimentele LGBT+ mythologie Narcissus.

IFL hoofdredacteur Jack Benjamin zegt: “ Deze aankondiging lijkt moeilijker elk jaar – we zijn overweldigd door de kwaliteit die we hebben ontvangen. De hoofdpijn die erbij komt kijken om het beste uit te kiezen elke keer is precies wat we willen hier bij Indy Film Library. Ik kan oprecht niet wachten om deze artiesten te introduceren aan ons publiek deze Mei.”
hits.content:
![Indy Film Library](https://i0.wp.com/indyfilmlibrary.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/New-Logo-Sharpened-Black.png?fit=3806%2C1146&ssl=1)

[Indy Film Library](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/)

Indy Film Library Awards Showcase – 2nd & 3rd May 2026 – Tickets on sale now

![](https://i0.wp.com/indyfilmlibrary.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Smal-Poster-2.png?resize=2000%2C1200&ssl=1)

# Indy Film Library finalises 2026 selection for two-day Amsterdam showpiece

![Indy Film Library](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/cbeda66fd3c559f683df8ca0ecb9e0ebd1873a3a4aca20be96db1da2a2a8f334?s=96&d=identicon&r=g)

Indy Film Library (IFL) is hosting two days of film screenings, celebrating the very best work from artists around the world.

Forming the first part of the organisation’s yearly awards shortlist, a selection of international shorts will kick off the event at Amsterdam’s **Filmhuis Cavia**, on **Saturday May 2nd 2026**.

Two programmes of shorts from Benelux-based filmmakers will follow, as the seventh annual event looks to introduce audiences to films which they will not find anywhere else, by filmmakers who represent the future of the film industry in the Netherlands and beyond.

Among the movies featured, cinema lovers will have a chance to witness breath-taking experimental, documentary and narrative shorts. These include **May Bresser**’s heartfelt coming of age drama **[*Wat we achterlachten*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/11/07/wat-we-achterlaten-the-things-we-leave-behind-2025-4-stars/)**; **Sando Heijnen**’s unnerving experimental thriller **[*Eindewerk*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/10/06/eindwerk-final-work-2024-4-stars/)**; and **Lisa Hukker**‘s rip-roaring musical documentary **[*Glock ’45: The Movie*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2026/01/21/glock-45-the-movie-4-stars/)**.

All films come with English subtitles for international viewers.

On the second evening of films, the event will move over to Amsterdam’s **Supermercator** venue, on **Sunday May 3rd** – for three more programmes of shorts, and an international documentary film.

After kicking off with **Danny Mitchell** and **Ross Domoney**’s **[*Threads of a Revolution*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/04/30/threads-of-a-revolution-2024-4-stars/)** – a feature-length documentary about the democratic experiments of a Kurdish community in the Rojava region – the night will close out with a procession of bright new works from the Benelux. Shorts include **Luuk Audenaerde**’s murderous thriller ***[Nevenschade](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/09/23/nevenschade-moral-hazard-2025-5-stars/)***, and **Marlena Stam**’s experimental LGBT+ mythology ***[Narcissus](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/06/13/narcissus-2025-5-stars/)***.

IFL Chief Editor Jack Benjamin said of the selection, “This announcement seems to get harder every year – we have been overwhelmed by the quality of work we received this season. But whittling down a programme to the very best of the best is exactly the kind of headache we want at Indy Film Library. I am truly excited to introduce these artists to our audiences this May.”

Following the screenings, the winning films of IFL’s 2026 submissions cycle will be announced. Each selection is rated 16+, and all films will show in Dutch or Flemish, with English subtitles.

**Tickets**are now on sale from **April 1st** via the **IFL website**. Tickets for [Day 1 are available here](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/tickets-cavia-ifl-awards-showcase/), and tickets for [Day 2 are available here](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/indy-film-awards-tickets/). Admission will also be available at the door, but attendees should purchase in advance to avoid disappointment.

### The full programme of films at Filmhuis Cavia on May 2nd is as follows:

**15:30 Doors Open**

**16:00 International Programme**

**[Full programme listing here](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2026/03/27/tickets-on-sale-for-ifl-international-showcase-at-filmhuis-cavia/)**

**19:00 Short Film Programme 1: Growth**

[*Pepita*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/09/17/pepita-2025-4-stars/)  
[*Wat we achterlachten*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/11/07/wat-we-achterlaten-the-things-we-leave-behind-2025-4-stars/)  
[*Le Sous Sol*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/08/06/le-sous-sol-2025-4-stars/)  
[*I must go on*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/05/09/i-must-go-on-2024-4-stars/)  
[*360°*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/06/27/360-2025-3-5-stars/)

**20:15-20:45 Q&A**

**21:00 Short Film Programme 2**: **Compositions**

[*Napoli-Amsterdam*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/06/16/napoli-amsterdam-2025-4-stars/)  
[*Eindewerk*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/10/06/eindwerk-final-work-2024-4-stars/)  
[*Cel Maatjes*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/12/10/cel-maatjes-cell-buddies-2025-4-stars/)  
[*Brother, may I have some oats too?*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/06/20/brother-may-i-have-some-oats-too-2025-3-5-stars/)  
[*Glock ’45: The Movie*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2026/01/21/glock-45-the-movie-4-stars/)

**22:00-22:30 Q&A**

**23:00 Close**

*NOTE: Filmhuis Cavia is an old venue. It is not accessible to people with disabilities.* *More information on accessing the venue is available*[***here***](https://www.filmhuiscavia.nl/info/bezoekersinformatie)*.*

### The full programme of films at Supermercator on May 3rd is as follows:

**15:00 Doors Open**

**16:00 Feature Documentary**

[*Threads of a Revolution*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/04/30/threads-of-a-revolution-2024-4-stars/)

**18:00 Short Film Programme 3: Audio-Visuals**

[*Pick you up at 6: Be here*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/07/28/pick-you-up-at-6-be-here-2025-5-stars/)  
[*Echt*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/09/12/echt-2025-4-stars/)

**18:35 Break**

**19:00 Short Film Programme 4: The Beat**

[*Nur Geträumt*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/12/17/nur-getraumt-just-a-dream-2025-4-5-stars/)  
[*Onderaards*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/07/18/onderaards-2025-the-beneath-4-stars/)  
[*Bubbling Baby*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/06/30/bubbling-baby-2025-4-5-stars/)  
[*Sonnet*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/08/11/sonnet-2025-3-5-stars/)  
[*Zeevonk*](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2026/02/18/zeevonk-2025-3-5-stars/)

**20:00-20:30 Q&A**

**21:00 Short Film Programme 5: Nightlife**

*[Nachtwolken](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2026/03/04/noctilucent-clouds-nachtwolken-2025-4-stars/)  
[Narcissus](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/06/13/narcissus-2025-5-stars/)  
[Nevenschade](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/09/23/nevenschade-moral-hazard-2025-5-stars/)  
[Alles is OK](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/07/21/alles-is-ok-everything-is-fine-2025-4-5-stars/)*

**22:20 Q&A**

**22:45 Winners of festival announced**

**23:00 Close**

![](https://i0.wp.com/indyfilmlibrary.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Smal-General.png?resize=780%2C654&ssl=1)

**/// NEDERLANDS ///**

Indy Film Library (IFL) laat een dag vol screenings zien, ter ere van het beste werk door filmmakers uit de hele wereld.

Het eerste onderdeel van de organisatie jaarlijkse shortlist prijzen, een selectie van internationale shortfilms start het evenement bij **Filmhuis Cavia** in Amsterdam op **Zaterdag 2 mei 2026.**

Twee shortfilm programma’s door filmmakers uit de Benelux. Het zevende jaarlijkse festival wilt graag het publiek introduceren met films die je nergens anders kunt zien. Films die de toekomst van Nederlandse en de daarbuiten filmindustrie representeren.

Onderling de getoonde films hebben filmliefhebbers de kans om adembenemende experimentele, documentaires en verhalende shorts te zien. Daarbij zit **May Bresser**’s oprechte coming of age drama [***Wat we achterlachten***](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/11/07/wat-we-achterlaten-the-things-we-leave-behind-2025-4-stars/); **Sando Heijnen**’s zenuwslopende experimentele thriller [***Eindewerk***](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/10/06/eindwerk-final-work-2024-4-stars/); en **Lisa Hukker**‘s spetterende muzikale documentaire [***Glock ’45: The Movie***](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2026/01/21/glock-45-the-movie-4-stars/).

Alle films komen met Engelse ondertiteling voor internationaal publiek.

Op de tweede filmavond verhuist het evenement naar **Supermercator** in Amsterdam op **Zondag 3 mei 2026.** Drie nieuwe shortfilm programma’s en een internationale documentaire worden getoond.

We beginnen met **Danny Mitchell** en **Ross Domoney**’s [***Threads of a Revolution***](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/04/30/threads-of-a-revolution-2024-4-stars/) – een speelfilm documentaire over de democratische experimenten van een Koerdische gemeenschap in de regio Rojava – De avond sluit met briljant nieuw werk uit de Benelux. Inclusief **Luuk Audenaerde**’s moorddadige thriller [***Nevenschade***](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/09/23/nevenschade-moral-hazard-2025-5-stars/), en **Marlena Stam**’s experimentele LGBT+ mythologie [***Narcissus***](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/2025/06/13/narcissus-2025-5-stars/).

IFL hoofdredacteur Jack Benjamin zegt: “ Deze aankondiging lijkt moeilijker elk jaar – we zijn overweldigd door de kwaliteit die we hebben ontvangen. De hoofdpijn die erbij komt kijken om het beste uit te kiezen elke keer is precies wat we willen hier bij Indy Film Library. Ik kan oprecht niet wachten om deze artiesten te introduceren aan ons publiek deze Mei.”

Na de vertoning worden de winnende films van IFL’s 2026 inzendingen aangekondigd. Elke selectie is 16+, en alle films zijn in het Nederlands of Vlaams met Engelse ondertiteling.

**Kaartjes** zijn verkrijgbaar via de **[IFL website](https://indyfilmlibrary.com/ifl-programme/)**. Toegang is ook mogelijk bij de deur, maar om teleurstelling te voorkomen word het aangeraden om een kaartje van te voren te kopen.

# CinemAsia Film Festival Amsterdam 2026: Five Days of Stories That Cross Every Border

![CinemAsia Film Festival Amsterdam 2026: Five Days of Stories That Cross Every Border](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/s6i9319f/production/3b4c77f7e72847e84b4c024d3a0d4490242519ce-1200x800.webp?w=1200&fm=webp&q=75&fit=fill)

"The Waves Will Carry Us" by director **Lau Kek-Huat**

**April in Amsterdam** still has that early-spring edge, the light is longer but the air is sharp, and the city is somewhere between waking up and deciding what kind of year it wants to have. It is a good moment for **cinema**. And for **five days** in April, the [**CinemAsia Film Festival**](https://cinemasia.nl/en/2026/02/10/cinemasia-2026-the-rediscovery-of-our-common-grounds/) makes Amsterdam one of the most interesting places in Europe to watch a film.

The **18th edition of CinemAsia** Film Festival Amsterdam 2026 runs from **8 to 12 April**, spreading across **four venues** in the city. It brings **40+ films** from across Asia and the Asian diaspora, two attending directors with genuinely remarkable stories, and a programme that goes well beyond the screening room, **food markets, karaoke, community events**, and a dedicated **queer space** rooted in Indonesian culture.

CinemAsia has been part of Amsterdam cultural calendar since 2004. It is **Europe largest pan-Asian film festival**, and it has been growing: 2024 saw a 20% attendance increase, surpassing pre-pandemic peaks. The 2026 edition carries a **theme**, **"Commons"**, that feels less like a curatorial concept and more like **a statement of intent** in a year when the idea of **shared ground** is doing a lot of work.

## Table of Contents

## The Theme — "Commons" — and Why It Lands in 2026

**CinemAsia 2026 edition** is built around the idea of commons: **shared ground, shared histories, shared humanity**. It is a word with both an ecological meaning and a political one, **common spaces** that belong to **everyone**, resources held in common, land that is not owned.

In the context of 2026 geopolitical moment, it lands differently than it might have five years ago. **Border tensions, identity politics, and the fracturing of shared cultural spaces** are not abstract concerns for the communities CinemAsia serves. The festival was founded in 2003 precisely because Amsterdam lacked a cultural platform for Asian stories. Today, with an estimated **350,000+ people of Asian** descent **living in the Netherlands**, including communities with deep historical ties going back to colonial connections with Indonesia, the idea of a commons is not just thematic. It is structural.

CinemAsia has a distinct cross-ethnic character worth noting. It is not organised around a single national cinema. It explicitly connects communities from **China, Hong Kong, Taiwan, Japan, Korea, India, Indonesia, Singapore, Thailand, the Philippines, and Vietnam** under the same roof. A film from **Cambodia** sits beside one from **Malaysia**. A **Philippine** documentary is programmed alongside a **Taiwanese** comedy. **The breadth is the point.** If you are arriving to the Asian film festival Amsterdam scene for the first time, CinemAsia is the place to start.

## Opening Film: Becoming Human

**Polen Ly** debut feature opens the festival with a ghost story that doubles as a meditation on cultural memory. **Set in Battambang, Cambodia**, the film follows **Thida, a spirit who has spent decades guarding a crumbling cinema**. When the building faces demolition and a young journalist arrives to document what remains, Thida must decide whether to move on or stay earthbound.

The film significance extends beyond its plot. [**Becoming Human**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Becoming_Human_(film)) is the **first** **Cambodian production** accepted **into the Venice Biennale College Cinema** development programme, a global fund selecting four projects annually with 200,000 euros in production support. Director **Polen Ly is self-taught**, rooted in social issues and environmental storytelling, and a Prince Claus Fund awardee. The film had its world premiere at Venice before being acquired by Lights On for world rights.

**Polen Ly will be in Amsterdam for the screening**. This is not a director on a brief press junket. This is a first-time feature filmmaker whose film exists partly because of a rare institutional bet on an unknown voice from a country rarely represented in international cinema. **The conversation after the credits will be worth staying for.**

## Closing Film: The Waves Will Carry Us

Where Becoming Human is quiet and haunted, **Lau Kek-Huat** closing film operates in a register closer to dark comedy. Ah Yao is a migration broker living in Taipei. His father dies in Malaysia. He goes back for the funeral and discovers his father secretly converted to Islam, which, in Malaysia multi-ethnic bureaucratic reality, creates a jurisdictional problem: the body falls under Islamic burial law, and retrieving it means navigating a system none of the siblings were prepared for.

**The film received five Golden Horse Award nominations**, including Best Director and Best Narrative Feature. The Golden Horse Awards are Taiwan most prestigious film prizes, and five nominations for a debut narrative feature is significant. **Lau Kek-Huat** previously **won Best Documentary** at the Golden Horse for From Island to Island, and his debut Boluomi screened in the New Currents competition at the Busan International Film Festival in 2019. **The Waves Will Carry Us** had its world premiere at the Hawaii International Film Festival 2025.

**Lau Kek-Huat will be present at the closing screening.** His films deal consistently with **diaspora identity**, **Malaysia colonial and post-colonial** complexity, and the experience of **Chinese Malaysians** navigating **multiple cultural inheritances**. The Q&A will be worth attending as much as the film.

## Beyond Screenings: Food, Karaoke, and Queer Nongkrong

One of the most useful things to understand about CinemAsia is that it is not just a film festival. The community programming runs alongside the screenings and, for many regulars, is equally the point.

### Queer Nongkrong

The name comes from the **Indonesian concept of nongkrong**, **the practice of hanging out**, leisurely and **without agenda**, spending time with people rather than getting anywhere in particular. [**Queer Nongkrong**](https://cinemasia.nl/en/filmfestival/queer-nongkrong-2025/) takes that concept and builds a **dedicated space for LGBTQ+ audiences** within the festival, combining **workshops, hangouts, and screenings** in a format that prioritises presence over programming. It is a genuine community space, not a marketing segment. Specific dates and times for 2026 are to be confirmed, check [cinemasia.nl](https://cinemasia.nl/en/filmfestival/queer-nongkrong-2025/) as the programme develops.

### Food Market

[**The festival food market**](https://cinemasia.nl/en/filmfestival/cinemasia-foodmarket/) is one of the most accessible entry points for people who have not come specifically for the cinema. **Asian food stalls**, the Eye Filmmuseum IJ waterfront setting, and the early-April air create a combination that works on its own terms. You do not need a ticket to a screening to enjoy the market, though the screenings are better if you have already eaten.

### Karaoke Night

[**The karaoke night**](https://cinemasia.nl/en/filmfestival/cinemasia-karaoke/) is exactly what it sounds like, and it is a deliberate programming choice. **Karaoke is a form of cultural participation deeply embedded across multiple Asian cultures**, and its inclusion in the festival programme is a statement about what CinemAsia considers **cultural expression** worth celebrating. Dates for all community events to be confirmed, check [cinemasia.nl](https://cinemasia.nl/en/filmfestival/cinemasia-karaoke/) closer to April.

## The Venues: Four Spaces, One Programme

Part of what makes CinemAsia logistically interesting is that it spreads across **four distinct Amsterdam venues** — each with its own character and audience.

### Eye Filmmuseum — Amsterdam Noord

The Eye is where the **opening and closing nights happen**, and its setting is part of the experience. The building sits on the northern bank of the IJ, a five-minute free ferry ride from Central Station, one of the most enjoyable micro-journeys in Amsterdam. **The architecture is cantilevered and angular**, jutting out over the waterfront. Four screens ranging from 67 to 300 seats. For a first visit to CinemAsia, the Eye is the right choice.

## Get Your Guide

### Studio/K — Amsterdam Oost

[**Studio/K**](https://studio-k.nu/) is a community-rooted **cultural venue** with a **cinema, a cafe, and a history of politically engaged programming**. It is in **Oost**, one of **Amsterdam** **most diverse neighbourhoods**, and its audience reflects that. Screenings here tend toward the adventurous end of the programme. Accessible by tram 14 or metro 51/53/54.

### Rialto De Pijp

[**Rialto**](https://rialtofilm.nl/en/venues/1/rialto-de-pijp) has been showing **CinemAsia films for years**. It is an Amsterdam **arthouse institution,** a neighbourhood cinema in the best sense, with regulars who treat it like a second home. De Pijp is a short walk or tram ride from the centre, and the Rialto programming within CinemAsia tends to draw an audience that knows what it is there for. Accessible by tram 3, 12, or 24.

### Rialto VU — Amsterdam Zuid

The [**VU**](https://rialtofilm.nl/en/rialto-vu) campus venue extends the festival reach into **south Amsterdam** and serves a student and local audience that the central venues might not reach. Accessible by metro 51.

## Mentioned Posts

![Poster written CinemAsia Film Festival ](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/s6i9319f/production/d1f13e2d7417be1e6340a9a719c56ffde8d02d11-600x296.png?w=480&fm=webp&q=75&fit=fill)

### CinemAsia Film Festival 2025: Guide to Asian Cinema Celebration

![Tilda Swinton: A Visionary Actress and Her Upcoming Performance at the Eye Film Museum in Amsterdam](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/s6i9319f/production/03f993c5bcf6b7631657420acc3aacf13fb59f10-1600x900.jpg?w=480&fm=webp&q=75&fit=fill)

### Tilda Swinton: A Visionary Actress and Her Upcoming Performance at the Eye Film Museum in Amsterdam

## How to Plan Your CinemAsia 2026 Visit

**Tickets are 8.50 to 10.50 euros per screening**. Booking platforms and ticket sales opening dates to be announced, check [**cinemasia.nl**](https://cinemasia.nl/en/) for updates.

## FAQ

### What is CinemAsia Film Festival Amsterdam?

CinemAsia is Amsterdam annual pan-Asian film festival, running since 2004. The 18th edition takes place 8-12 April 2026 across four venues: Eye Filmmuseum, Studio/K, Rialto De Pijp, and Rialto VU. It presents 40+ films from Asia and its diaspora and is one of Europe largest festivals dedicated to Asian cinema.

### When is CinemAsia 2026?

CinemAsia 2026 runs from 8 to 12 April 2026 in Amsterdam. The opening night is at Eye Filmmuseum on 8 April; the closing night is also at the Eye on 12 April.

### How much are CinemAsia 2026 tickets?

Tickets are 8.50 to 10.50 euros per screening. Ticket sales open closer to the festival — check cinemasia.nl for exact dates and booking platforms.

### Is CinemAsia only for Asian audiences?

No. CinemAsia is explicitly designed to bridge Asian diaspora communities and Dutch society broadly. The festival welcomes anyone interested in independent film, Asian culture, and diverse storytelling. Community events including the Food Market and Karaoke Night are particularly accessible for first-timers.

### What films are showing at CinemAsia 2026?

The full programme has not yet been announced as of February 2026. Confirmed so far: opening film Becoming Human (Cambodia, director Polen Ly) on 8 April, and closing film The Waves Will Carry Us (Malaysia/Taiwan, director Lau Kek-Huat) on 12 April. Both directors will be attending. The complete 40+ film lineup will be announced on cinemasia.nl.

## Find Your Way In with OUTHERE

[**CinemAsia**](https://staging.outhere.guide/event/cinemasia-film-festival-2026-0b6e) consistency is what makes it worth paying attention to year after year. Not just as a festival for a specific community, but as a gauge of where **Asian cinema** is going, and as one of Amsterdam genuine c**ontributions to European cultural life**. The 2026 edition has two strong anchors in its opening and closing films, a theme that does not feel arbitrary, and a programme structure that makes room for people who do not consider themselves film buffs.

**OUTHERE keeps track of what is happening in Amsterdam cultural calendar** throughout the year. Explore our [**Amsterdam events guide**](https://www.outhere.guide/place/amsterdam) for more of what the city has to offer this spring, or browse our [**Netherlands culture picks**](https://www.outhere.guide/place/netherlands) if you are planning a broader trip. Whatever you choose, we hope to see you out there, possibly at the Eye, watching a Cambodian ghost make a choice.

![Outhere Guide Logo](/_app/immutable/assets/logo-with-text-white.C-tYhbip.png)
![Outhere Guide Logo](/_app/immutable/assets/logo-with-text-black.ClZiMC9h.png)

Arie Biemondstraat 111, Amsterdam

# Special Screening: Fanon

After the sold-out screenings in Amsterdam and Rotterdam, we’re pleased to present another screening of Fanon on Thursday, January 15, 2026, at 18:30 in LAB111, Amsterdam! Don’t miss this powerful biopic about the influential anti-colonial psychiatrist, writer, and activist Frantz Fanon, with a specific focus on his pivotal years in Algeria (1953–1956) and his role in the independence struggle there. The screening will be introduced by Eva Webster.

Arie Biemondstraat 111, Amsterdam

# Special Screening: Fanon

After the sold-out screenings in Amsterdam and Rotterdam, we’re pleased to present another screening of ***Fanon*** on **Thursday, January 15, 2026**, at **18:30** in **LAB111, Amsterdam!** Don’t miss this **powerful biopic** about the influential **anti-colonial psychiatrist, writer, and activist Frantz Fanon**, with a specific focus on his pivotal years in **Algeria (1953–1956)** and his role in the **independence struggle** there. The screening will be introduced by **Eva Webster**.

[𝘚𝘤𝘳𝘰𝘭𝘭 𝘯𝘢𝘢𝘳 𝘣𝘦𝘯𝘦𝘥𝘦𝘯 𝘷𝘰𝘰𝘳 𝘥𝘦 𝘕𝘦𝘥𝘦𝘳𝘭𝘢𝘯𝘥𝘴𝘦 𝘷𝘦𝘳𝘴𝘪𝘦]

𝗦𝗽𝗲𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗦𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗲𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴: 𝗙𝗮𝗻𝗼𝗻  
Date: Thursday, January 15, 2025  
Time: 18:30 hrs (doors open 18:15 hrs)  
Venue: LAB111  
Address: Arie Biemondstraat 111, Amsterdam  
Title: 𝗙𝗮𝗻𝗼𝗻  
Director: Jean-Claude Barny  
Country: Algeria, France, Luxembourg, Canada, Belgium  
Year: 2024  
Length: 132′  
Language: French and Arabic  
Subtitles: English  
Extra: Introduced by 𝗘𝘃𝗮 𝗪𝗲𝗯𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗿  
Extra: Presented by 𝗔𝗳𝗿𝗶𝗰𝗮𝗱𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗰 and 𝗖𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗯𝗯𝗲𝗮𝗻 𝗖𝗿𝗲𝗮𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗶𝘁𝘆  
More info & tickets: [lab111.nl/africadelic-and-caribbean-creativity-present-fanon/](https://www.lab111.nl/africadelic-and-caribbean-creativity-present-fanon/)

**About** ***Fanon***  
***Fanon*** (2024) is a compelling biographical drama directed by Jean-Claude Barny, a French director of Guadeloupean and Trinidadian origin, that focuses on Fanon’s tenure at the Blida psychiatric hospital in Algeria during the 1950s. In 1953, the Martiniquan psychiatrist became the head of the hospital’s psychiatry department, where he treated both French soldiers and Algerian victims of torture and introduced innovative, patient-centered methods of psychiatric care. His progressive approach faced resistance from colleagues and the institution’s director. As the Algerian War of Independence intensified, Fanon’s commitment to decolonization led him to align with the National Liberation Front (FLN), deepening his involvement in the fight against colonial oppression.

The film’s narrative intertwines pivotal moments from Fanon’s earlier life in Martinique and France with contemporary scenes in Algeria, and ends with his untimely death from leukemia in 1961 at the age of 36 and his burial in Algeria, at a cemetery dedicated to fallen FLN fighters. His final book, ***The Wretched of the Earth***, which he dictated from his hospital bed, was published shortly before his death and became a powerful manifesto for anti-colonial struggles around the world. Fanon, who would have become 100 in July 2025, remains a key intellectual figure in the field of postcolonial studies and current decolonization debates.

**About Eva Webster**  
Eva Webster is a 21-year-old student of French and Caribbean heritage, with roots in Martinique, Guadeloupe, and Dominica. She is currently studying Political Science at the University of Amsterdam, where her academic interests are shaped by Caribbean and African history, politics, and questions of inequality. Alongside her studies, she works as a junior programmer for both Caribbean Creativity and Africadelic. Eva’s passion lies in culture in all its forms, from her love of R&B to her dedication to sewing and designing clothing inspired by traditional Caribbean garments. She sees creative expression as a powerful tool to reclaim identity, celebrate heritage, and strengthen community bonds. She values collective spaces where culture, dialogue, and belonging can flourish. Her ambition is to contribute to cultural and political initiatives that elevate and amplify the voices and experiences of minority communities.

**About Africadelic**  
Africadelic is a platform that is committed to the programming and promotion of African and African diasporic cultural creativity, diversity and activism in the Netherlands. The annual Africadelic Festival is an ode to the African continent and its worldwide diaspora, and offers a showcase of the cultural wealth and profound influence Afrodescendants have on global culture and society. Provisionally scheduled from May 20-25, 2026, next year’s festival offers another week filled with concerts, films, and talks. At the same time, they are organizing one-off events throughout the year, including this special screening of ***Fanon***, to kick off 2026. For more information, visit [www.africadelic.com](http://www.africadelic.com/?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAYnJpZBExZlVhaTFGOGRlYnJLeWprVXNydGMGYXBwX2lkEDIyMjAzOTE3ODgyMDA4OTIAAR50tJT5FYtUvnWUf92f-RUcyHnrZwwdBRRiovDj-hMVSMGvBy38QHQNMI-ZlQ_aem_9PxFcTKGRy9P6HlkVMqN0A).

**About Caribbean Creativity**  
Caribbean Creativity is an Amsterdam-based non-profit organization that is, since 2008, committed to the programming and promotion of Caribbean and Caribbean-themed cinema in the Netherlands and beyond. Over the years, Caribbean Creativity has hosted over 300 screenings in Dutch cinemas and at film festivals, including numerous premieres. In 2020, they launched YardVibes, a streaming platform featuring content from independent Caribbean and African filmmakers. YardVibes currently offers over 100 titles, including feature films, web series, documentaries, and short films, with new content added monthly. For more information, visit [www.caribbeancreativity.nl](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.caribbeancreativity.nl%2F%3Ffbclid%3DIwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAYnJpZBExZlVhaTFGOGRlYnJLeWprVXNydGMGYXBwX2lkEDIyMjAzOTE3ODgyMDA4OTIAAR7rwBXuErj7iMnSnEkQ7gvJRWARCbB6D00yzTtxeRByhes7EnkCnDueqYUKsg_aem_ssF8zTZ0qfgt0DvQyYF9KA&h=AT1iHLIZ3iOS-4nE6fdFyCVEiQn0P9ZIBR_zqPZF_sIVNWjUi2rLjs4eZzZOf3uGa5HISLrZ81bAqX3TTn0czuDtRvASMeSM7WE_2OZceUksmPEeFWmzHnPbzufTnyk_oxOtLCbPGEOBHw&__tn__=q&c[0]=AT3EpPyIT8CoPwFO_hi-vXdVrLyBXfDeOktm00X9aQTpnRFuo5UXmcPe-m7Rh1usf_9jT7gS3AlFoO02jtL50KMC4kTdWmrIg46ZJw494ylpWZ_Ad4ve5_MtQ2YujWThEGwWV6KC7smcNpRhEDTzQFJM9U3_isFQb2c).

—

[𝘕𝘦𝘥𝘦𝘳𝘭𝘢𝘯𝘥𝘴]

Na twee uitverkochte vertoningen in Amsterdam en Rotterdam zijn we verheugd om een extra vertoning van ***Fanon*** aan te kunnen kondigen op **donderdag 15 januari 2026** om **18:30 uur** in **LAB111, Amsterdam**! Mis deze krachtige **biopic over de invloedrijke antikoloniale psychiater, schrijver en activist Frantz Fanon** niet, met een specifieke focus op zijn cruciale jaren in **Algerije (1953–1956)** en zijn rol in de **onafhankelijkheidsstrijd** daar. De vertoning wordt ingeleid door **Eva Webster**.

**Speciale Vertoning: Fanon**  
Datum: donderdag 15 januari 2025  
Tijd: 18:30 uur (deuren open om 18:15 uur)  
Locatie: Arie Biemondstraat 111, Amsterdam  
Adres: Otto Reuchlinweg 996, Rotterdam  
Titel: ***Fanon***  
Regie: Jean-Claude Barny  
Land: Algerije, Frankrijk, Luxemburg, Canada, België  
Jaar: 2024  
Duur: 132’  
Taal: Frans en Arabisch  
Ondertiteling: Engels  
Extra: Inleiding door **Eva Webster**  
Extra: Gepresenteerd door **Africadelic** en **Caribbean Creativity**  
More info & tickets: [lab111.nl/africadelic-and-caribbean-creativity-present-fanon/](https://www.lab111.nl/africadelic-and-caribbean-creativity-present-fanon/)

**Over *Fanon***  
***Fanon*** (2024) is een meeslepend biografisch drama geregisseerd door Jean-Claude Barny, een Franse regisseur van Guadeloupeaanse en Trinidadiaanse afkomst, dat zich richt op Fanons periode in het psychiatrisch ziekenhuis van Blida in Algerije tijdens de jaren vijftig. In 1953 werd de Martinicaanse psychiater hoofd van de psychiatrische afdeling, waar hij zowel Franse soldaten als Algerijnse slachtoffers van marteling behandelde en vernieuwende, patiëntgerichte methoden van psychiatrische zorg introduceerde. Zijn progressieve aanpak stuitte op weerstand van collega’s en de directeur van het instituut. Naarmate de Algerijnse Onafhankelijkheidsoorlog heviger werd, leidde Fanons inzet voor dekolonisatie ertoe dat hij zich aansloot bij het Nationaal Bevrijdingsfront (FLN), waardoor zijn betrokkenheid bij de strijd tegen koloniale onderdrukking werd verdiept.

De film verweeft cruciale momenten uit Fanons eerdere leven in Martinique en Frankrijk met hedendaagse scènes in Algerije, en eindigt met zijn vroegtijdige dood aan leukemie in 1961 op 36-jarige leeftijd en zijn begrafenis in Algerije, op een begraafplaats voor gevallen FLN-strijders. Zijn laatste boek, ***The Wretched of the Earth***, dat hij dicteerde vanuit zijn ziekenhuisbed, werd kort voor zijn dood gepubliceerd en groeide uit tot een krachtig manifest voor antikoloniale strijd wereldwijd. Fanon, die dit jaar honderd jaar zou zijn geworden, blijft een sleutelfiguur in het veld van postkoloniale studies en de actuele dekolonisatiedebatten.

**Over Eva Webster**  
Eva Webster is een 21-jarige student met Franse en Caribische roots, afkomstig uit Martinique, Guadeloupe en Dominica. Ze studeert momenteel Politicologie aan de Universiteit van Amsterdam, waar haar academische interesses worden gevormd door Caribische en Afrikaanse geschiedenis, politiek en vraagstukken rond ongelijkheid. Naast haar studie werkt ze als junior programmeur voor zowel Caribbean Creativity als Africadelic. Eva’s passie ligt bij cultuur in al haar vormen: van haar liefde voor R&B tot haar toewijding aan het naaien en ontwerpen van kleding geïnspireerd door traditionele Caribische kledingstukken. Ze ziet creatieve expressie als een krachtig middel om identiteit terug te claimen, erfgoed te vieren en gemeenschappen te versterken. Ze hecht veel waarde aan collectieve ruimtes waar cultuur, dialoog en een gevoel van verbondenheid tot leven komen. Haar ambitie is bij te dragen aan culturele en politieke initiatieven die de stemmen en ervaringen van minderheidsgemeenschappen versterken en zichtbaarder maken.

**Over Africadelic**  
Africadelic is een platform dat zich inzet voor de programmering en promotie van Afrikaanse en Afrikaans-diasporische culturele creativiteit, diversiteit en activisme in Nederland. Het jaarlijkse Africadelic Festival is een ode aan het Afrikaanse continent en zijn wereldwijde diaspora, en biedt een showcase van de culturele rijkdom en de diepe invloed die Afrodescendants hebben op de mondiale cultuur en samenleving. Het festival, volgend jaar gepland van 20-25 mei 2026, biedt een week gevuld met concerten, films en talks. Tegelijkertijd organiseren ze het hele jaar door losse evenementen, waaronder deze speciale vertoning van ***Fanon***, de eerste van 2026. Voor meer informatie, bezoek [www.africadelic.com](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.africadelic.com%2F%3Ffbclid%3DIwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAYnJpZBExZlVhaTFGOGRlYnJLeWprVXNydGMGYXBwX2lkEDIyMjAzOTE3ODgyMDA4OTIAAR6Ekr8WbK4CHCSqq5eS5szL2vZY3BGHJ9k_Z4JWIjZ9OQx8JHZdKizGAOr_VA_aem_rnq6nmp9ry7JweOPN5AsIA&h=AT2avqbFM_kBI--tM7WXjI9xyz0XC2M9xoEJupZGiRpVKfWw_9wpjvCAhR4x10KCBhwfeDeQRmu6FCrLAEHnAw2OAMpEYUfxLFHQQVBim11ScvxFgK_oEaK-tpB3y6QJQaadMyAmZ5mCPQ&__tn__=q&c[0]=AT3EpPyIT8CoPwFO_hi-vXdVrLyBXfDeOktm00X9aQTpnRFuo5UXmcPe-m7Rh1usf_9jT7gS3AlFoO02jtL50KMC4kTdWmrIg46ZJw494ylpWZ_Ad4ve5_MtQ2YujWThEGwWV6KC7smcNpRhEDTzQFJM9U3_isFQb2c).

**Over Caribbean Creativity**  
Caribbean Creativity is een in Amsterdam gevestigde non-profitorganisatie die zich sinds 2008 inzet voor de programmering en promotie van Caribische en Caribisch-georiënteerde cinema in Nederland en daarbuiten. In de loop der jaren heeft Caribbean Creativity meer dan 300 vertoningen georganiseerd in Nederlandse bioscopen en op filmfestivals, waaronder talloze premières. In 2020 lanceerden ze YardVibes, een streamingplatform met content van onafhankelijke Caribische en Afrikaanse filmmakers. YardVibes biedt momenteel meer dan 100 titels, waaronder speelfilms, series, documentaires en korte films, met maandelijks nieuwe toevoegingen. Voor meer informatie, bezoek [www.caribbeancreativity.nl](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.caribbeancreativity.nl%2F%3Ffbclid%3DIwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAYnJpZBExZlVhaTFGOGRlYnJLeWprVXNydGMGYXBwX2lkEDIyMjAzOTE3ODgyMDA4OTIAAR7rwBXuErj7iMnSnEkQ7gvJRWARCbB6D00yzTtxeRByhes7EnkCnDueqYUKsg_aem_ssF8zTZ0qfgt0DvQyYF9KA&h=AT1iHLIZ3iOS-4nE6fdFyCVEiQn0P9ZIBR_zqPZF_sIVNWjUi2rLjs4eZzZOf3uGa5HISLrZ81bAqX3TTn0czuDtRvASMeSM7WE_2OZceUksmPEeFWmzHnPbzufTnyk_oxOtLCbPGEOBHw&__tn__=q&c[0]=AT3EpPyIT8CoPwFO_hi-vXdVrLyBXfDeOktm00X9aQTpnRFuo5UXmcPe-m7Rh1usf_9jT7gS3AlFoO02jtL50KMC4kTdWmrIg46ZJw494ylpWZ_Ad4ve5_MtQ2YujWThEGwWV6KC7smcNpRhEDTzQFJM9U3_isFQb2c).
