# Amsterdam Film Agenda: 14 films to see in the cinema in May

Going to the cinema in Amsterdam? These are the best films (according to the Your Little Black Book team) to see in the cinema this month.

##### YLBB Notes

## Fancy a trip to the cinema in Amsterdam? Read on for the new film schedule for Amsterdam.

A movie afternoon or evening is always a good idea! Especially when there is a fun movie playing. In our new Amsterdam cinema schedule, you can discover the latest films to watch in the cinema in May. It's fun to do with girlfriends or as a date with your loved one. Order a nice big bucket of popcorn to go with it and your movie date can begin. Many films are premiering again this month, from comedies to thrillers and touching movies. Whatever your mood, there is bound to be a film playing to your taste! View the Amsterdam Cinema Schedule for the month of May here! And did you know that you can find last month's films via [Pathé Home](https://www.awin1.com/cread.php?awinmid=17314&awinaffid=373571&campaign=filmagenda+amsterdam) You can also just watch it from the comfort of your own home? Bonus tip :-).

Just back from Catalonia and I thought: I'll share some tips right away.  
  
During our road trip, we stayed two nights in the Ebro Delta. An incredibly fun stop for families and foodies, where there is plenty to do.  
  
• A boat trip to the Musclarium is a must! Here you eat oysters and mussels straight from the farm, in the middle of the sea.  
  
 · Admire the flamingos and black ibises that can be found here all year round.  
  
 · Rice is grown in the Ebro Delta all year round. For paella, but Kensho also makes sake and miso from it! You can visit them during a tasting.  
  
 Cycling along the rice fields, canoeing on the Ebro River, and visiting various beaches. It was still a bit too cold right now, but there is plenty to do.  
  
 • The towns are very low-key and not that exciting, but you can find fantastic local food there. From the Michelin-awarded Les Moles\* to local eateries like Vermuteria Bonmarisc and Lo Tinglado. We ate a lot of fish, but also anemone fritters (a first!).  
  
Now back to work, and I’m going to create an extensive travel guide of this road trip through Catalonia, but you’ll want to save these tips for a future adventure.  
  
✖️✖️✖️  
🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck   
  
{ In this post, I share tips for the Ebro Delta in Spain (Delta de l'Ebre). More tips for Catalonia can be found at www.yourlittleblackbook.me }

Here you eat oysters and mussels straight from the farm, in the middle of the sea. · Admire the flamingos and black ibises that can be found here all year round. · Rice is grown in the Ebro Delta all year round. For paella, but Kensho also makes sake and miso from it! You can visit them during a tasting. · Cycling along the rice fields, canoeing on the Ebro River, and visiting various beaches. It was still a bit too cold now, but there is plenty to do. · The towns are very low-key and not that exciting, but you can find fantastic local food there. From the Michelin-awarded Les Moles* to local eateries like Vermuteria Bonmarisc and Lo Tinglado. We ate a lot of fish, but also anemone fritters (a first!). Now back to work, and I’m going to create an extensive travel guide of this road trip through Catalonia, but you’ll want to save these tips for a future adventure. ✖️✖️✖️ 🖊️ @anne_de_buck { In this post, I share tips for the Ebro Delta in Spain (Delta de l'Ebre). More tips for Catalonia can be found at www.yourlittleblackbook.me }](https://www.yourlittleblackbook.me/wp-content/plugins/instagram-feed-pro/img/placeholder.png)

A cookbook from Grandma. Three Michelin stars. And a beautiful 70s villa in a quiet neighborhood in Bangkok, where you are welcomed as if you were at the chefs' home.  
  
The Sühring brothers do not cook based on trends; they cook from memories. Of their grandmother's farm, of the flavors of their youth in Germany, and of techniques passed down through generations.  
  
@relaischateaux invited me to experience Sühring during a solo lunch, and this is truly my ultimate definition of culinary self-care.  
  
This was a surprising afternoon in Bangkok, where I ate the best German food I have ever tasted. And that in a city where I normally avoid European restaurants. Well, for @restaurant\_suhring, you really want to make that exception.  
  
Signature dishes that I still think back to regularly?  
🍴 Labskaus with caviar  
🍴 Enleta waffle with Pfalz vinegar  
🍴 Game pie  
🍴 Dry aged Duck  
  
TIP: If you go to Sühring, reserve the seats at the kitchen bar so you have a view of the chefs at work!  
  
Follow @yourlbb for more tips you can trust.  
  
✖️✖️✖️  
🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck

This is one of the nicest free outings in the spring! Did you know that you can gather (or pick, for the connoisseurs 😉) oysters yourself in various places in the Netherlands? I’m in Zeeland for a bit this weekend, and if we can, we’ll go again.  
  
📍In this video, I show where you can fill your bucket.   
  
Comment oester for the comprehensive guide with all details about all 8 locations + handy tips and rules.  
  
Important to know:  
 – Max. 10 kilos per person (≈ 1 bucket)  
 – For personal use only  
 – Do not scoop or scrape the soil  
 – Go at low tide  
  
Handy to take with you:  
Bucket, screwdriver, boots, work gloves, tea towel, clean clothes and garbage bags (for the oysters and dirty clothes).  
  
And of course: also bring an oyster knife, lemon and champagne 🍋🥂Because you want to taste something right away!   
  
The oyster season runs from September to about April / early May, so now that the water isn't so cold, this is the perfect outing.   
  
In the full guide, I share exactly where you can easily find them without much effort. Leave a comment and I'll send it to you right away!

## Watching movies with a twist: these are the best movie nights in Amsterdam

### Cinair Europe

Watching movies under the starry sky, but next level. At Cinair Europe, well-known films are screened at unique outdoor locations, such as historic buildings, gardens, or hidden spots you wouldn't normally discover. With a drink in your hand and headphones on (silent cinema style), it feels like stepping into another world for a moment. The exact locations and dates vary by edition. [so keep an eye on the website >>](https://www.cinair.eu/en)

### Movie night at the Supperclub

If you're in the mood for a movie night with just that little bit extra, then you have to be at [Supper](https://supper.nl/movienights/) are. Every Tuesday evening, they transform the restaurant into an intimate cinema with great classics on the screen, while you sink into one of their iconic white lounge beds. Think: cozy atmosphere, tasty bar bites, and drinks within reach. Book early, because these movie nights are popular!   
Order date:  
– April 28 – Focus  
– May 12 – The Tourist

### Cinema Culinary

Some films aren't just meant to be seen, they're meant to be tasted. At Cinema Culinair, you don't just watch films like Chef, Ratatouille, or The Menu; you eat exactly what you see on the screen or what is inspired by it! Served precisely to the second, in the form of a brunch or a 7-course dinner. Think: the scene starts, the dish arrives. Enjoyment with all your senses. Perfect for an original brunch date or a culinary evening out. [View the full Cinema Culinair film schedule here >>](https://cinemaculinair.nl)

![Amsterdam cinema agenda ](https://www.yourlittleblackbook.me/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/bioscoopagenda-amsterdam-cinema-culinair-1.jpg)

Cinema Culinary © Your Little Black Book

## Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026: 5 X New in May

### A Useful Ghost

Sometimes love returns in the most unexpected forms. In A Useful Ghost, March is inconsolable after the loss of his wife, until she suddenly reappears… as a ghost inside a vacuum cleaner. Sounds absurd? It is, but in a charming way. While his family grapples with this bizarre situation, Nat turns out to be surprisingly handy and even helps the family business out of trouble.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Filmhallen, Kriterion, Rialto, FC Hyena, The Movies  
Genre: Drama, Comedy  
On view from: May 7, 2026

### The Sheep Detectives

What if sheep are secretly smarter than you think? In The Sheep Detectives, life on the farm seems quiet, until a mystery turns everything upside down. While shepherd George unsuspectingly reads aloud from detective books, it turns out his sheep have been listening all along. So, it's time for action: they take the investigation into their own hands and dive into an adventure full of clues, suspicious characters, and clever discoveries.   
Showing at: Filmhallen, Pathé Cinemas, Het Ketelhuis, VUE // , Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé Cinemas  
Genre: Family, Comedy  
On view from: May 7, 2026

### In the Gray

A billion dollars, a ruthless adversary, and a mission that goes anything but according to plan. In the Grey follows a secret team of elite agents who are brought out of the shadows to right a massive heist. What begins as a tightly organized operation quickly turns into a dangerous game of deception, strategy, and survival.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, VUE // Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Action, Drama, Thriller  
On view from: May 14, 2026

### Merrily We Roll Along

What happens to friendship when success and ambition get in the way? Merrily We Roll Along follows the bond between composer Franklin and his two best friends, writer Charley and Mary, over a period of thirty years. What once began as a close friendship slowly falls out of balance due to choices, career, and life itself.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, VUE // Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Music, Drama, Thriller  
On view from: May 14, 2026

### Wasteman

A minor offense, but major consequences. Wasteman follows the life of Taylor, who is serving a long prison sentence and tries to survive primarily by remaining inconspicuous. He fills his days with small routines and memories of his son. But when early release suddenly comes within reach, a new, charismatic cellmate puts everything on edge.  
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, The Movies, Kriterion, LAB111, Rialto, Studio/K, FC Hyena   
Genre: Drama  
On view from: May 21, 2026

## Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026: more films to see in May

### The Devil Wears Prada 2

Okay, so this is one I'm really looking forward to. Twenty years after the first part, Miranda Priestly, Andy, and the rest are back in a completely revamped fashion world where everything is faster, more digital, and more ruthless than ever. How they hold their own? You'll want to be there. Tip: watch part 1 again on [Disney +](https://www.disneyplus.com/en-pt/browse/entity-35ee3632-230a-4e4b-8520-46063f9282a3) of [Prime Video](https://www.primevideo.com/detail/The-Devil-Wears-Prada/0TZUGTVNJUZ5PN2X7WHS6I15H5) For the ultimate throwback. One thing is certain: this will be \*the\* film for fashion lovers and fans from the very beginning.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Studio/K, The Movies, Rialto, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Drama, comedy  
On view from: April 30, 2026

### Michael

Michael Jackson may be gone, but his influence is still felt everywhere. In the new film Michael, you dive into the life of the King of Pop: from his breakthrough with the Jackson Five to his legendary solo career. The film showcases not only his greatest hits but also the man behind the music. With impressive performances and iconic moments, you get the feeling as if you have a front-row seat to his story. A must-see for fans of Michael's music and his legacy.   
Showing at: VUE Amsterdam, The Movies, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Biography, drama

## Amsterdam Cinema and Film Agenda 2026: more films to see in May

### The Drama

What should have been a dream week leading up to your wedding changes completely when a big secret from the past comes to light. In The Drama, we follow Charlie and Emma. Their relationship is suddenly put under immense strain as the big day draws ever closer. Starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, you get an intense and uncomfortably honest love story about trust, doubt, and the question: how well do you really know someone?   
Showing at: VUE Amsterdam, The Movies, Studio/K, De Filmhallen, EYE, FC Hyena, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Romance, comedy and drama

### A Family

In A Family, we follow teenagers Eli and Nina, who each try to cope in their own way with their parents' (Carice van Houten and Pieter Embrechts) acrimonious divorce. As they are torn between loyalty, anger, and grief, they both search for stability. A poignant and realistic family drama that shows what happens when love comes under pressure.   
Showing at: VUE Amsterdam, Rialto, Kriterion, Het Ketelhuis, De Filmhallen, EYE, FC Hyena, Pathé cinemas  
Genre: Drama

## Amsterdam Cinema Agenda May 2026: going to the movies with the kids

### The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

Is your mini-me going through a Mario phase too? Then you definitely want to add this one to your list. In The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, Mario trades the Mushroom Kingdom for an adventure in space, full of new worlds, challenges, and, of course, familiar faces. Following the success of the previous film, this sequel promises to be just as fun and action-packed.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Studio/K, Het Ketelhuis, De Filmhallen  
Genre: Adventure, animation

### Tafiti – Across the Desert

A meerkat and an aardvark as best friends? According to Grandpa, that’s simply impossible. But when Tafiti’s grandpa is in mortal danger, he simply \*must\* head into the desert in search of a rare flower. Against his will, he is joined by the cheerful and slightly clumsy Borstel. What follows is an exciting and funny adventure in which they discover that differences sometimes make for the best friendships.   
Showing at: Pathé cinemas, Het Ketelhuis  
Genre: Animation, kids

![Just back from Catalonia and I thought: I'll share some tips right away. During our road trip, we stayed two nights in the Ebro Delta. An incredibly fun stop for families and foodies alike, where there is plenty to do. · A boat trip to the Musclarium is a must! Here you eat oysters and mussels straight from the farm, in the middle of the sea. · Admire the flamingos and black ibises that can be found here all year round. · Rice is grown in the Ebro Delta all year round. For paella, but Kensho also makes sake and miso from it! You can visit them during a tasting. · Cycling along the rice fields, canoeing on the Ebro River, and visiting various beaches. It was still a bit too cold now, but there is plenty to do. · The towns are very low-key and not that exciting, but you can find fantastic local food there. From the Michelin-awarded Les Moles* to local eateries like Vermuteria Bonmarisc and Lo Tinglado. We ate a lot of fish, but also anemone fritters (a first!). Now back to work, and I’m going to create an extensive travel guide of this road trip through Catalonia, but you’ll want to save these tips for a future adventure. ✖️✖️✖️ 🖊️ @anne_de_buck { In this post, I share tips for the Ebro Delta in Spain (Delta de l'Ebre). The Sühring brothers don't cook based on trends; they cook based on memories. Of their grandmother's farm, of the flavors of their youth in Germany, and of techniques passed down through generations. @relaischateaux invited me to experience Sühring during a solo lunch, and this is truly my ultimate definition of culinary self-care. This was a surprising afternoon in Bangkok, where I ate the best German food I have ever tasted. And that in a city where I normally avoid European restaurants. Well, for @restaurant_suhring, you really want to make that exception. Signature dishes that I still think back to regularly? 🍴 Labskaus with caviar 🍴 Enleta waffle with Pfalz vinegar 🍴 Game pâté 🍴 Dry-aged Duck TIP: When you go to Sühring, reserve seats at the kitchen bar so you have a view of the chefs at work! ![This is one of the best free outings in the spring! Did you know that you can gather (or pick, for the connoisseurs 😉) oysters yourself at various locations in the Netherlands? I’m in Zeeland for a bit this weekend, and if we can make it, we’ll go again. 📍In this video, I show you where you can fill your bucket. Comment *oester* for the comprehensive guide with all the details about all 8 locations + handy tips and rules. Important to know: – Max. 10 kilos per person (≈ 1 bucket) – For personal use only – Do not scoop or scrape the seabed – Go at low tide Handy things to bring: Bucket, screwdriver, boots, work gloves, tea towel, clean clothes, and garbage bags (for the oysters *and* dirty clothes). And of course: bring an oyster knife, lemon, and champagne too 🍋🥂 Because you’ll want to taste something right away, won’t you! The oyster season runs from September to about April / early May, so now that the water isn't so cold, this is the perfect outing. In the full guide, I share exactly where you can easily find them, without much effort. Comment oyster and I'll send it to you right away! # AMSTERDAM STUDENT FILM FESTIVAL

29 t/m 31 May 2026

#### Every year, a group of passionate student volunteers organizes and produces the *Amsterdam Student Film Festival* (AmsFF) to provide visibility for aspiring artists and, in the process, foster a community of young people across Amsterdam who are passionate about film.

#### 

Every year, a group of passionate student volunteers organizes and produces the Amsterdam Student Film Festival (AmsFF) to provide visibility for aspiring artists and, in the process, foster a community of young people across Amsterdam who are passionate about film.

AmsFF began in 2022 when six Amsterdam University College students recognized an absence of platforms for student work and decided to create one from the ground up, with virtually no funding or formal structure. Now, three years later, the festival is independent from academic institutions and is composed of an even larger team of volunteers from a variety of academic and professional backgrounds, all excited to create the most ambitious edition of AmsFF yet.

The festival will take place May 29th to 31st across five independent Amsterdam venues: FC Hyena, De Uitkijk, Het Documentaire Paviljoen, LAB111 and Droog. As in previous years, a jury of diverse film industry professionals will work to screen the short films and provide feedback for the filmmakers, and audience members will also be able to interact with the filmmakers at select screenings.

Keep an eye on the website for ticket sales!

*Elk jaar organiseert en produceert een groep gepassioneerde studentvrijwilligers het Amsterdam Student Film Festival (AmsFF) om opkomende kunstenaars zichtbaarheid te bieden en daarbij een gemeenschap te creëren van jonge mensen in heel Amsterdam die een passie voor film delen.*

*AmsFF begon in 2022, toen zes studenten van Amsterdam University College het gebrek aan platforms voor studentenwerk opmerkten en besloten er zelf een op te zetten, vrijwel zonder financiering of formele structuur. Nu, drie jaar later, is het festival onafhankelijk van academische instellingen en bestaat het uit een nog groter team van vrijwilligers met uiteenlopende academische en professionele achtergronden, die allemaal enthousiast zijn om de meest ambitieuze editie van AmsFF tot nu toe te realiseren.*

*Het festival vindt plaats van 29 tot en met 31 mei op vijf onafhankelijke locaties in Amsterdam: FC Hyena, De Uitkijk, Het Documentaire Paviljoen, LAB111 en Droog. Net als in voorgaande jaren zal een jury van diverse filmprofessionals uit de industrie de korte films selecteren en feedback geven aan de filmmakers. Voor bezoekers biedt dit niet alleen de unieke kans om experimentele nieuwe films te bekijken, maar ook om de filmmakers vragen te stellen, bijvoorbeeld tijdens Q&A’s.*

*Houd de website in de gaten voor de ticketverkoop!*

29 t/m 31 May 2026

#### Every year, a group of passionate student volunteers organizes and produces the *Amsterdam Student Film Festival* (AmsFF) to provide visibility for aspiring artists and, in the process, foster a community of young people across Amsterdam who are passionate about film.

####

The festival will take place May 29th to 31st across five independent Amsterdam venues: FC Hyena, De Uitkijk, Het Documentaire Paviljoen, LAB111 and Droog. As in previous years, a jury of diverse film industry professionals will work to screen the short films and provide feedback for the filmmakers, and audience members will also be able to interact with the filmmakers at select screenings.

Keep an eye on the website for ticket sales!

Nu, drie jaar later, is het festival onafhankelijk van academische instellingen en bestaat het uit een nog groter team van vrijwilligers met uiteenlopende academische en professionele achtergronden, die allemaal enthousiast zijn om de meest ambitieuze editie van AmsFF tot nu toe te realiseren.*

*Het festival vindt plaats van 29 tot en met 31 mei op vijf onafhankelijke locaties in Amsterdam: FC Hyena, De Uitkijk, Het Documentaire Paviljoen, LAB111 en Droog. Net als in voorgaande jaren zal een jury van diverse filmprofessionals uit de industrie de korte films selecteren en feedback geven aan de filmmakers. Voor bezoekers biedt dit niet alleen de unieke kans om experimentele nieuwe films te bekijken, maar ook om de filmmakers vragen te stellen, bijvoorbeeld tijdens Q&A’s.*

hits.title: 2026 Amsterdam International Short Film Festival, First Edition Officially Closes to a Full House at Lab111
hits.snippet:
Held in the heart of Amsterdam, the night was met with an engaged and enthusiastic audience, thoughtful post-screening discussions, and a palpable sense that something essential had begun. The full house confirmed a belief at the core of the festival’s mission: short films matter, and audiences are eager to experience them in a focused, cinematic context.

We would like to extend a heartfelt thank you to our special guests — Ieva Jakusa, Annigje Peters, Ernst de Bruijn, Joey Kroon  — whose presence and generosity greatly enriched the evening and contributed to the festival’s atmosphere of exchange, curiosity, and shared passion for cinema.

Why Short Films [...] “as eye-opening as it is unnerving (2000 x 500 px)-3.png-3.png)

# 2026 Amsterdam International Short Film Festival, First Edition Officially Closes to a Full House at Lab111

![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/b3ca3c_2526c38e9445457286904c737f2132cc~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_147,h_221,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,blur_2,enc_avif,quality_auto/b3ca3c_2526c38e9445457286904c737f2132cc~mv2.jpg)

The **first edition of the Amsterdam International Short Film Festival** concluded last night, **February 19**, with a **sold-out screening at Lab111**, marking a powerful and confident debut for a festival entirely dedicated to the art, urgency, and expertise of short filmmaking.

Held in the heart of **Amsterdam**, the night was met with an engaged and enthusiastic audience, thoughtful post-screening discussions, and a palpable sense that something essential had begun. The full house confirmed a belief at the core of the festival’s mission: **short films matter, and audiences are eager to experience them in a focused, cinematic context**.

We would like to extend a heartfelt **thank you to our special guests** — *Ieva Jakusa, Annigje Peters, Ernst de Bruijn, Joey Kroon*  — whose presence and generosity greatly enriched the evening and contributed to the festival’s atmosphere of exchange, curiosity, and shared passion for cinema.

**Why Short Films**

The Amsterdam International Short Film Festival was founded from a clear conviction: **short films are not a stepping stone, but a cinematic form in their own right**.

Short filmmaking demands precision, vision, and mastery. It is often where innovation happens first — formally, politically, emotionally. By launching this festival, we aim to **highlight short-film expertise**, give space to filmmakers who fully embrace the form, and offer audiences a concentrated experience of storytelling at its most distilled and daring.

![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/b3ca3c_47652d97e21b4b9896c15e5766d3f93b~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_250,h_250,fp_0.50_0.50,q_30,blur_30/b3ca3c_47652d97e21b4b9896c15e5766d3f93b~mv2.jpg)
![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/b3ca3c_47652d97e21b4b9896c15e5766d3f93b~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_323,h_323,fp_0.50_0.50,q_90/b3ca3c_47652d97e21b4b9896c15e5766d3f93b~mv2.jpg)
![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/b3ca3c_97cfc38cc6f344a0a45ff5694f9c000e~mv2.jpeg/v1/fill/w_251,h_250,fp_0.50_0.50,q_30,blur_30/b3ca3c_97cfc38cc6f344a0a45ff5694f9c000e~mv2.jpeg)
![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/b3ca3c_97cfc38cc6f344a0a45ff5694f9c000e~mv2.jpeg/v1/fill/w_324,h_323,fp_0.50_0.50,q_90/b3ca3c_97cfc38cc6f344a0a45ff5694f9c000e~mv2.jpeg)
![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/b3ca3c_c4854284bac9495e9a424064fbccee7b~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_250,h_250,fp_0.50_0.50,q_30,blur_30/b3ca3c_c4854284bac9495e9a424064fbccee7b~mv2.jpg)
![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/b3ca3c_c4854284bac9495e9a424064fbccee7b~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_323,h_323,fp_0.50_0.50,q_90/b3ca3c_c4854284bac9495e9a424064fbccee7b~mv2.jpg)

**Award Winners — First Edition**

The jury proudly announced the winners of the inaugural edition:

**Best Film**

**An excess baggage** (France) — *Myriam Garcia Marienstras*

A deeply intimate portrait of loneliness and maternal dependency, following Sophie as her emotional world unravels when her daughter leaves for summer camp.

**Best Director**

**NŪR** (Congo) — *Ryan Youngblood*

A harrowing, unflinching journey into the reality of Islamic State camps in the Democratic Republic of Congo, blending reenactment and testimony to expose a hidden world.

**Best Cinematography**

**Lovers’ Story: fragment(s)** (Italy / Korea, Republic of) — *Marta Irene Giotti*

Three love stories unfold across one summer night in Seoul, visually capturing intimacy, jealousy, and farewell through a lyrical, fragmentary lens.

**Best Drama Film**

**Measurements** (Italy) — *Marta Capossela*

A chilling psychological chamber drama depicting control, abuse, and trauma within the confined space of a shared apartment.

**Best Documentary Film**

**Dressing in Dresses** (Netherlands) — *Ieva Jakusa*

A personal and political exploration of cultural heritage, examining how tradition is reshaped by nationalism and capitalism.

**Best Experimental Film**

**Kiesler's Body** (Austria) — *Ganaël Dumreicher*

An architectural and cinematic séance that resurrects Frederic Kiesler’s radical ideas, turning space itself into a living, breathing screen.

**Best Animated Film**

**Trotter** (Netherlands) — *Annigje Peters & Manouk Moreau*

A poetic metamorphosis tale in which a man wakes up as a horse and slowly finds belonging in a new herd.

**Best Music Video**

**Filicide** (France) — *Christophe Ideal*

A haunting encounter between a disabled dancer and a spectral creature, merging movement and apparition.

**Looking Forward**

With its first edition now officially closed, the Amsterdam International Short Film Festival has established itself as **a vital new platform for short cinema**, rooted in quality, risk-taking, and respect for the form.

We thank the filmmakers, jury members, guests, and — above all — the audience for making this opening edition such a strong and meaningful beginning. This is only the start.

**Short films deserve the spotlight.**

<https://www.amsterdamshortfilmfestival.com>

hits.snippet:
# Imagine Film Festival

Imagine Fantastic Film Festival

Imagine Film Festival returns in November 2026. PLEASE NOTE: The dates and times listed for 2026 are subject to change. Imagine is the largest fantasy film festival in the Netherlands, featuring the best in fantasy, horror, science fiction, and everything in between.

## Imagine Film Festival

### Dates

### Location

## Imagine....

Imagine is all about imagination. According to Imagine, you can imagine anything as crazy, magical, scary, or surprising as you like, because Weird Is Wonderful.

Keep an eye on the website for the program, dates, and showtimes for Imagine in 2026.

## Dates and times

| Date | Time |
 --- |
| Saturday 31 October | Time: 12:00 - 23:00 |

Imagine Film Festival returns in November 2026. PLEASE NOTE: The dates and times listed for 2026 are subject to change. Imagine is the largest fantasy film festival in the Netherlands, featuring the best in fantasy, horror, science fiction, and everything in between.

Imagine is all about imagination. According to Imagine, you can imagine anything as crazy, magical, scary, or surprising as you like, because Weird Is Wonderful.

Keep an eye on the website for the program, dates, and showtimes for Imagine in 2026.

hits.content:
# Filmhuis Amsterdam

Unieke Arthouse Films in Rialto



Rialto is your art house cinema in Amsterdam for exceptional films: from award-winning arthouse titles to non-Western gems, from documentaries to timeless classics on the big screen. At our venues in De Pijp and on the VU campus, our programming team curates an independent selection for curious film lovers. We introduce you to filmmakers past and present, open doors to new perspectives, and create space for reflection through specials, introductions, and Q&As.

## What to expect from our art cinema

At our art cinema, film is at the heart of everything we do — but the experience goes beyond the screening. We offer a carefully curated film programme with premieres, festival favourites and *previously unreleased* titles rarely shown elsewhere. Expect a mix of arthouse films, Dutch cinema, international classics, contemporary documentaries and themed programmes. Our focus lies on quality, context, and discovery: a place to find new voices and rediscover familiar ones.

## Programming: from premieres to specials

Each week we present a diverse selection of films and specials. You’ll find pre-premieres, audience favourites, thematic series and a steady stream of events with guests, Q&As, and talks. We regularly collaborate with festivals and cultural partners, allowing you to not only watch films but also meet the makers and explore current topics. Our theatres are designed for optimal experience: high-quality image and sound, comfortable seating and an intimate atmosphere that fits an art house cinema.

Curious what’s playing today and in the coming weeks? Check the [Amsterdam cinema listings](en/calendar) or browse all current titles via [Films Amsterdam](en/films).

## Locations in Amsterdam

Rialto screens films at two locations in the city: the historic art house cinema Rialto De Pijp and our venue Rialto VU on De Boelelaan. Both locations embody the atmosphere of an art cinema and host specials, events, and educational programmes. More information about directions, theatres, and facilities can be found via [Rialto De Pijp and Rialto VU locations](en/venues).

## For every film lover

Whether you love discovering new indie films, have a passion for the *nouvelle vague*, or like to be surprised by unconventional titles — our art house cinema is for you. We programme broadly and inclusively, highlighting filmmakers from diverse regions and film cultures, and we regularly screen films *with English subtitles*. For our youngest visitors, we also offer a dedicated selection of accessible films and family-friendly events.

Planning a visit with kids? Discover [Children’s films in Amsterdam](en/specials/2/kids-movies-in-rialto). Want to know more about what we offer? Start with [Cinema Amsterdam](en/bioscoop-amsterdam) for an overview of our atmosphere, features and facilities.

## Context, conversation and community

An art cinema is more than just a movie theatre. We organise specials, post-screening talks with filmmakers, thematic programmes around icons such as Akira Kurosawa or today’s trailblazers, and events that highlight emerging voices. Through introductions, side programmes, and encounters with makers, we deepen the viewing experience and connect film lovers with each other. Before and after the film, our foyer and café offer space for conversation.

## Practical information: tickets and visit

Tickets are available online via our agenda and at the box office on site (subject to availability). You’ll find all current times in the [Amsterdam cinema listings](en/calendar). Each film description indicates whether the film is English spoken or has Dutch/English subtitles. Have a question about accessibility, language or side programmes? Get in touch — we’re happy to help.

## Frequently asked questions

### What makes this art house cinema different from a regular movie theatre?

In an art cinema, it’s all about curation and context. We select films for their artistic value and social relevance, offer depth through specials and talks, and create a space where you can see films rarely found in mainstream theatres. The experience is intimate, personal, and meaningful.

### What kind of films does Rialto, the art house cinema of Amsterdam, screen?

We show arthouse films, non-Western cinema, Dutch productions, documentaries, classics, and themed programmes. You’ll find both new releases and reissues, as well as festival highlights — often accompanied by introductions or Q&As.

### Are there film premieres at this art cinema in Amsterdam?

Yes. Every week we programme premieres and pre-premieres, often with added context or special guests. The current overview and ticket sales can be found in the [Amsterdam cinema listings](en/calendar).

## Discover our full programme

Ready to choose your next film? Start with [Films Amsterdam](en/films) for the complete overview, check what’s playing today and tomorrow via the [Amsterdam cinema listings](en/calendar), and plan your visit to [Rialto De Pijp and Rialto VU](en/venues/).

# Tickets & Locations

## TICKET INFO

##### Tickets for Imagine Fantastic Film Festival can be bought on our website from October 7th onwards or physically at our ticket registers during the festival.

[PROGRAMME](/en/programme2025/)[FAQ](/en/faq)[Helpdesk](mailto:helpdesk@imaginefilmfestival.nl)

###### Regular Screening Ticket Prices

* Regular: from € 13,50
* Discount:  € 9,50\*
* Cinevillepas: € 0,00 \*\*

\* Students, CJP, Stadspas with ‘groene stip’. These discounts are only available at the cash register.  
 \*\*Cineville is valid at all locations for all regular screenings.  
 \*\*An additional fee may be added for some special or interactive screenings.  
 \*\*Cineville members are allowed a maximum of 15 open reservations in total at once. See the terms on [their website.](http://cineville.nl) Questions about Cineville reservations/etc can be sent to [klantenservice@cineville.nl.](mailto:klantenservice@cineville.nl)

###### Masterclasses, Workshops, Special Events and Screenings

Prices vary, please check the event pages and the locations for specific pricing. 

* Opening / Closing night – Invite only
* Talks / Masterclasses € 8,50
* VR regular € 9,00\*
* VR special rate € 13,50\*
* Filmconcert € 18,50 / Cineville + € 5,00

\*VR Tickets are only sold online and at the FilmHallen.

###### **Free events**

Some of our specials are free of charge. This is indicated on the event pages. The exhibitions in LAB111 are also free.

# LOCATIONS

Imagine takes place at three main locations. For info on Accessibility at the locations click here: **[Accessibility](https://www.imaginefilmfestival.nl/en/accessibility/)**

* ### [LAB111](#tab_69f15bda82179)

  [Arie Biemondstraat 111, 1054 PD Amsterdam](#tab_69f15bda82179)
* ### [De FilmHallen](#tab_69f15bda82196)

  [Hannie Dankbaarpassage 12, in het complex De Hallen, Amsterdam](#tab_69f15bda82196)
* ### [Hal West (VR)](#tab_69f15bda821a5)

  [Hannie Dankbaarpassage 24, in het complex De Hallen, Amsterdam](#tab_69f15bda821a5)

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg%20xmlns%3D'http%3A%2F%2Fwww.w3.org%2F2000%2Fsvg'%20viewBox%3D'0%200%20768%20512'%2F%3E)

LAB111 is a quirky cult cinema located in a monumental old pathological anatomical laboratory in Amsterdam.

[WEBSITE](https://www.lab111.nl/)

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg%20xmlns%3D'http%3A%2F%2Fwww.w3.org%2F2000%2Fsvg'%20viewBox%3D'0%200%20768%20512'%2F%3E)

The FilmHallen is located in the De Hallen Amsterdam complex, a monumental tram depot in the middle of Amsterdam. You can find the entrances to the Hannie Dankbaarpassage on the Ten Kate market and the Tollensstraat (side street Kinkerstraat).

[WEBSITE](https://filmhallen.nl/)

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg%20xmlns%3D'http%3A%2F%2Fwww.w3.org%2F2000%2Fsvg'%20viewBox%3D'0%200%20768%20576'%2F%3E)

Hal West is our new VR location. You can find the entrance in De Hallen, near the FilmHallen.

[WEBSITE](https://halwest.amsterdam/)

# EXTRA LOCATIONS

In addition to LAB111 on Nov 9th, [The Best of Imagine](https://www.imaginefilmfestival.nl/en/on-tour/) will screen in KINO in Rotterdam (November 15th), LHC in Utrecht (November 15th), LAB-1 in Eindhoven (November 29th) and Forum in Groningen (November 30th).

* ### [KINO Rotterdam](#tab_69f15bda84b61)

  [Gouvernestraat 129 – 133, 3014 PM Rotterdam](#tab_69f15bda84b61)
* ### [Louis Hartlooper Complex](#tab_69f15bda84b80)

  [Tolsteegbrug 1, 3511 ZN Utrecht](#tab_69f15bda84b80)
* ### [LAB-1](#tab_69f15bda84b8f)

  [Keizersgracht 19, 5611 GC Eindhoven](#tab_69f15bda84b8f)
* ### [Forum Groningen](#tab_69f15bda84b9a)

  [Nieuwe Markt 1, 9712 KN Groningen](#tab_69f15bda84b9a)

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg%20xmlns%3D'http%3A%2F%2Fwww.w3.org%2F2000%2Fsvg'%20viewBox%3D'0%200%20768%20455'%2F%3E)

With the arrival of KINO, the historic building on Gouvernestraat is again part of the rich Rotterdam film history.

[WEBSITE](http://kinorotterdam.nl)

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg%20xmlns%3D'http%3A%2F%2Fwww.w3.org%2F2000%2Fsvg'%20viewBox%3D'0%200%20768%20511'%2F%3E)

LHC is a cinema, cultural centre and café in Utrecht, which has become a much-loved meeting point since its opening in 2004.

[WEBSITE](https://hartlooper.nl/)

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg%20xmlns%3D'http%3A%2F%2Fwww.w3.org%2F2000%2Fsvg'%20viewBox%3D'0%200%20768%20512'%2F%3E)

LAB-1 is a stage for films, live music, theater, exhibitions, visuals, DJs, and events, day and night. LAB-1 represents retro charm, coziness, dynamism, and creativity—a place to enjoy, hang out, and feel at home.

[WEBSITE](https://www.lab-1.nl/)

![](data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,%3Csvg%20xmlns%3D'http%3A%2F%2Fwww.w3.org%2F2000%2Fsvg'%20viewBox%3D'0%200%20768%20432'%2F%3E)

Tomorrow’s possibilities in today’s world. This underpins everything we do at the Forum Groningen, which includes a libary, a cinema and more.

[WEBSITE](https://forum.nl/en)

Rialto VU is located in a new building on the Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam campus at De Boelelaan 1111. With four state-of-the-art screening rooms, Rialto VU makes a significant cultural contribution not only to the VU Campus but also to the surrounding areas of Buitenveldert, Zuidas, and Amstelveen.

A wide movie selection

Our programming features a broad selection of films, ranging from arthouse gems to Hollywood blockbusters. We provide a platform for filmmakers from diverse cultures and backgrounds, allowing you to explore new perspectives and stories. [...] Which movies are playing in Amsterdam?

Curious about the movies currently screening in Amsterdam? Our cinema schedule provides a complete overview of all the wonderful and unique titles currently showing at Rialto. Rialto screens films from all over the world, from independent productions to major Hollywood releases. In addition to feature movies, we also show documentaries and short films, keeping our selection diverse and dynamic.

For lovers of movie history, we regularly program classic films while also giving space to contemporary filmmakers who tell innovative stories. At Rialto De Pijp and Rialto VU, you experience movies as they were meant to be: with outstanding picture and sound quality, in an inviting atmosphere where movie enthusiasts come together. [...] Agatha's Almanac

##### Films in Amsterdam

Amsterdam is a city with a vibrant movie culture and a wide range of films. Whether you enjoy arthouse cinema, award-winning productions, documentaries, or feature movies, there is always something to see. At Rialto, you can enjoy a diverse program featuring movies from both the Netherlands and abroad. We offer both renowned classics and exciting new releases.

Which movies are playing in Amsterdam?

Agatha's Almanac](https://rialtofilm.nl/en/films/2726/agathas-almanac)

##### Films in Amsterdam

Amsterdam is a city with a vibrant movie culture and a wide range of films. Whether you enjoy arthouse cinema, award-winning productions, documentaries, or feature movies, there is always something to see. At Rialto, you can enjoy a diverse program featuring movies from both the Netherlands and abroad. We offer both renowned classics and exciting new releases.

**Which movies are playing in Amsterdam?**

Curious about the movies currently screening in Amsterdam? [Our cinema schedule](en/calendar) provides a complete overview of all the wonderful and unique titles currently showing at Rialto. Rialto screens films from all over the world, from independent productions to major Hollywood releases. In addition to feature movies, we also show documentaries and short films, keeping our selection diverse and dynamic.

For lovers of movie history, we regularly program classic films while also giving space to contemporary filmmakers who tell innovative stories. At Rialto De Pijp and Rialto VU, you experience movies as they were meant to be: with outstanding picture and sound quality, in an inviting atmosphere where movie enthusiasts come together.

In addition to our regular screenings, we frequently host special events, such as film festivals, retrospectives, and interviews with filmmakers. This makes Rialto not just a place to watch movies but also a platform for deeper engagement and dialogue. Here, you don’t just discover new movies—you also gain insight into the stories behind them.

[**The cinema for Amsterdam**](en/bioscoop-amsterdam)

At Rialto, there is a movie for everyone, regardless of age or interest. In addition to our regular programming, we have a special selection of films for children and families. Our [kids movies](en/specials/2/kids-films) feature a mix of adventurous animated movies and inspiring stories, ensuring that even young movie lovers can enjoy a true cinema experience.

We also focus on inclusive programming, showcasing movies that highlight diverse perspectives and contribute to a broader understanding of social themes.

[**Our locations in Amsterdam**](en/venues)

Rialto has two locations across Amsterdam: Rialto De Pijp and Rialto VU, each with its own unique atmosphere and character. Our theaters are equipped with modern technology to ensure the best possible viewing experience. The intimate setting and carefully curated programming make a visit to Rialto truly special.

In the vibrant heart of De Pijp, on Ceintuurbaan, you’ll find Rialto De Pijp—a historic cinema with a unique ambiance. Since its founding in 1921, Rialto has grown into a gathering place for movie lovers seeking diverse and boundary-pushing programming.

Rialto VU is located in a new building on the Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam campus at De Boelelaan 1111. With four state-of-the-art screening rooms, Rialto VU makes a significant cultural contribution not only to the VU Campus but also to the surrounding areas of Buitenveldert, Zuidas, and Amstelveen.

**A wide movie selection**

Our programming features a broad selection of films, ranging from arthouse gems to Hollywood blockbusters. We provide a platform for filmmakers from diverse cultures and backgrounds, allowing you to explore new perspectives and stories.

In addition to the latest releases, we also screen classics and specials that aren't shown in other cinemas. You can find all our [special screenings](en/specials) on our website. Whether you love stunning visuals, profound storytelling, or movies that tackle social issues, Rialto has something for everyone.

**More than just movies**

At Rialto, it’s not just about watching movies—it’s about experiencing them. We regularly host events such as introductions, discussions, and Q&A sessions with filmmakers. Through Rialto’s [Docupodium](en/specials/5/docupodium), we spotlight exceptional documentary makers, with interviews conducted by Hanneke Groenteman after their films. Additionally, we collaborate with various film festivals and cultural organizations to make our offerings even more diverse and engaging.

We also provide a platform for local and emerging filmmakers to present their work, such as through our special program, [Rialto for Short](en/specials/7/rialto-for-short). This ensures that Rialto remains not only a place to watch movies but also a meeting place where movie culture is celebrated. A visit to our cinemas is always a unique and inspiring experience.

**Experience movies in Amsterdam**

Amsterdam has a rich movie culture, and Rialto is an essential part of it. Whether you’re a dedicated cinephile or simply looking for a fun night out, you are always welcome at Rialto. [Check our schedule](en/calendar) to see which movies are playing in Amsterdam today and find one that suits you.

# Art film houses

A fun thing to do in Amsterdam is visiting one of Amsterdam’s many art film houses. Cult cinemas offer plenty of weird and interesting films. And if you don’t feel like seeing a film, the attached bar, café, and restaurant are often pretty cool places in Amsterdam to eat, drink or just spend a pleasant evening.

![Eye Film Museum Amsterdam](https://whatsupwithamsterdam.com/cms/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/Eye-Film-Museum-1024x672.jpg)

# Art film houses

A fun thing to do in Amsterdam is visiting one of Amsterdam’s many art film houses. Cult cinemas offer plenty of weird and interesting films. And if you don’t feel like seeing a film, the attached bar, café, and restaurant are often pretty cool places in Amsterdam to eat, drink or just spend a pleasant evening.

## Here are 15 Art House Film Cinemas in Amsterdam

Center of Amsterdam

### 1. Cinecenter

Cinecenter is a small art-house film cinema located at Leidseplein. It is a great little theatre with a small bar. Cinecenter screens “quality film’, which means it may screen Hollywood blockbusters along with independent films and art house films.

## Are Films in Holland subtitled?

Yes, all films in Holland have subtitles in Dutch. English films are always subtitled and never dubbed. If the film is not English-spoken, subtitles will likely be in Dutch. Before you go to a non-English spoken film in Holland, ask the cinema if the film is subtitled in English.

### 2. Melkweg Cinema

De Melkweg (Milky way) at Leidseplein is a music venue with two main stages for live music. The building is an old milk factory that was squatted in the 1970s by a theatre group. It quickly turned into a youth center. Nowadays it’s one of [Amsterdam’s most famous clubs](https://whatsupwithamsterdam.com/clubbing/ "Clubbing"). And there is a cool art house film cinema in the attic of the building.

![Amsterdam art house film cinema Melkweg](https://s3-eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/static.melkweg.nl/uploads/images/scaled/inspiration_view_large/24565)

### 3. De Uitkijk

Certainly, the Uitkijk is the most romantic film theatre in Amsterdam to watch a film. Located near Leidseplein, but hardly noticeable from the outside. Inside, a lovely 1920s avant-garde décor puts you right in the mood.

### 4. De Balie

De Balie is a cultural venue located at Leidseplein. There are 3 halls to fill with debates, seminars, and theatre. One is a cinema. The program of De Balie aims to ‘open up debate on social, political and cultural matters’.

![Amsterdam Cinema with bar De Balie](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Fdebalie.nl%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2018%2F08%2FBalie_Warmer-28-1024x683.jpg&f=1&nofb=1)

Amsterdam North

### 5. EYE Film Museum

The Amsterdam Eye Film Institute screens classic films, documentaries, shorts, fiction, Dutch films and independent films. The Dutch film institute EYE has an impressive film archive, dating back to silent movies. EYE Film Museum dedicates itself to restoring and digitalizing films and to build up an audience for Dutch films abroad.

![EYE Film Museum](https://whatsupwithamsterdam.com/cms/wp-content/uploads/2012/07/filmmuseum2-1024x768.jpg)

### 6. FC Hyena, for films and wine

It’s on the other side of the IJ-water in [Amsterdam Noord](https://whatsupwithamsterdam.com/amsterdam-noord/) and not easy to reach, but it’s a great little find: FC Hyena. At FC Hyena they like quality in their wine, their food and their films. It has an easy-going vibe. Films are mainstream, classics as well as independent films.

![](https://fchyena.nl/media/films/_w600/53636678_644881985945408_8499510979070001152_o.jpg)

Amsterdam West

### 7. Amsterdam Art House Film Theatre The Movies

The Movies at Haarlemmerdijk is Amsterdam’s oldest movie theatre. The nostalgic cinema has a lovely Art Deco interior. The Movies screens new films from directors like Lars von Trier, Pedro Almodovar, Woody Allen, and Wim Wenders.

### 8. De FilmHallen

Opened in 2014, [the Amsterdam Halls](https://whatsupwithamsterdam.com/the-amsterdam-halls/) were an immediate success. The buildings at the Bellamyplein in Amsterdam West (near Kinkerstraat) date back to 1902 -1928. Part of them were as renovated and turned into a cinema. Every day there are films screened in 9 different film rooms. From Hollywood to European film, foreign films from around the world and documentaries.

![art house film cinema amsterdam](https://whatsupwithamsterdam.com/cms/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/IMG_3589-1024x683.jpg)

### 9. LAB111

The cult cinema LAB111 is located in the Oud-West neighborhood. The building is near the complex of buildings that once served as hospitals, the Wilhelmina Gasthuis (WG-terrein). LAB111 was a pathological anatomy laboratory  Pathology Laboratory.

![Lab111 restaurant Strangelove](https://scontent-ams4-1.xx.fbcdn.net/v/t1.0-9/82995014_2438322146271772_2968082118208389120_o.jpg?_nc_cat=111&_nc_ohc=pGUN7_esWz8AX-_4NB1&_nc_ht=scontent-ams4-1.xx&oh=dbcb0e219a0b8db9d5c24ddd62ea85bc&oe=5EFD13D0 )

### 10 Het Ketelhuis

Het Ketelhuis (‘kettle house’) started out as a cinema for Dutch children films but has turned into a full art-house cinema. Focus is still on Dutch films though (no subtitling in English). Het Ketelhuis is located at [Westergasfabriek](https://whatsupwithamsterdam.com/westergasfabriek/ "Westergasfabriek").

![Ketelhuis cult cinema in Amsterdam](https://whatsupwithamsterdam.com/cms/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/IMG_7121-1024x768.jpg)

### 11. Filmhuis Cavia

[Filmhuis Cavia](http://www.filmhuiscavia.nl) is a low budget art film house cinema in Amsterdam. It is not-for-profit and run by volunteers. Their history lies in the [squatter’s movement](https://whatsupwithamsterdam.com/amsterdam-squats/) of Amsterdam. Movies are €5, but don’t expect too much comfort.

### 12. OT301’s Cinema of the Dam’d

Amsterdam former squatters hub OT301 once was the Dutch Film Academy. It is fitting then that the building should use its 76-seat cinema on the top floor. The non-profit foundation Cinema of the Dam’d curates the film program. On screen are classics as well as independent/activist documentaries and fictional works ranging from cult to queer to classics.

East

### 13. Rialto

The Amsterdam film theatre Rialto is in [De Pijp](https://whatsupwithamsterdam.com/de-pijp/), near Sarphatipark. Rialto has been screening films since 1921. Rialto has a preference for European and independent art-house films. Occasionally, special guests introduce the film or there’s an interview or afterparty. There are 3 rooms and a bar with wifi.

### 14. Kriterion

Kriterion is a student-run movie house and bar, located across the University of Amsterdam at Roetersstraat. Popular, not just among students.

### 15. Club and cinema Studio/K

Cinema, restaurant and club Studio/K is located in the East of Amsterdam (Timorplein). Studio/K is run entirely by students (by the same student organization as Kriterion).

I hadn’t visited A’Dam for quite a few years and was totally gobsmacked by seeing the EYE from the Schipol train into Centraal in 2013. ## Studio/K

Studio/K cinema terrace at Timorplein

Run by a team of enthusiastic students, this forward-looking centre functions as an arthouse cinema, a music venue, a gallery and a restaurant. The two screening rooms lay on an outstanding range of quality films focusing on cinema that has emerged from outside Hollywood including countries such as Turkey, Morocco and India. You’ll find an equally impressive array of eclectic club nights, festivals, markets and other open-air activities which extend outdoors to the spacious terrace.

Studio/K | Timonplein 62, Oost

## LAB111

Camera Japan location at LAB111 exterior [...] ## Cinecenter Amsterdam

Cinecenter

Located opposite Melkweg in the heart of the city, Amsterdam's Cinecenter is the place to go for the latest independent and art house films. Inside you’ll find stylish, futuristic interiors and four intimate screening rooms. Cinecenter specialises in art house films with a specific focus on French and Spanish speaking regions. The modern bar is the perfect place to relax with a glass of wine before and after the film.

Cinecenter Amsterdam, Lijnbaansgracht 236, Centrum

## Pathé de Munt

Pathé de Munt cinema foyer interior [...] ## Filmtheater De Fabriek

Filmtheater De Fabriek boasts three modern screening rooms, a spacious café and a fantastic riverside terrace in Zaandam, just outside of Amsterdam. The cinema is located in a historic 1920s building that once used to be a boys’ school and still has some distinctive architectural details like the beautifully tiled floors. Every month, De Fabriek also offers introductions, talks and panel discussions to provide more context to the films in their programming.

Filmtheater De Fabriek | Jan Sijbrandsteeg 12, Zaandam

## Pathé City

# Specialty film houses and cinemas in Amsterdam

## Pathé Tuschinski

![Pathé Tuschinski cinema grand room interior](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F2ykwyv8j25jz-pathe-tuschinski-5.webp&w=3840&q=75)

A stone’s throw from Rembrandtplein, the Pathé Tuschinski Cinema was voted the most stunning movie theatre in the world. Commissioned by Abraham Icek Tuschinski in 1921, the interior and exterior blend several designs, with a heavy emphasis on Art Deco. Like the décor, the movies are also a mix of styles; Hollywood blockbusters play next to art house darlings. Be sure to arrive early enough to gaze admiringly at the ornate ceiling. Or, up the date-night ante and book private old-school love seats complete with a snack and movie-inspired cocktails from the swanky bar.

[Pathé Tuschinski](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/pathe-tuschinski) | Reguliersbreestraat 26-34, Centrum

## Eye Filmmuseum

![Eye Filmmuseum - film museum and cinema.](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F5k1ur23kcwjd-eye-filmmuseum.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Eye Filmmuseum’s striking architecture quickly transformed it into one of Amsterdam’s modern icons. Situated on the northern bank of the IJ, just opposite Central Station, this cinema and film museum, café/restaurant and cutting-edge event location is a must-see. Eye has four comfortable screening rooms where visitors can enjoy the latest art house fare and the finest restored classics along with retrospectives, lectures, Q&As, performances and live music. In April, the museum also hosts the [Imagine Film Festival](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/see-and-do/whats-on/festivals/overview-film-festivals/imagine-film-festival), where cinema lovers can immerse themselves in all of the new cinematic realms that the movie genre has to offer.

[Eye Filmmuseum](/en/whats-on/calendar/museums-and-galleries/museums/eye-filmmuseum) | IJpromenade 1, Noord

## De Filmhallen

![De Filmhallen interior De Hallen Cinema](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F4gfz6jp25wi8-de-filmhallen-cinema-5.webp&w=3840&q=75)

All housed in a converted tram depot that’s had something of a face-lift, De Hallen comprises a great range of artisanal shops, a hip industrial-chic hotel, a colossal indoor food court and De Filmhallen, the largest independent cinema in the Netherlands. With its nine screens and varied programming, film enthusiasts will always find something new to watch from the latest releases to festival programming and quality world cinema. Don’t miss the Parisien Auditorium, outfitted in authentic art deco interiors.

[De Filmhallen](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/de-filmhallen) | Bellamyplein 47, Oud-West

## Studio/K

![Studio/K cinema terrace at Timorplein](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2Fymgrjbp8d9o-studio-k-timorplein-cecile-obertop-redactioneel.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Run by a team of enthusiastic students, this forward-looking centre functions as an arthouse cinema, a music venue, a gallery and a restaurant. The two screening rooms lay on an outstanding range of quality films focusing on cinema that has emerged from outside Hollywood including countries such as Turkey, Morocco and India. You’ll find an equally impressive array of eclectic club nights, festivals, markets and other open-air activities which extend outdoors to the spacious terrace.

[Studio/K](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/studio-k) | Timonplein 62, Oost

## LAB111

![Camera Japan location at LAB111 exterior](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F2h1wqco142cy-vita-i-amsterdam-beeld-3.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Housed in one of the city’s most unusual converted spaces: a former pathological anatomy lab, this cinema and cultural space is about as quirky as it gets. On the agenda, you’ll see a range of special screenings, animated features, modern classics and in-depth documentaries alongside regular showings with English subtitles. The space also hosts contemporary art exhibitions, lectures and concerts, whilst the media café, studios and workshop rooms are an excellent meeting ground for the city’s creatives.

[LAB111](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/lab111) | Arie Biemondstraat 111, Oud-West

## Cinema De Vlugt

![](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F5w8d3ao6bo8v-cinema-de-vlugt.webp&w=3840&q=75)

At [Cinema De Vlugt](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/cinema-de-vlugt), worlds come together. Located in the Plein '40-'45 neighbourhood of Nieuw-West, you'll find an offering of the latest films; from blockbusters to quirky international cinema. It's housed in the Vlugt cultural house but is a standout venue on its own; perfect for a beer whether you're watching a film later or not.

[Cinema De Vlugt](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/cinema-de-vlugt) | Burgemeester de Vlugtlaan 125, Nieuw-West

## FC Hyena

![FC Hyena cinema cafe restaurant in Noord](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F3faayholombo-fc-hyena-overview-2.webp&w=3840&q=75)

This boutique cinema and eatery has transformed Amsterdam Noord into the perfect hip hangout for film lovers. A great mix of art-house and popular films are programmed across two screens, each with its homemade comfy seating. At the restaurant, you can enjoy light bites such as tacos, oysters and pastries from the wood oven, along with natural wines from the bar.

[FC Hyena](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/fc-hyena) | Aambeeldstraat 24, Noord

## Kriterion

![Bench Movie Theater Kriterion cinema cafe interior](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F6uiosku3wvcz-bench-movie-theater-kriterion-marie-charlotte-peze-rechtenvrij.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Established in 1945, this dynamic student-run movie theatre emerged from the resistance movement of WWII when its founders became involved in hiding children from persecution. Today, the cinema is still a student association and meeting place; the café attracts an eclectic crowd of film buffs discussing movies and social causes over beers and peanuts. Along with screenings of progressive films and the more interesting Hollywood flicks, you can catch sneak previews, exhibitions, talks and festival events - so don’t forget to dust off your debating hat.

[Kriterion](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/kriterion) | Roeterstraat 170, De Plantage

## Rialto

![Rialto cinema front of building at night](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F3z7kcsp7ipx5-rialto-front-building-fotograaf-bas-losekoot.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Dominating the skyline of De Pijp with its spectacular art deco façade, the Rialto has been a much-loved neighbourhood movie theatre since the 1920s. The cinema spotlights an eclectic mix of foreign-language titles, documentaries and art-house movies and inside, you’ll find three screens as well as a stylish bar. Watch international movies with English subtitles on Mondays.

[Rialto](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/rialto-de-pijp) | Ceintuurbaan 338, De Pijp

## Ketelhuis Cinema

![Het Kethuis cinema interior in Westerpark](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F5vs1dnz0z4b3-het-ketelhuis-inside-overview-arjen-veldt.webp&w=3840&q=75)

What started from humble beginnings as a pop-up cinema in the Westergas cultural complex - a converted gasworks in the centre of Westerpark - Ketelhuis Cinema has grown into an important landmark for Dutch cinema, international arthouse and documentary film. The driving force behind the venue is a dedicated team of volunteers who keep an eye on the smooth projection of the films and keep the food and drinks flowing from behind the well-stocked bar. The venue also hosts annual festivals including [Cinekid](https://www.cinekid.nl/en), Roze Filmdagen and [Humans of Film](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/see-and-do/whats-on/festivals/overview-film-festivals/humans-of-film-festival).

[Ketelhuis Cinema](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/ketelhuis-cinema) | Pazzanistraat 4, Westerpark

## Pathé Arena

![Pathe Arena Boulevard IMAX cinema](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F6qk4f029qo2w-arena-boulevard-imax-klapfilmnl-rechtenvrij.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Sandwiched between AFAS Live, Amsterdam ArenA, Ziggo Dome and the growing selection of entertainment options in Amsterdam Zuidoost, Pathé Arena is one of the most modern film theatres in the city. The multiplex boasts 14 screens (including an IMAX screen), 602 cushy chairs and state-of-the-art facilities for your viewing pleasure.

[Pathé Arena](https://www.pathe.nl/bioscoop/arena) | Arena boulevard 600, Zuidoost

## De Uitkijk

![Movietheather De Uitkijk](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F5b1kmfd4qdoq-dcdeuitkijkcc-bysa-20-erwin-verbruggen.webp&w=3840&q=75)

The oldest art-house film theatre in continuous use in the Netherlands, De Uitkijk was founded by a group of cinephiles in 1929 on the Prinsengracht. Since reopening in 2007, it’s now run by students, becoming part of the Kriterion family. Enjoy a healthy mixture of arthouse and independent films and special screenings of French classics. You can also go all-in with their Champagne Package, which includes two romantic seats on the balcony, two bottles of prosecco and a choice of chocolates or salty snacks.

[De Uitkijk](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/de-uitkijk) | Prinsengracht 452, Centrum

## Filmhuis Cavia

![Filmhuis Cavia](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F740oyw5pv1d1-zienema2.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Filmhuis Cavia is an underground not-for-profit cinema showing cult flicks, alternative movies and the best of world cinema. The venue was founded in the 1980s by a squatter’s movement and is the oldest underground art house theatre in Amsterdam, paying special attention to LGBTQ+ and activist causes. It’s a cosy set-up inside, with only 40 seats and a DIY mentality that’s maintained by volunteers. Entry for screenings is also only 5 euros!

[Filmhuis Cavia](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/filmhuis-cavia) | Van Hallstraat 52, Westerpark

## Filmtheater De Fabriek

Filmtheater De Fabriek boasts three modern screening rooms, a spacious café and a fantastic riverside terrace in Zaandam, just outside of Amsterdam. The cinema is located in a historic 1920s building that once used to be a boys’ school and still has some distinctive architectural details like the beautifully tiled floors. Every month, De Fabriek also offers introductions, talks and panel discussions to provide more context to the films in their programming.

[Filmtheater De Fabriek](https://www.de-fabriek.nl/) | Jan Sijbrandsteeg 12, Zaandam

## Pathé City

![Pathé City cinema interior](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F3pff2px2lpr0-pathe-city-4.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Nestled amongst the bars and shopping areas, this central cinema offers a cosy place to put your feet up (not literally) away from the bustling Leidseplein. On the menu, you’ll find a great mix of mainstream films and the occasional art-house flick. The halls themselves are over 85 years old, but the theatre has recently been given a complete make-over with luxurious armchairs complete with a handy table for eating and drinking.

[Pathé City](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/pathe-city) | Kleine-Gartmanplantsoen 15-19, Centrum

## Cinecenter Amsterdam

![Cinecenter](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F5go1lj5nysjn-cine-center-2-studiont-redactioneel.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Located opposite Melkweg in the heart of the city, Amsterdam's Cinecenter is the place to go for the latest independent and art house films. Inside you’ll find stylish, futuristic interiors and four intimate screening rooms. Cinecenter specialises in art house films with a specific focus on French and Spanish speaking regions. The modern bar is the perfect place to relax with a glass of wine before and after the film.

[Cinecenter Amsterdam](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/cinecenter-amsterdam), Lijnbaansgracht 236, Centrum

## Pathé de Munt

![Pathé de Munt cinema foyer interior](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F54gtqi98n34t-pathe-de-munt-2.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Amsterdam’s Pathé de Munt is the bigger, more modern cinema around the corner from its sister theatre, Pathé Tuschinski. This large multiplex screens a huge range of popular movies, with films shown with original soundtracks and subtitles unless specifically stated. Keep a particular eye on children’s films agenda and look for 'OV' designating the original version, otherwise, you’ll sit through dubbed versions of the latest Pixar film.

[Pathé de Munt](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/pathe-de-munt) | Vijzelstraat 15, Centrum

## De Balie

![De Balie exterior logo](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F5bydd3n9avx0-de-balie-1.webp&w=3840&q=75)

This cultural centre in a former courthouse close to Leidseplein has a daily programme of debates, talks and theatre productions focused on contemporary arts and social and political issues. The in-house cinema hosts a changing programme of the best international films and documentaries, from new releases to classic movies - so you’ll be sure to leave with a new critical perspective.

[De Balie](/en/whats-on/calendar/theatre-and-stage/stage/de-balie) | Kleine-Gartmanplantsoen 10, Centrum

## De Filmkoepel

![Filmkoepel cinema foyer in Haarlem](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmedia.iamsterdam.com%2Fw_907%2Ch_514%2F74p17xa0nzfq-filmkoepel-1.webp&w=3840&q=75)

This newly-opened cinema in Haarlem is housed in a national monument that was once, of all things, a prison. Constructed at the end of the 19th century, the design of this stunning building was based upon panopticon principles and features a mesmerising free-standing domed ceiling. Nowadays, De Filmkoepel - with a total of 600 seats spread across six halls - is the place to catch beautiful arthouse films, the best of Hollywood and everything in between. There are also regular film festivals and premieres with special guests.

[De Filmkoepel](https://filmkoepel.nl/?faf=today) | Harmenjansweg 4, Haarlem

![iAmsterdam logo](/images/iamsterdam-logo-white.svg)

# Amsterdam Special Cinemas and Film Houses

Posted by [Dana Marin](https://amsterdamian.com/author/dana/ "Posts by Dana Marin") | Feb 24, 2025 | [Amsterdam](https://amsterdamian.com/category/see/amsterdam/), [Lifestyle](https://amsterdamian.com/category/see/shopping-and-lifestyle/) | [0](https://amsterdamian.com/see/amsterdam/amsterdam-special-cinemas-and-film-houses/#comments)

![Amsterdam Special Cinemas and Film Houses](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Photo-by-Liam-McGarry-on-Unsplash.jpg)

For film lovers, it’s a treat to have the chance to explore the world of Amsterdam’s specialty cinemas and film houses. This city has a theatre for every type of cinephile, from those looking for something beyond the blockbuster to those after a more offbeat experience. Especially on a rainy day, when you feel like hiding from the grey sky for a little while, you can choose to see a movie at one of these amazing places. And if you visit the cinema more often than just when you have to hide from the rain, you can consider getting yourself a [Cineville card](https://www.cineville.nl/en/card) and enjoy limitless movies around the city every month.

[![Pathe Tuschinski Foyer](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/pathetuschinski-foyerbeneden1c-948020-1024x683.jpg)](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/pathetuschinski-foyerbeneden1c-948020-scaled.jpg)

Pathe Tuschinski Foyer. Photo by Pathe.

*This page includes affiliate links. If you click and purchase, I may receive a small commission at no extra costs to you.*

## A guide to some of the most charming and unusual cinemas in Amsterdam that you’ll love discovering.

### EYE Filmmuseum

This iconic building by the IJ River is a museum and also a cinema with a selection of world movies, documentaries, and experimental films. As a national film museum, [the Eye](https://www.eyefilm.nl/en) manages more than 55.000 films from all genres. It organises various [exhibitions and events](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/what-to-do-in-amsterdam-a-guide-to-main-events-throughout-the-year/) throughout the year, and it’s internationally acclaimed. It can be a full day experience with visiting the exhibition, watching a movie and stopping by the beautiful café.

↪ [Get tickets for Eye Filmmuseum](https://www.tiqets.com/en/amsterdam-attractions-c75061/tickets-for-exhibition-guido-van-der-werve-tangible-futility-p1027523/)

### Rialto in De Pijp

Located in the [De Pijp neighbourhood](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/a-day-in-de-pijp-neighbourhood-amsterdam-tips-by-a-local/), [Rialto](https://rialtofilm.nl/) is a cozy, independent cinema known for screening indie films, documentaries, and foreign films you won’t find anywhere else. It’s the perfect spot if you love to experience world cinema in an intimate, friendly setting. Plus, the café is ideal for a post-film discussion. If you don’t speak Dutch, then make sure to check the list of English-spoken films or the foreign films with English subtitle.

### The Movies

Amsterdam’s oldest cinema, dating back to 1912, [The Movies](https://themovies.nl/) brings a classic, old-school vibe with its art deco interior and red velvet seats. Quite the setting for a special cinema experience! They offer a wide range of films, from indie and arthouse to bigger international releases. After the show, you can stop by the restaurant for a dinner.

[![The Movies Cinema](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Marion-Golsteijn-via-Wikimedia-Commons-1024x768.jpg)](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Marion-Golsteijn-via-Wikimedia-Commons.jpg)

The Movies. Photo by Marion Golsteijn, via Wikimedia Commons

### Lab 111

Located in an old pathological anatomical laboratory, this [quirky](https://amsterdamian.com/see/amsterdam/lost-in-books-kok-antiquariaat-in-amsterdam/) venue offers a mix of classics, arthouse films, and retrospectives. The building’s fascinating history adds an extra layer of atmosphere (just don’t think too much about it) making each screening feel like a special event. They also organise various events like special screenings and small festivals (don’t forget to check the language requirements). [LAB111](https://www.lab111.nl/) features a cozy bar and restaurant as well, perfect for a casual drink or meal before or after the movie.

### Kriterion

Founded in 1945 for students, to help them find a job to pay for their studies, [Kriterion](https://www.kriterion.nl/) is still run by students today. This is not just a cinema but also a cultural hotspot with a long and turbulent history that began during the Second World War with the resistance group around Wouter van Zeytveld and Piet Meerburg. Before transforming this place into a cinema, the group was part of the Resistance and saved Jewish children by hiding them at locations around the city. Today, you’ll find here an eclectic mix of art house, classic, and experimental films in an unpretentious and relaxed setting. It’s perfect for those who like a casual, community-driven cinema experience.

### De Uitkijk

This charming little cinema is one of the oldest in Amsterdam and offers a cozy, vintage experience. With just one screen, [De Uitkijk](https://uitkijk.nl/) focuses on showing classic, independent, and auteur films. Since 2007, De Uitkijk has been a sister project of the Kriterion foundation and is run by students.

### Cinecenter

[Cinecenter](https://cinecenter.nl/), an intimate and cozy arthouse movie theatre in the center of Amsterdam (near Leidseplein), is ideal for watching independent and European films that are often hard to find elsewhere. Many of these come with English subtitles, but you’ll have to check in advance.

### FC Hyena

[FC Hyena](https://fchyena.nl/) in the north of Amsterdam offers a fresh take on the cinema experience. This is a small boutique cinema that focuses on art house films and documentaries but also offers a dining experience with locally-sourced meals. The casual, living room-style setup makes you feel right at home.

### FilmHallen

Located in De Hallen complex (a former tram depot) in [Amsterdam West](https://amsterdamian.com/see/amsterdam/the-wild-wild-west-of-amsterdam/), [FilmHallen](https://filmhallen.nl/) is a stylish cinema with a range of films from Hollywood blockbusters to indie and European releases. The interior, with its industrial-chic design, gives it a unique vibe, and you can enjoy the surrounding food court before or after your movie.

[![FilmHallen](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/FilmHallen-1024x683.jpg)](https://amsterdamian.com/filmhallen/)

Photo courtesy of FilmHallen

### Pathé Tuschinski

Although [part of the big Pathé chain](https://www.pathe.nl/nl/bioscopen/koninklijk-theater-tuschinski) and not a quirky cinema, [Tuschinski](https://amsterdamian.com/see/amsterdam/royal-theater-tuschinski-amsterdams-most-beautiful-cinema/) is a one-of-a-kind experience. It’s often regarded as one of the most beautiful cinemas in the world, with its stunning Art Deco and Jugendstil interior. They show a variety of films, including major international releases, but it’s the atmosphere that will leave you speechless. If you don’t believe me, just enter the main hallway and look around, and then you’ll understand. Every detail of this building was carefully designed and executed and you’ll feel as if you’re there for some opera or theatre show, not your regular Hollywood movie.

[![Pathe Tuschinschi](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/pathetuschinski-zaal2b-417308-1024x683.jpg)](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/pathetuschinski-zaal2b-417308.jpg)

Photo: Pathe Tuschinschi

### Studio/K

Another place run by a group of students, [Studio/K](https://studio-k.nu/specials/) is a combination of a cinema, music and events venue, gallery and restaurant. The films are a mix of mainstream and international. Also a location for the Sprouts Festival.

### Filmhuis Cavia

Founded in 1983 by a squatter’s movement, [Filmhuis Cavia](https://www.filmhuiscavia.nl/) is the smallest cinema in Amsterdam (only 40 seats), a non-profit and the oldest underground film house in the city. They pay a special attention to LGBTQ+ and activist causes.

### Ketelhuis

In the beautiful [Westerpark](https://amsterdamian.com/see/photos/cherry-blossom-in-the-time-of-pandemic/), Westergas cultural complex is home for a couple of entertainment venues. Among these, the [Ketelhuis Cinema](https://www.ketelhuis.nl/), an international arthouse and documentary film run by a team of volunteers, stands out. You can have a stop at the bar and then watch a movie from the selection of small, artistic and more commercial feature films, documentaries and short films. They also host annual festivals including Cinekid , Roze Filmdagen and Humans of Film.

[![Het Ketelhuis](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/2048px-Westerpark_amsterdam_Klonneplein_2-1024x768.jpeg)](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/2048px-Westerpark_amsterdam_Klonneplein_2.jpeg)

Het Ketelhuis. Photo Wiki Commons

### Bonus – Pluk de Nacht (Outdoor cinema)

This one’s a bit different – [Pluk de Nacht](https://plukdenacht.nl/) is an annual outdoor film festival that takes place during summer. Screenings are held by the IJ River, offering the special experience of enjoying films under the stars. Bring a blanket, grab a drink from the bar, and enjoy a relaxed night of indie and classic films by the water.

[![Photo Pluck de Nacht by lionangelphotography](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Photo-Pluck-de-Nacht-by-lionangelphotography.jpg)](https://amsterdamian.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Photo-Pluck-de-Nacht-by-lionangelphotography.jpg)

Photo courtesy of Pluck de Nacht. By lionangelphotography

With such a great selection of specialty cinemas, maybe next time you want to catch a movie you’ll choose to skip the usual multiplex and explore one of these hidden gems in Amsterdam!

---

Stay tuned for more and follow Amsterdamian on [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/theamsterdamian/) and [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/amsterdamian.page) for more stories about life in the Netherlands. Please share this post if you liked it!

**Check out my photo book: [Amsterdam Through the Seasons](https://www.blurb.com/b/10882891-amsterdam-through-the-seasons)!**

Love what you’re reading? [Support my work with a small donation](https://ko-fi.com/amsterdamian).

[Subscribe to the newsletter](https://amsterdamian.com/newsletter/)!
