# The Movies

After a closure of two and a half years, The Movies, the oldest cinema in Amsterdam, has reopened. After the reopening, The Movies will continue its line in terms of programming: a mix of the finest new arthouse films, the best of Hollywood, documentaries and classics.

## The Movies

### Location

### Contact

## Arthouse

The Movies is the oldest cinema in Amsterdam (although De Uitkijk also has a shot at that title) and a household name for Amsterdammers. The film theater is located on the Haarlemmerdijk and has four cinemas, all decorated in a classic Art Deco style. The Movies mainly shows art house films, documentaries and the better Hollywood productions.

After a closure of two and a half years, The Movies, the oldest cinema in Amsterdam, has reopened. After the reopening, The Movies will continue its line in terms of programming: a mix of the finest new arthouse films, the best of Hollywood, documentaries and classics.

## The Movies

### Location

### Contact

## Arthouse

The Movies is the oldest cinema in Amsterdam (although De Uitkijk also has a shot at that title) and a household name for Amsterdammers. The film theater is located on the Haarlemmerdijk and has four cinemas, all decorated in a classic Art Deco style. The Movies mainly shows art house films, documentaries and the better Hollywood productions.

## **History**

The Movies was founded in 1912 under the name Bioscoop Tavenu. Two years later the name was changed to Cinema Hollandia and the current name was chosen in the 1970s.

## Exhibition, Films, Talks & Events Eye(s) Open

### New Perspectives on Colonial Film Heritage

3 April — 6 September 2026

Eleven artists respond to Eye’s collection of colonial-era films from Indonesia and Suriname. The artists have created ten new works based on these films.

[Learn more](https://www.eyefilm.nl/en/programme/eyes-open/1583300)

## Films, Talks & Events 2001

### A Time Capsule

18 April — 14 June 2026

Eye marks the 25th anniversary of the year 2001 with a generous helping of nostalgia, screening 25 films that were released in 2001.

[Learn more](https://www.eyefilm.nl/en/programme/2001-a-time-capsule/1600279)

## Public lecture series This is Film! 2026

### Film Heritage in Practice

4 March — 13 May 2026

Annual public lecture series devoted to notable projects in the fields of film restoration and film heritage, with international guest speakers and film screenings.

[Learn more](https://www.eyefilm.nl/en/programme/this-is-film-2026/1584859)

## Ongoing Eye Classics

### Do you know your classics?

Eye now brings film history even closer with Eye Classics. On the big screen, screened in 35mm whenever possible.

[Learn more](https://www.eyefilm.nl/en/programme/eye-classics/63587)

## Permanent exhibition

### Discover the world of moving image, from the past to the present

On the lower level and throughout Eye we illuminate various aspects of the world of the moving image. Learn about how film developed, from its early beginnings to the present day.

[Learn more](https://www.eyefilm.nl/en/permanent-exhibition)

  New Perspectives on Colonial Film Heritage

hits.snippet:
We take pride in our astute selection process. The Amsterdam Independent Film Festival is known for discovering hidden gems that later go on to successfully thrive at A-list festivals, with some films even catching the eye of the Academy Awards. In 2021 for example, we selected 'Ala Kachuu – Take and Run', directed by Maria Brendle, which was nominated in 2022 for an Oscar in the Best Live Action Short Film category. [...] Connecting Cultures Program: Each year, The Bigger Screen selects ten countries and grants filmmakers from those areas the ability to submit their work free of charge to partner festivals all over the world. This opens the door of film exposure to those who may not otherwise be able to enter. As we support inclusivity and shareable films, this program exemplifies our mission.

Local Filmmakers Program: We understand that the cost of making a film can be taxing, so to encourage rising talent, we waive the festival submission fee to local talent. With numerous worldwide locations for festivals, this provides great opportunity for filmmakers around the world to enter the international film scene. This opportunity opens the world of film. Think global, act local, right? [...] At the Amsterdam Independent Film Festival, we focus on film. The festival will also bring independent artistic visions to an audience that demands a more fulfilling experience than the latest Hollywood fare.  
   
 In Amsterdam’s LAB111 — a cult cinema located in a former anatomical pathology hospital — we’ll be screening selections of remarkable independent films from all over the world free of charge, giving film-makers the widest possible exposure and freedom from commercial constraints.  
   
 No red carpets. No elitist audiences. No distraction from the art itself. The Amsterdam Independent Film Festival will provide an intimate environment for film-makers and film lovers to come together and share in the beauty of artistic expression.

##### Awards

## [AMsterdam INDEPENDENT FILM FESTIVAL](https://www.amsterdamfilmfestival.org)



[![6_edited.png](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/b3ca3c_9a8dde82ac284ef08e3544a7528cd053~mv2.png/v1/fill/w_78,h_82,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/6_edited.png)](https://www.finalcutmagazine.com)

##### 15-18 OCT 2025 \\ LAB 111 \\ AMSTERDAM \\ FREE ENTRY



## THE FESTIVAL

##### AT THE AMSTERDAM INDEPENDENT FILM FESTIVAL, WE FOCUS ON FILM. THE FIFTH EDITION WILL BRING INDEPENDENT ARTISTIC VISIONS TO AN AUDIENCE THAT DEMANDS A MORE FULFILLING EXPERIENCE THAN THE LATEST HOLLYWOOD FARE.

The glitz and glamour of showbusiness in the mainstream film festival circuit can sometimes detract from the true purpose of the event — the celebration of creative film-making. While the red carpets, the champagne parties, and the high admission prices may be good for grabbing headlines and driving profits, they may also prevent the dissemination of art to the audience.

We take pride in our astute selection process. The Amsterdam Independent Film Festival is known for discovering hidden gems that later go on to successfully thrive at A-list festivals, with some films even catching the eye of the Academy Awards. In 2021 for example, we selected 'Ala Kachuu – Take and Run', directed by Maria Brendle, which was nominated in 2022 for an Oscar in the Best Live Action Short Film category.

At the Amsterdam Independent Film Festival, we focus on film. The festival will also bring independent artistic visions to an audience that demands a more fulfilling experience than the latest Hollywood fare.  
   
 In Amsterdam’s LAB111 — a cult cinema located in a former anatomical pathology hospital — we’ll be screening selections of remarkable independent films from all over the world free of charge, giving film-makers the widest possible exposure and freedom from commercial constraints.  
   
 No red carpets. No elitist audiences. No distraction from the art itself. The Amsterdam Independent Film Festival will provide an intimate environment for film-makers and film lovers to come together and share in the beauty of artistic expression.

##### Awards

The selected films will compete for the following awards:

Best Narrative Feature Film   
 Best Documentary Feature Film   
 Best Animated Feature Film   
 Best Narrative Short Film   
 Best Documentary Short Film   
 Best Animated Short Film   
 Best Experimental Film

Best Music Video

##### Previous editions

Find out more about the previous editions of the Amsterdam Independent Film Festival:

[2025](https://www.amsterdamfilmfestival.org/2025)

[2021](https://www.amsterdamfilmfestival.org/2021)

[2024](https://www.amsterdamfilmfestival.org/2024)

[2023](https://www.amsterdamfilmfestival.org/2023)

[2022](https://www.amsterdamfilmfestival.org/2022)

[2020](https://www.amsterdamfilmfestival.org/2020)

[2019](https://www.amsterdamfilmfestival.org/2019)

[2018](https://www.amsterdamfilmfestival.org/2018)

##### PrOUD PARTNER OF THE BIGGER SCREEN

Amsterdam Independent Film Festival is supported by [The Bigger Screen](https://www.thebiggerscreen.org), an organization that aims to make film as an art form more accessible and more inclusive. 

​

Supporting Film Festivals: Film festivals are not only a place to share films, but they are a safe haven for film makers to connect, share, and learn. The Bigger Screens supports and engages with over 25 respected international film festivals from mainstays like the 61st Ann Arbor Film Festival and the Brussels Independent Film Festival to emerging events such as the Venice Film Week and The Producer’s Night Los Angeles. These events bring filmmakers and filmgoers together, providing that place to connect and engage in a comfortable setting, away from Hollywood glitz and glamour.

[Connecting Cultures Program](https://www.thebiggerscreen.org/connecting-cultures-program): Each year, The Bigger Screen selects ten countries and grants filmmakers from those areas the ability to submit their work free of charge to partner festivals all over the world. This opens the door of film exposure to those who may not otherwise be able to enter. As we support inclusivity and shareable films, this program exemplifies our mission.

[Local Filmmakers Program](https://www.thebiggerscreen.org/local-filmmakers-program): We understand that the cost of making a film can be taxing, so to encourage rising talent, we waive the festival submission fee to local talent. With numerous worldwide locations for festivals, this provides great opportunity for filmmakers around the world to enter the international film scene. This opportunity opens the world of film. Think global, act local, right?

[Inclusive Audience Program](https://www.thebiggerscreen.org/inclusive-audience-program): Our screenings are always low or even free of charge to provide opportunity for interested film goers to view up and coming, challenging, and inspiring pieces of art, encouraging students, seniors, and those in underprivileged environments to share the experience of film and cinema. We welcome film goers, regardless of income and financial status.

[The Tarkovski Grant](https://www.thetarkovskigrant.org): All selected filmmakers will receive The Tarkovski Grant, a film festival submission fee waiver package with an average value of about $250. This not only saves you a lot of money, but it also recognizes your work as something to watch for during the selection procedure, making a selection with another partner film festival more likely - but of course, never guaranteed. The Tarkovski Grant supports hundreds of filmmakers each year in their challenging journeys as independent filmmakers, by promoting existing films, no matter the genre, style or length.

More about us: Shortcutz Amsterdam is the only initiative in The Netherlands exclusively focussing on promoting new Dutch film talent, and connecting up-and-coming filmmakers with established professionals.

We promote new Dutch cinema through our network of over 30 partners – including Eye Film Museum, Pathé cinemas, festivals around the world, and several special affiliated events.

Through our weekly sessions at De Kring, we showcase the work of new Dutch film talent, and invite film professionals to share their stories and insight. Filmmakers get the chance to promote their work, talk to their audience, and interact with professionals.

Film-lovers have the opportunity to watch films, learn about new talent, and get to know their favourite Dutch filmmakers. [...] All films screened at Shortcutz Amsterdam are watched by a JURY TEAM made up of some of the most respected names of the industry and are eligible to compete at the Shortcutz Amsterdam Annual Awards.

And it’s all done while sipping a nice cold beer (or a glass of wine, whatever you prefer – we don’t judge). Check out our events:

### Discover Dutch Film Talent (Free & in English) [...] ## Over ons

Do you want to meet the people behind the scenes or dive into a film project? Join us every Tuesday at the artistic society ‘de Kring’ directly on Leidseplein on the 1st floor above the restaurant palladium, from 8pm-11pm. Every week you get to watch two short films by up-and-coming directors and meet an established Dutch director, actor or producer. At the end of each screening the filmmakers engage in a discussion/Q&A with the audience. All the movies are subtitled in English and the Q&As are in English too in order to connect the city’s international community with Dutch cinema. Hope to see you there! The suggested donation is €2.-

## Over ons

Do you want to meet the people behind the scenes or dive into a film project? Join us every Tuesday at the artistic society ‘de Kring’ directly on Leidseplein on the 1st floor above the restaurant palladium, from 8pm-11pm. Every week you get to watch two short films by up-and-coming directors and meet an established Dutch director, actor or producer. At the end of each screening the filmmakers engage in a discussion/Q&A with the audience. All the movies are subtitled in English and the Q&As are in English too in order to connect the city’s international community with Dutch cinema. Hope to see you there! The suggested donation is €2.-

More about us: Shortcutz Amsterdam is the only initiative in The Netherlands exclusively focussing on promoting new Dutch film talent, and connecting up-and-coming filmmakers with established professionals.

We promote new Dutch cinema through our network of over 30 partners – including Eye Film Museum, Pathé cinemas, festivals around the world, and several special affiliated events.

Through our weekly sessions at De Kring, we showcase the work of new Dutch film talent, and invite film professionals to share their stories and insight. Filmmakers get the chance to promote their work, talk to their audience, and interact with professionals.

Film-lovers have the opportunity to watch films, learn about new talent, and get to know their favourite Dutch filmmakers.

All films screened at Shortcutz Amsterdam are watched by a [JURY TEAM](http://www.shortcutzamsterdam.com/jury) made up of some of the most respected names of the industry and are eligible to compete at the [Shortcutz Amsterdam Annual Awards](http://www.shortcutzamsterdam.com/competition).

And it’s all done while sipping a nice cold beer (or a glass of wine, whatever you prefer – we don’t judge). Check out our events: <http://www.shortcutzamsterdam.com/events-2018/>

hits.title: Arthouse Cinema Amsterdam | Independent films at Rialto
hits.snippet:
# Arthouse Amsterdam

# Arthouse cinema in Amsterdam – films at Rialto

## Your destination for arthouse cinema in Amsterdam

Looking for arthouse cinema in Amsterdam? At Cinema Rialto you’ll discover a carefully curated programme of distinctive feature films, documentaries and non-Western cinema. Rialto has long been a key player in Amsterdam’s arthouse scene, focusing on films that challenge, inspire and broaden perspectives.

With two locations – Rialto De Pijp and Rialto VU – Rialto offers a diverse and in-depth arthouse programme for curious film lovers.

## What is arthouse cinema? [...] ## What is arthouse cinema?

Arthouse films are characterised by artistic vision, strong directorial voices and thematic depth. Rather than mainstream commercial storytelling, these films focus on form, narrative experimentation and social relevance. At Rialto, arthouse cinema includes a strong emphasis on non-Western cinema.

## A carefully curated arthouse programme

Rialto’s programmers select films that align with the cinema’s mission: presenting stories that encourage reflection and understanding. The programme includes:

 International arthouse features
 Non-Western cinema and emerging filmmakers
 Artistically driven documentaries
 Films engaging with social and political themes
 Festival premieres and award-winning titles

## Arthouse films with context [...] ## Arthouse films with context

Many screenings at Rialto include introductions, Q&As or discussions with filmmakers and partners, turning a cinema visit into a shared cultural experience.

## Discover current arthouse films

Curious which arthouse films are currently playing at Rialto? Check the full agenda for up-to-date listings and showtimes.

View the full agenda

View the calendar

# Arthouse Amsterdam



# Arthouse cinema in Amsterdam – films at Rialto

## Your destination for arthouse cinema in Amsterdam

Looking for **arthouse cinema in Amsterdam**? At **Cinema Rialto** you’ll discover a carefully curated programme of distinctive feature films, documentaries and **non-Western cinema**. Rialto has long been a key player in Amsterdam’s arthouse scene, focusing on films that challenge, inspire and broaden perspectives.

With two locations – **Rialto De Pijp** and **Rialto VU** – Rialto offers a diverse and in-depth arthouse programme for curious film lovers.

## What is arthouse cinema?

Arthouse films are characterised by artistic vision, strong directorial voices and thematic depth. Rather than mainstream commercial storytelling, these films focus on form, narrative experimentation and social relevance. At Rialto, arthouse cinema includes a strong emphasis on **non-Western cinema**.

## A carefully curated arthouse programme

Rialto’s programmers select films that align with the cinema’s mission: presenting stories that encourage reflection and understanding. The programme includes:

* International arthouse features
* Non-Western cinema and emerging filmmakers
* Artistically driven documentaries
* Films engaging with social and political themes
* Festival premieres and award-winning titles

hits.content:
# Film Calendar Amsterdam

##### 

# Film Calendar Amsterdam – Rialto’s current film programme

## Discover what’s playing in Rialto’s cinema schedule

Looking for an up-to-date **cinema schedule in Amsterdam** with special films, premieres, and one-off screenings? In the cinema schedule of **Cinema Rialto**, you’ll find a carefully curated overview of all films and events currently showing (and coming up soon). From international arthouse films and documentaries to **non-Western cinema**, family screenings, specials, and classics: at Rialto you’ll discover cinema that broadens your perspective.

Rialto has two locations in Amsterdam: **Rialto De Pijp** on Ceintuurbaan and **Rialto VU** on the Vrije Universiteit campus. In our schedule, you can easily see what’s on per day and per location, including start times and practical details.

## A cinema schedule for lovers of remarkable cinema

Rialto’s schedule is made for anyone who wants more than the standard cinema line-up. We programme films by makers from all over the world, with a strong focus on **non-Western cinema**, new perspectives, urgent themes, and cinematic quality. Expect award-winning festival titles, premieres fresh from Cannes, Berlin, or Venice, and documentaries that add depth and context.

Our schedule is updated weekly, so you’ll always have a current overview of:

This way, you’ll know exactly what not to miss.

## More than films: specials and events

Alongside regular screenings, Rialto also hosts a wide range of **specials and events**: themed programmes, collaborations with cultural partners, film-and-talk evenings, and events connected to social or cultural topics.

Examples include:

These specials add context and turn a visit to Rialto into more than “just a film”.

## Per location: De Pijp and VU

In the schedule, you can clearly see which films are playing at which location. **Rialto De Pijp** is known for its intimate atmosphere and rich arthouse programme, with plenty of room for **non-Western cinema**. **Rialto VU** is a lively meeting place for students, locals, and film lovers on campus.

For each screening you’ll find:

So you can easily choose what fits your plans best.

## Who is Rialto’s cinema schedule for?

This schedule is for:

Whether you’re picking a film spontaneously or planning ahead, the schedule helps you find the right screening fast.

## Always current and easy to use

Rialto’s cinema schedule is designed to be clear and easy to use on desktop and mobile. Browse by day, and click through to the full agenda with all showtimes and ticket options.

Want to make sure you don’t miss anything? Checking the schedule regularly is the best way to stay on top of our current programme.

## View the full agenda and book your tickets

Curious what’s on today, tomorrow, or later this week? View the full agenda and book your tickets right away.

##

###### Information

###### Friends of Rialto

### Opening & Closing

World Cinema Amsterdam is an initiative of and organised by theatre Rialto, who offers screenings all year round. The premiere programme of Rialto focusses on European and non-western independent films.

###### Contact

Rialto | World Cinema Amsterdam  
Ceintuurbaan 338  
1072 GN Amsterdam  
worldcinemaamsterdam@rialtofilm.nl

###### Information [...] Virginio and Silvia, an elderly Quechua couple, experience the far-reaching effects of climate change on the Andean plateau.

Police detective Oh Yoo-jin gets bogged down in a shocking murder case involving high school student Sohee.

The life of Sarya, a Kurdish refugee in Japan, is turned upside down when her father's residence permit is revoked.

Chile, 1976: 49-year-old Carmen is asked to take care of the mysterious Elias, during the right-wing dictatorship of General Pinochet.

Go Cuba! proudly presents three shorts this year: Los coleccionistas, Ultima canción para Mayaan and Tundra.

In this programme, four short films by Evani Gilds are shown in three blocks.

In this programme, all five episodes of Loverboy Amsterdam can be seen, divided over three blocks.

Het Filmgesprek challenges filmgoers to explore their perception of films with conversations about films and life questions.

Rialto's own youth editorial LA RIOT presents a fine fringe program including Q&A with director July Jung.

hits.snippet:
## Venue:

# Atrabilious Experimental Film Festival 2018

For the fourth time in a row the Amsterdam Experimental Film Fest Atrabilious will take place in Filmhuis Cavia, Amsterdam. Atrabilious has in a short time managed to establish itself as a vanguard of new as well as established experimental filmmakers: a showcase of experimental film-video art of over 16 international artists. Over a period of two days more than 70 short films will be shown, including unique, never shown before material specially curated by Ronald Zapruder.

## Dates:

## Venue:

# The films of Martha Colburn at Filmhuis Cavia (AMIA-UvA)

'Acrophobic Babies' by Martha Colburn [...] Wheeler Winston Dixon was part of the New York underground experimental film scene in the 1960s, and has been making films steadily ever since. His complete film work is archived in the permanent collection of The Museum of Modern Art. On July 6th, at Filmhuis Cavia, Amsterdam, Dixon will present, live, in person, his videos American Dream, Look, Prison State, The Catastrophe Series, Real & Unreal and previously unscreened new video works.

## Dates:

## Venue:

# Gwendolyn Audrey Foster: Queer Feminist Punk Experimental Films

Gwendolyn Audrey Foster Experimental Films Filmhuis Cavia, 6 July 2018

Filmhuis Cavia (in Amsterdam) presents:  
"Gwendolyn Foster: Queer Feminist Punk Experimental Films."

## Dates:

## Venue:

# Atrabilious Experimental Film Festival 2018 [...] experimental film screening Amsterdam

An evening of Surrealism, animation, political videoart, and handmade experimental short films (often incorporating archival materials) made in response to the shock and trauma of the 21st Century. Highlighting punk, no budget, eco/feminist, lgbtq+, post-structuralist, hand-painted, hand-processed, etched and scratched films, agit-prop, personal films; détournements, and 3D animation; from Dadaism to one-of–a-kind dreamy Surrealist cine-poems. Screening and Q & A at 19:30.

## Dates:

## Venue:

# Wheeler Winston Dixon: An American Dream and Other Films

Experimental Films of Wheeler Winston Dixon - Screening at Filmhuis Cavia, July 6th
hits.content:
# [Experimental Cinema](/site/en "Home page")

## News and resources on experimental films

# Filmhuis Cavia

![](https://expcinema.org/site/sites/default/files/styles/large/public/logo-cavia.gif?itok=GzJPNnux)

Filmhuis Cavia is a counterculture cinema in Amsterdam, founded in 1983 by a squatters movement, which programs films you aren't likely to see anywhere else. It is run entirely by volunteers and our programmers work hard to find you brilliant movies and documentaries, particularly by female directors, from outside of the West or 35mm. Our cozy cinema has just 40 chairs, but our bar is fun and our prices are friendly so don't be shy about checking us out!

## Events in this venue

# [Artists' Moving Image: Eric Leiser](/site/en/events/artists-moving-image-eric-leiser)

![](https://expcinema.org/site/sites/default/files/styles/frontpage/public/neph2005-.jpg?itok=LGN2sbAr)

On Friday 8 and Saturday 9 October, Cavia will be all about the work of award-winning artist, experimental filmmaker, animator and holographer **Eric Leiser**. There will be two different screenings with lots of short films and a feature film on Saturday. He will also be present for a Q&A afterwards**.** In our foyer you'll be able to admire RELICS: an exhibition of analog reflection hologram sculptures, dioramas with projection, drawings and woodblock prints by Eric Leiser.

## Dates:

## Venue:

# [Artists' Moving Image: Eric Leiser](/site/en/events/artists-moving-image-eric-leiser)

![](https://expcinema.org/site/sites/default/files/styles/frontpage/public/neph2005-.jpg?itok=LGN2sbAr)

On Friday 8 and Saturday 9 October, Cavia will be all about the work of award-winning artist, experimental filmmaker, animator and holographer **Eric Leiser**. There will be two different screenings with lots of short films and a feature film on Saturday. He will also be present for a Q&A afterwards**.** In our foyer you'll be able to admire RELICS: an exhibition of analog reflection hologram sculptures, dioramas with projection, drawings and woodblock prints by Eric Leiser.

## Dates:

## Venue:

# [Atrabilious Experimental Filmfest #5](/site/en/events/atrabilious-experimental-filmfest-5)

![](https://expcinema.org/site/sites/default/files/styles/frontpage/public/atrabilious5-poster.jpeg?itok=VVmZLFfp)

**Atrabilious Experimental Filmfest** (5th ed.) will take place again at Filmhuis Cavia, Amsterdam!

Atrabilious has in a short time, managed to establish as an vanguard of new and established experimental filmmakers and video artists. Atrabilious is a showcase festival with more than 21 artist and 70 films over an duration of two days. For the 5th edition, the focus will be ‘living in time in turmoil’, the program is curated by artist Ronald Bijleveld.

## Dates:

## Venue:

# [Crawling through the Wreckage: Avant Garde Film & Video Artists Respond to the Trauma of the 21st Century](/site/en/events/crawling-through-wreckage-avant-garde-film-video-artists-respond-trauma-21st-century)

![experimental film screening Amsterdam](https://expcinema.org/site/sites/default/files/styles/frontpage/public/Filmhuis%20Cavia%20Poster_6.jpg?itok=ZB3nqyUB "Filmhuis Cavia\" Crawling Through the Wreckage")

An evening of Surrealism, animation, political videoart, and handmade experimental short films (often incorporating archival materials) made in response to the shock and trauma of the 21st Century. Highlighting punk, no budget, eco/feminist, lgbtq+, post-structuralist, hand-painted, hand-processed, etched and scratched films, agit-prop, personal films; détournements, and 3D animation; from Dadaism to one-of–a-kind dreamy Surrealist cine-poems. Screening and Q & A at 19:30.

## Dates:

## Venue:

# [Wheeler Winston Dixon: An American Dream and Other Films](/site/en/events/wheeler-winston-dixon-american-dream-and-other-films)

![Experimental Films of Wheeler Winston Dixon - Screening at Filmhuis Cavia, July 6th](https://expcinema.org/site/sites/default/files/styles/frontpage/public/Dixon%20Cavia%20Poster.jpg?itok=6M2Pq_Fx "Wheeler Winston Dixon, Experimental Filmmaker at Filmhuis Cavia, Amsterdam July 6th, 2018")

**Wheeler Winston Dixon** was part of the New York underground experimental film scene in the 1960s, and has been making films steadily ever since. His complete film work is archived in the permanent collection of The Museum of Modern Art. On July 6th, at Filmhuis Cavia, Amsterdam, Dixon will present, live, in person, his videos *American Dream*, *Look, Prison State*, *The Catastrophe Series*, *Real & Unreal* and previously unscreened new video works*.*

## Dates:

## Venue:

# [Gwendolyn Audrey Foster: Queer Feminist Punk Experimental Films](/site/en/events/gwendolyn-audrey-foster-queer-feminist-punk-experimental-films)

![Gwendolyn Audrey Foster Experimental Films Filmhuis Cavia, 6 July 2018](https://expcinema.org/site/sites/default/files/styles/frontpage/public/Foster%20Cavia%20Poster_0.jpg?itok=DE4pBk0z "Gwendolyn Audrey Foster: Queer Punk Feminist Experimental Films at Filmhuis Cavia, July 6th, 2018")

**Filmhuis Cavia** **(in Amsterdam) presents:**  
"**Gwendolyn Foster: Queer Feminist Punk Experimental Films**."

## Dates:

## Venue:

# [Atrabilious Experimental Film Festival 2018](/site/en/events/atrabilious-experimental-film-festival-2018)

![](https://expcinema.org/site/en/system/files/styles/frontpage/private/webform/files/poster%20atrabilious%204%20copy-2_1.png?itok=GPcOLhsV)

For the fourth time in a row the **Amsterdam Experimental Film Fest Atrabilious** will take place in Filmhuis Cavia, Amsterdam. Atrabilious has in a short time managed to establish itself as a vanguard of new as well as established experimental filmmakers: a showcase of experimental film-video art of over 16 international artists. Over a period of two days more than 70 short films will be shown, including unique, never shown before material specially curated by Ronald Zapruder.

## Dates:

## Venue:

# [The films of Martha Colburn at Filmhuis Cavia (AMIA-UvA)](/site/en/events/films-martha-colburn-filmhuis-cavia-amia-uva)

!['Acrophobic Babies' by Martha Colburn](https://expcinema.org/site/sites/default/files/styles/frontpage/public/50.1%20acrophobic%20babies_1.jpg?itok=Pfw4NgBI "'Acrophobic Babies' by Martha Colburn")

Filmhuis Cavia, in partnership with the Association of Moving Image Archivists Student Chapter at the University of Amsterdam (AMIA-UvA), presents the films of **Martha Colburn** in a free public exhibition plus a Q&A with the artist. The screening will show Colburn's past work on 35mm, 16mm and video, featuring phantasmagoric animation, music videos, and found footage experiments never before shown in the Netherlands.

## Dates:

## Venue:

# [The Screening Room: Hans Richter](/site/en/events/screening-room-hans-richter)

![](https://expcinema.org/site/sites/default/files/styles/frontpage/public/HansRichter-Filmstudie.jpg?itok=7BEKC8Xk)

The Screening Room Cavia presents the legendary **Hans Richter**! The German avant-gardist, film experimenter, painter and graphic artist, with 6 of his short experimental films, some specially screened with a new musical underscore. After a short break the surrealist feature film **Dreams That Money Can Buy** will be shown.

The following will be screened:

## Dates:

## Venue:

# [The Screening Room: Clive Holden](/site/en/events/screening-room-clive-holden)

![](https://expcinema.org/site/sites/default/files/styles/frontpage/public/cliveholden2__0.jpg?itok=7gg3KgIK)

**Clive Holden** is a Canadian new media artist, filmmaker and poet. His best-known and publicized project to date is the award-winning "film poem" series *Trains of Winnipeg*, a collection of 14 short films featuring Holden's poetry with musical accompaniment by Christine Fellows, John K. Samson, Jason Tait, Steve Bates and Emily Goodden. Nine of these short films we will screen during Cavia's Screening Room on Sunday the 24th of September.

## Dates:

## Venue:

is an independent website dedicated to provide information and promote **experimental & avant-garde film**.

hits.title: Keep an Eye Filmacademie Festival 2026
hits.snippet:
## Keep an Eye Filmacademie Festival 2026

Wed 24 June to Sun 5 July 2026  
  
The Keep an Eye Filmacademie Festival is the opportunity to discover and experience the new film talent of the Netherlands. The festival offers a unique glimpse into the film world of tomorrow, with screenings, showcases, and exhibitions across three locations.  
  
Almost 100 students from the Bachelor of Film and Master of Film will present their final exam work at Eye Filmmuseum, Het Documentaire Paviljoen as well as at the Netherlands Film Academy.

Amsterdam University of the Arts  
 Jodenbreestraat 3  
 1011 NG Amsterdam, The Netherlands

## Keep an Eye Filmacademie Festival 2026

**Wed 24 June to Sun 5 July 2026**  
  
The Keep an Eye Filmacademie Festival is the opportunity to discover and experience the new film talent of the Netherlands. The festival offers a unique glimpse into the film world of tomorrow, with screenings, showcases, and exhibitions across three locations.  
  
Almost 100 students from the Bachelor of Film and Master of Film will present their final exam work at Eye Filmmuseum, Het Documentaire Paviljoen as well as at the Netherlands Film Academy.

![](/media/nfa/_processed_/c/8/csm_icon-montage_834979a8af.png)

Amsterdam University of the Arts  
 Jodenbreestraat 3  
 1011 NG Amsterdam, The Netherlands

**Essential cookies**: These ensure that the site works properly and give you a smooth experience. They turn on automatically and you cannot turn them off.  
**Analytical cookies**: These collect anonymous data about how you use our website. We use this information to improve the site and your experience. For example, we use Google Analytics.

Discover more about how we protect your privacy in our [cookie statement](/en/about-the-ahk/contact/cookies/).

Essential cookies ensure that the AHK websites function optimally, giving you a smooth and efficient experience. These necessary cookies are automatically enabled to ensure everything works properly, without needing your consent. This way, you can immediately enjoy all the features of our website. It is not possible to deny these cookies.

The AHK uses analytical cookies to anonymously analyze website usage. These cookies provide insights into usage, allowing the AHK to make adjustments for optimal performance. Statistics are collected through web analysis software such as Google Analytics.

hits.title: IDFA: International Documentary Film Festival Amsterdam | I amsterdam
hits.snippet:
IDFA

IDFA returns in 2026. Every November, this international film festival takes place in Amsterdam, transforming the city into a paradise for documentary enthusiasts. It takes place in various locations, from Rembrandtplein to Amsterdam West and Zuidoost. With (inter)national premieres and competitions, films by debutants and students, children's documentaries, a film market, and many international guests.

## IDFA: International Documentary Film Festival Amsterdam

### Dates

### Location

## Largest documentary festival [...] Keep an eye on the IDFA website for more information and ticket sales. Note: The listed showtimes and prices are approximate; check the website for exact times.

## Dates and times

| Date | Time |
 --- |
| Thursday 12 November | Time: 14:30 - 23:00 |
| Friday 13 November | Time: 14:30 - 23:00 |
| Saturday 14 November | Time: 14:30 - 23:00 |

## IDFA: International Documentary Film Festival Amsterdam

### Dates

### Location

### PHIL Haarlem

## Others also read

### IDFA: International Documentary Film Festival Amsterdam [...] ### Dates

### Location

## Largest documentary festival

No other film festival in the world attracts as many high-quality documentaries, film fans, and film professionals from the Netherlands and abroad as IDFA. IDFA is the world's largest documentary film festival, and this year it once again features the biggest and newest (inter)national documentaries.

## Special view of the world

IDFA screens more than 300 international documentaries, each offering a unique perspective on the world. Viewers are transported to both the poorest and richest places on earth, sometimes poetically, sometimes with a powerful statement. The topics are incredibly diverse, but the films share one thing in common: they provoke thought and encourage discussion.

## IDFA: International Documentary Film Festival Amsterdam

### Dates

### Location

## Largest documentary festival

No other film festival in the world attracts as many high-quality documentaries, film fans, and film professionals from the Netherlands and abroad as IDFA. IDFA is the world's largest documentary film festival, and this year it once again features the biggest and newest (inter)national documentaries.

## Special view of the world

IDFA screens more than 300 international documentaries, each offering a unique perspective on the world. Viewers are transported to both the poorest and richest places on earth, sometimes poetically, sometimes with a powerful statement. The topics are incredibly diverse, but the films share one thing in common: they provoke thought and encourage discussion.

Keep an eye on the [IDFA website](https://www.idfa.nl/nl/) for more information and ticket sales. **Note:** **The listed showtimes and prices are approximate; check the website for exact times.**

# Eye International Conference 2026

## The Future of Film Programming

31 May — 1 June 2026

scroll down

![still from Arthème opérateur (Ernest Servaès, FR 1913)](https://assets.eyefilm.nl/images/programme-item/_560x407_crop_center-center_none/still_Arthème-opérateur-1.jpg)
![still from Arthème opérateur (Ernest Servaès, FR 1913)](https://assets.eyefilm.nl/images/programme-item/_560x407_crop_center-center_none/still_Arthème-opérateur-1.jpg)

still *Arthème opérateur* (Ernest Servaès, FR 1913)

Eye International Conference is a meeting point for scholars, archivists, curators, programmers, film heritage professionals, filmmakers, students, artists, and film enthusiasts from across the globe to explore contemporary professional and academic issues affecting the field of audiovisual heritage.

On 31 May through 1 June 2026, Eye Filmmuseum, the University of Amsterdam and Utrecht University will present the 11th Eye International Conference with the theme ‘The Future of Film Programming’.

![still from Arthème opérateur (Ernest Servaès, FR 1913)](https://assets.eyefilm.nl/images/programme-item/_560x407_crop_center-center_none/still_Arthème-opérateur-2.jpg)
![still from Arthème opérateur (Ernest Servaès, FR 1913)](https://assets.eyefilm.nl/images/programme-item/_560x407_crop_center-center_none/still_Arthème-opérateur-2.jpg)

still *Arthème opérateur* (Ernest Servaès, FR 1913)

![still from Arthème opérateur (Ernest Servaès, FR 1913)](https://assets.eyefilm.nl/images/programme-item/_591x444_crop_center-center_none/still_Arthème-opérateur-2.jpg)
![still from Arthème opérateur (Ernest Servaès, FR 1913)](https://assets.eyefilm.nl/images/programme-item/_591x444_crop_center-center_none/still_Arthème-opérateur-2.jpg)

still *Arthème opérateur* (Ernest Servaès, FR 1913)

Marking the conclusion of the [Programmers of the Future](https://www.eyefilm.nl/en/academic/traineeships/programmers-of-the-future), a traineeship at Eye Filmmuseum that has supported the next generation of film programmers, this edition of Eye International Conference will focus on the theme of film programming. We will explore the ways in which film programming, film heritage, and collections connect to respond to changing technological, political and socioeconomic landscapes.

## Call for Proposals

The call for proposals is now closed.   
We are unable to accept late submissions.

The call for proposals is now closed.   
We are unable to accept late submissions.

## Travel Grant Programme

Eye has a travel grant programme for accepted speakers of the Eye International Conference. The grants can be used to partially offset registration and travel costs. You can read more about how to apply in the [Call for Proposals](https://assets.eyefilm.nl/downloads/blocks/Eye-International-Conference-2026-call-for-proposals.pdf).

# Festival entries

Entries for IDFA 2026 are now open. 

For all important information about regulations and deadlines read the overview below. The 39th edition of the festival takes place November 12-22, 2026.

All entries will be reviewed thoroughly and carefully by our international programming team. These experts from around the world join in the discussion that leads up to IDFA’s final selection. The selection process is overseen by IDFA’s Artistic Director.

Before you enter your work, please be sure to read the regulations thoroughly as well as the FAQ for film entries and the FAQ for new media & performance entries. Entering your work into the festival implies that you have read and accepted all entry regulations. [...] | Deadline | Type of entry | Fee |

# Festival entries

Entries for IDFA 2026 are now open. 

For all important information about regulations and deadlines read the overview below. The 39th edition of the festival takes place November 12-22, 2026.

All entries will be reviewed thoroughly and carefully by our international programming team. These experts from around the world join in the discussion that leads up to IDFA’s final selection. The selection process is overseen by IDFA’s Artistic Director.

Before you enter your work, please be sure to read [the regulations](https://professionals.idfa.nl/program/festival-entries/festival-entry-regulations) thoroughly as well as the [FAQ for film entries](https://professionals.idfa.nl/en/program/festival-entries/faq-film-entries) and the [FAQ for new media & performance entries](https://professionals.idfa.nl/en/program/festival-entries/faq-interactive-immersive-project-entries/). Entering your work into the festival implies that you have read and accepted all entry regulations.

| Deadline | Type of entry | Fee |
| --- | --- | --- |
| April 14 | Deadline for Film Fund DocLab Interactive Grant  Early deadline for new media & performance works | * Film Fund DocLab Interactive Grant: [request fee waiver](https://idfa.clickup.com/forms/90151355208/f/2kyq10u8-18715/86UGXVWBATXGR2F2J4) * Student: €25 * Others: €35 |
| April 23 | Early deadline for films | * Student films: €25 * Films under 40 mins: €25 * Films 40 mins or longer: €45 |
| May 28 | Second deadline for films | * Student films: €25 * Films under 40 mins: €40 * Films 40 mins or longer: €65 |
| June 16 | Deadline for new media & performance entries | * Students: €25 * Others: €45 |
| July 9 | Final deadline for films | * Student films: €25 * Films under 40 mins: €50 * Films 40 mins or longer: €85 |

1. Please read the [festival entry regulations](https://professionals.idfa.nl/program/festival-entries/festival-entry-regulations) carefully.  
2. Go to [MyIDFA](https://professionals.idfa.nl/myidfa/submissions/) (in the section ‘My submissions’) to send in the online entry form and pay the entry fee.  
3. For any questions about the entry process, take a look at our [film entries FAQ](https://professionals.idfa.nl/en/program/festival-entries/faq-film-entries) and the [new media & performance FAQ](https://professionals.idfa.nl/en/program/festival-entries/faq-interactive-immersive-project-entries/).  
4. If your question is not answered in the FAQs, get in touch with us.

To complete the entry process, it is required to fill out our online entry form and complete payment of the entry fee. See the table below for an overview of all deadlines and corresponding entry fees.  
  
If the main country (or countries) of production is on the [IBF country list](https://cdn2.assets-servd.host/idfa-cms/production/uploads/Professionals-Training-and-Funding/IBF-Classic-Country-List-2026.pdf?dm=1767799815), your entry is eligible for a fee waiver based on IDFA's discretion. In this case, we encourage you to fill out a [fee waiver request form](https://idfa.clickup.com/forms/90151355208/f/2kyq10u8-18715/86UGXVWBATXGR2F2J4).

Please read the [festival entry regulations](https://professionals.idfa.nl/program/festival-entries/festival-entry-regulations) carefully before sending in your film.

Check out our frequently asked questions about [film entries](https://professionals.idfa.nl/en/program/festival-entries/faq-film-entries).

Check out our frequently asked questions about [new media & performance entries](https://professionals.idfa.nl/en/program/festival-entries/faq-interactive-immersive-project-entries/).

If you require assistance entering your film because you are a filmmaker with a disability, or you experience language barriers in the entry process, please get in touch with us:

Phone: +31 (0)20 6273329  
 Email: [program@idfa.nl](mailto:program@idfa.nl)

To all filmmakers: If your film is selected, we encourage you to provide more accessible film formats when possible. Your contact at the program department will get in touch about whether you have a version available with English subtitles, subtitles for the deaf and hard of hearing, and/or audio description. For more information, please refer to the guidelines for [subtitles for the deaf and hard of hearing](https://subcatch.nl/upload/subcatch_richtlijnen_en_aanlevering_ods_savt_20240901.pdf) (in Dutch) and [audio description](https://www.earcatch.nl/upload/earcatch_guidelines_and_delivery_ad_20240901.pdf) from the Audiovisual Accessibility Foundation (SAVT).

IDFA is the leading festival for the art of documentary film and new media. Every November, the festival presents a trend-setting program of approximately 250 selected titles in five competitions, several thematic sections, a physical exhibition, and a range of live performances. The eleven-day event annually welcomes some 295,000 visits, 3,000 professionals, and 40,000 students to Amsterdam, making it the leading documentary film festival in the world.

International Documentary Film Festival Amsterdam

Vondelpark 3, 1071 AA Amsterdam, The Netherlands

Phone: +31 (0)20 6273329   
E-mail (film entries): [program@idfa.nl](mailto:program@idfa.nl)   
E-mail (new media and performance entries): [doclab@idfa.nl](mailto:doclab@idfa.nl)  
Website: [www.idfa.nl](http://www.idfa.nl/)  
VAT number: NL826332572B01

If you have a question about the regulations, please see our [film entries FAQ](https://professionals.idfa.nl/en/program/festival-entries/faq-film-entries/) and [new media & performance entries FAQ](https://professionals.idfa.nl/en/program/festival-entries/faq-interactive-immersive-project-entries/).

![]()

# **Sprouts Film Festival**

## ecocinema & debut films

  

## For five editions now, the team behind Sprouts has developed a recurring platform for fictional ecocinema and socially critical debut films. We have steadily grown into an interdisciplinary film festival, now present across six venues in three of Amsterdam’s city districts: Studio/K, Kriterion, De Uitkijk, Droog, MidWest, and QRU. While a five-year anniversary calls for celebration, each day we are reminded of why we felt the need for a festival like Sprouts in the first place. Autocrats, tech bros, and far-right factions continue to wreak havoc on our planet, disrupting ecosystems and undermining international law and human dignity. This is why we cherish the brave filmmakers of our 2026 edition so deeply: they defy systems to share bold, uncompromising visions. We also support the shaping of a new generation of green storytellers by hosting our very first Sprouts Eco Shorts Pitch and Industry Day. From 1 - 7 June, let us focus on bringing people together and inspiring one another to resist. Whether by visiting our free Eco Expo, joining our community dinners, attending outdoor screenings, supporting our fundraiser for Sudan, or simply watching a film together.

## Follow us on ︎ [@sproutsfilmfestival](https://www.instagram.com/sproutsfilmfestival/)  or sign up to our [newsletter.](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSd7fs8AgXuAbOkTJvE0CzHOMqlqfCHH9OUPcYV_qUAT3RgKEw/viewform)

![]()  
  
   

![]()

## About the festival

  
 Everyday our lives are flooded by crises, not least of all a seemingly ever-growing climate crisis. It is therefore no surprise that Dutch society has seen a proliferation of protests. Sprouts wants to make the cinema a site of protest by addressing these crises at hand through fiction film, because the power of the imagination can help us battle polarization and foresee effective ways of living together harmoniously with one another and the more-than-human world.  
  
As a festival we strive towards a greener and more diverse alternative to the regular films on offer in Amsterdam. Cinema can make tangible how our current global extinction crisis is never isolated. Battling racism and xenofobia, fighting for LGBTIQ+ and women’s rights, decolonizing Europe, adressing mental health, these issues and more all intersect and find a voice at Sprouts.    
  
French philosopher Gaston Bachelard wrote of our imagination as being botanical. Like a rooted tree that can connect the sky and the earth, the real and the dreamed. At Sprouts we could not agree more. Fiction makes up our festival DNA. That many of our selected eco films are also first works attests to the importance felt by a new generation of filmmakers to better take care of what we have. Sprouts wishes to nurture these brave trailblazers. We hope that you too will give them your full support.  
  
Throughout the year Sprouts is dedicated to offer more. This website functions as a running archive voor fictional ecocinema in order to broaden the category, because there is more than The Day After Tomorrow (2004). Now we need it more than ever.

# [Archive](Archive-back-page)

Discovering the right platform to showcase a ***[Film](https://www.wfcn.co/films)*** can shape its journey from creation to global recognition. As the world of cinema continues to expand, 2026 brings an exciting lineup of [***Film Festivals 2026***](https://www.wfcn.co/film-festivals) that champion fresh voices, bold storytelling, and diverse artistic visions. # CinemAsia Film Festival

The 19th edition of the CinemAsia Film Festival returns to the city in early April 2027. Please note: The dates and times mentioned for 2027 are subject to change.

## CinemAsia Film Festival

### Dates

### Location

## A fresh look at CinemAsia's legacy

In a world where visible and invisible borders are becoming increasingly tangible, this edition of CinemAsia focuses on the theme of commons: shared ground, shared history, and shared humanity. Using cinema as a meeting place, the festival brings together perspectives from different cultures, generations, and life experiences. [...] ### Junior Grachtenfestival

Disclaimer [...] Featuring films from China, Hong Kong, Taiwan, Japan, Korea, India, Indonesia, Singapore, Thailand, the Philippines, Vietnam, and more, CinemAsia is one of the largest Asian film festivals in Europe and serves as a creative platform for Asian representation and filmmaking. From blockbusters and inspiring documentaries to independent productions.

## At various locations in the city

Over 40 films from Asia and the diaspora, CinemAsia Film Festival presents five days of surprising, moving, and challenging films by emerging talents and iconic filmmakers at Eye Filmmuseum, Studio/K, and Rialto De Pijp.

# CinemAsia Film Festival

![](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fapp.thefeedfactory.nl%2Fapi%2Fassets%2F698dae6d02af92434daf0461%2FCinemAsia_2026_Landscape_1920x850_00164.webp&w=3840&q=75)

The 19th edition of the CinemAsia Film Festival returns to the city in early April 2027. Please note: The dates and times mentioned for 2027 are subject to change.

## CinemAsia Film Festival

### Dates

### Location

## A fresh look at CinemAsia's legacy

In a world where visible and invisible borders are becoming increasingly tangible, this edition of CinemAsia focuses on the theme of commons: shared ground, shared history, and shared humanity. Using cinema as a meeting place, the festival brings together perspectives from different cultures, generations, and life experiences.

Featuring films from China, Hong Kong, Taiwan, Japan, Korea, India, Indonesia, Singapore, Thailand, the Philippines, Vietnam, and more, CinemAsia is one of the largest Asian film festivals in Europe and serves as a creative platform for Asian representation and filmmaking. From blockbusters and inspiring documentaries to independent productions.

## At various locations in the city

Over 40 films from Asia and the diaspora, CinemAsia Film Festival presents five days of surprising, moving, and challenging films by emerging talents and iconic filmmakers at Eye Filmmuseum, Studio/K, and Rialto De Pijp.

CinemAsia offers unique perspectives and cultural depth, creating a platform for dialogue, exchange, and meaningful encounters. Since its founding in 2003, CinemAsia has become one of Europe's leading Asian film festivals.

Visit the CinemAsia [website](https://cinemasia.nl/en/) for more information and the complete program.

# Jeffrey Babcock over alternatieve filmcultuur

## De geest van de jaren zeventig

![](https://filmkrant.nl/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/babcock-1000x563.jpg)

In the Shadow of the Blue Rascal Cinema, kraakpand De Slang, Amsterdam 2012

Jeffrey Babcocks filmvertoningen in Amster­damse kraakpanden bieden een radicaal alter­natief voor de extravaganties van Holly­wood en het brede aanbod in de film­theaters. Met zijn expliciete afwijzing van de commer­ciële filmcultuur vecht hij voor een andere visie op de culturele rol van cinema.

Door Nicola Romagnoli

Al ruim een decennium organiseert Jeffrey Babcock filmvertoningen op diverse underground-locaties in Amsterdam, van een kerker onder de Torensluisbrug tot Filmhuis Cavia en van krakersbolwerk Joe’s Garage tot rockpodium De Nieuwe Anita, gevestigd in een voormalig kraakpand en trouw aan die geschiedenis. De van origine Amerikaanse filmmaker emigreerde in de jaren tachtig naar Nederland. Met zijn ‘nomadische cinema’ wil hij de vergeten politieke en sociale mogelijkheden van de cinema nieuw leven inblazen, in de geest van de tegencultuur die hij ooit in de stad aantrof.  
Babcock begon zijn filmavonden uit een groeiende onvrede met het filmaanbod in zijn stad, dat steeds meer in de greep van een commerciële logica kwam, zeker na het wegvallen van het Desmet Filmtheater in 2000. "Het voelde alsof we de cinema weer moesten verdedigen. De industrie is zo kleindenkend geworden in wat cinema kan zijn: linksom of rechtsom, het moet entertainment zijn. Aangezien de officiële vertoningsplekken ten onder gingen aan dat commerciële denken, ben ik begonnen alternatieve locaties op te starten, een heel ander netwerk."

Heel erg gratis  
Doordat hij zijn filmavonden organiseert in het alternatieve circuit, kan Babcock de clandestiene vertoningen goedkoop houden. "Als mensen een film komen kijken waar ze vooraf helemaal niets van afweten, nemen ze een risico. Dus ik wil dat mijn vertoningen gratis zijn, of anders zo goedkoop mogelijk. Ik begon tien jaar geleden op de Leidsestraat met elke zondag een gratis avond: gratis films, gratis eten, een gratis winkel — heel erg gratis. Daardoor geef je mensen de mogelijkheid dat risico te nemen, dat is mijn strategie."  
Het enige wat Babcocks programmering begrenst, is zijn eigen blikveld. "Elke film die ik vertoon, moet iets hebben dat hem uniek maakt, iets dat je niet eerder hebt gezien. Het maakt me niet uit of het een cultfilm is, een horrorfilm, met een groot of klein budget — als het maar geen formulewerk is." Dat betekent overigens niet dat Babcocks voorstellingen alleen gericht zijn op een groepje fanatici. "Ik wil vooral het idee tegenspreken dat Hollywood de enige manier is om jezelf  te vermaken. Er is zo veel meer: ook als je gewoon entertainment wil, kun je zonder blockbusters."

Ongemakkelijke stoelen  
Van experimentele Tsjechische komedies tot psychedelische misdaadfilms: Babcocks programmering beslaat de volledige breedte van wat film vermag. "Elke film heeft zijn eigen wereld", vindt Babcock, "Dus laten we naar gekraakte ruimtes als Joe’s Garage gaan, wat ongemakkelijke stoelen neerzetten en films draaien van Alain Tanner, een compleet vergeten Zwitserse regisseur uit de jaren zeventig, of de Japanse maker Terayama. Het soort films dat EYE zou moeten draaien, maar wat ze nooit zullen doen. Laten we ze dan in die kraakpanden draaien, de plekken waar het niet om geld draait, en daar onze cultuur omheen bouwen."  
Babcocks pogingen de filmische tegencultuur in leven te houden vindt enthousiaste medestanders in de krakersbolwerken van de stad, die zelf evenzeer onder vuur liggen. De krakersscene van de jaren zeventig en tachtig bepaalde het gezicht van het Amsterdamse culturele aanbod, een invloed die voortduurt tot de dag van vandaag, stelt Babcock. "Het was een heel vrije en diverse stad. Er was ruimte voor kleine autonome gebieden. Zonder die krakersinvloeden zou de stad nu een stuk rechtser, conservatiever en strikter zijn."  
Maar tegenwoordig lopen commerciële concertzalen als De Melkweg en Paradiso, die zijn ontstaan dankzij de inspanningen van de krakerswereld, liever niet meer te koop met dat verleden. Door de gemeenschappelijke en anti-commerciële ideologie van krakersbolwerken als Joe’s Garage en Het Spinhuis te omarmen, plaatst Babcock zijn filmvertoningen in een groter sociaal-politiek kader. "De hele stad wordt gladgestreken, gecommercialiseerd, geprivatiseerd en vermerkt. Maar mijn cinema’s komen voort uit de geest die in de jaren zeventig door de stad waarde. Die probeer ik in leven te houden."

Urgentie  
Babcocks voorstellingen hebben een intense intimiteit en urgentie, die in commerciële theaters per definitie ontbreekt. "Daar wordt het publiek als vee naar binnen gestuwd", beklaagt Babcock zich. "Ze krijgen als eerste de reclames voorgeschoteld, en na de voorstelling worden ze zo snel mogelijk weer afgevoerd zodat de volgende meute naar binnen kan."  
Babcock pakt het anders aan: hij begint zijn vertoningen met een monoloog, waarin hij het politieke, sociale, esthetische en historische belang van de film uiteenzet. Het opent de dialoog met het publiek, die na de film nadrukkelijk wordt voortgezet. "Ik wil dat mensen na afloop blijven praten, over de film en over de wereld. Dat is een belangrijk element van mijn cinema’s." Juist de collectivistische sfeer in de krakersscene voedt deze discussies, en nodigt ertoe uit om nog urenlang te blijven praten. Of je er nu voor het eerst bent, of een vaste bezoeker: Babcocks schier eindeloze politieke en esthetische passie voor cinema werkt aanstekelijk, en wakkert een verlangen aan dat de politieke kaalslag en cinematografische leegte van de multiplexen ver achter zich laat.  
Het succes van de vertoningen toont aan dat er een behoefte bestaat aan iets dat de conventies van de mainstream overstijgt. Babcock blijft vechten voor een alternatieve invulling voor wat film kijken en film vertonen kan betekenen. "Ik ben niet alleen geïnteresseerd in alternatieve films. Het moet samengaan met alternatieve bioscopen, en alternatieve distributie. Het moet in elkaar grijpen; het een bestaat niet zonder het ander."

In the Shadow of the Blue Rascal Cinema, kraakpand De Slang, Amsterdam 2012

### Meld u aan voor de nieuwsbrief

Elke week alles over de nieuwste films en het filmnieuws in uw mailbox? Meld u nu aan voor de Filmkrant Nieuwsbrief.

![Filmkrant](https://filmkrant.nl/wp-content/themes/filmkrant/assets/images/logo.png)

hits.title: Filmhuis Amsterdam | Unieke Arthouse Films | Rialto
hits.snippet:
### Wat maakt dit filmhuis anders dan een gewone bioscoop?

Bij een filmhuis draait het om curatie en context. We selecteren films op artistieke waarde en maatschappelijke relevantie, bieden verdieping met specials en gesprekken, en creëren een plek waar je films ziet die je niet snel in het reguliere aanbod vindt. De beleving is kleinschalig, persoonlijk en inhoudelijk.

### Welke soorten films vertoont Rialto, het filmhuis van Amsterdam?

We vertonen arthouse films, niet-Westerse cinema, Nederlandse producties, documentaires, klassiekers en themaprogramma’s. Je vindt bij ons zowel nieuwe releases als heruitgaven en festivalhoogtepunten, regelmatig aangevuld met inleidingen of Q&A’s.

### Zijn er ook premières bij het filmhuis in Amsterdam? [...] ## Programmering: van premières tot specials

Elke week presenteren we een gevarieerde selectie films en specials. Je vindt bij ons voorpremières, reprises van publiekslievelingen, themareeksen en een vaste stroom aan programma’s met gasten, Q&A’s en talks. We werken geregeld samen met festivals en partners, waardoor je bij ons niet alleen films ziet, maar ook makers ontmoet en actuele thema’s verkent. Onze zalen zijn ingericht voor optimale beleving: goed beeld en geluid, comfortabele stoelen en een intieme sfeer die past bij een filmhuis.

Benieuwd wat er vandaag en de komende weken draait? Bekijk de filmhuisagenda Amsterdam of blader door alle actuele titels via Films Amsterdam.

## Locaties in Amsterdam [...] ## Voor iedere filmliefhebber

Of je nu graag moderne indie-films ontdekt, een liefde hebt voor de nouvelle vague, of je laat verrassen door onconventionele titels: ons filmhuis is er voor jou. We programmeren breed en inclusief, met aandacht voor makers uit verschillende regio’s en filmculturen, en we bieden regelmatig films with English subtitles. Voor de jongste kijkers is er bovendien een eigen aanbod met toegankelijke titels en aanvullend programma.

Plan je bezoek met kinderen via Kinderfilms Amsterdam. Wil je weten wat er nog meer te beleven is? Start bij Bioscoop Amsterdam voor een overzicht van onze functies, sfeer en voorzieningen.

## Context, gesprek en community
hits.content:
Vanwege Koningsdag zijn we op maandag 27 april de gehele dag gesloten.

# Filmhuis Amsterdam

Unieke Arthouse Films in Rialto



Rialto is jouw filmhuis in Amsterdam voor bijzondere cinema: van bekroonde arthouse tot niet-Westerse parels, van documentaires tot klassiekers op het grote doek. In onze zalen in De Pijp en op de VU-campus programmeert ons team een eigenzinnige selectie voor nieuwsgierige filmliefhebbers. We laten je kennismaken met makers van nu en toen, openen deuren naar andere perspectieven en maken ruimte voor verdieping met specials, inleidingen en nagesprekken.

## Wat je van ons filmhuis mag verwachten

In ons filmtheater staat de film centraal, maar de ervaring gaat verder dan de vertoning. We bieden een zorgvuldig samengesteld filmprogramma met premières, festivalfavorieten en *previously unreleased* titels die je elders zelden ziet. Denk aan een mix van arthouse films, Nederlandse film, internationale klassiekers, hedendaagse documentaires en themaprogramma’s. Onze focus ligt op kwaliteit, context en ontdekking: een plek waar je nieuwe stemmen vindt en vertrouwde namen opnieuw kunt bekijken.

## Programmering: van premières tot specials

Elke week presenteren we een gevarieerde selectie films en specials. Je vindt bij ons voorpremières, reprises van publiekslievelingen, themareeksen en een vaste stroom aan programma’s met gasten, Q&A’s en talks. We werken geregeld samen met festivals en partners, waardoor je bij ons niet alleen films ziet, maar ook makers ontmoet en actuele thema’s verkent. Onze zalen zijn ingericht voor optimale beleving: goed beeld en geluid, comfortabele stoelen en een intieme sfeer die past bij een filmhuis.

Benieuwd wat er vandaag en de komende weken draait? Bekijk de [filmhuisagenda Amsterdam](filmhuis-amsterdam-agenda) of blader door alle actuele titels via [Films Amsterdam](nl/films).

## Locaties in Amsterdam

Rialto programmeert op twee locaties in de stad: het historische filmhuis Rialto De Pijp en onze locatie Rialto VU aan de Boelelaan. Beide locaties ademen de sfeer van een filmhuis en bieden ruimte voor specials, evenementen en educatie. Meer informatie over routes, zalen en voorzieningen vind je via [Locaties Rialto de VU en de Pijp](nl/locaties/).

## Voor iedere filmliefhebber

Of je nu graag moderne indie-films ontdekt, een liefde hebt voor de *nouvelle vague*, of je laat verrassen door onconventionele titels: ons filmhuis is er voor jou. We programmeren breed en inclusief, met aandacht voor makers uit verschillende regio’s en filmculturen, en we bieden regelmatig films *with English subtitles*. Voor de jongste kijkers is er bovendien een eigen aanbod met toegankelijke titels en aanvullend programma.

Plan je bezoek met kinderen via [Kinderfilms Amsterdam](nl/specials/2/kinderfilms-in-rialto). Wil je weten wat er nog meer te beleven is? Start bij [Bioscoop Amsterdam](nl/bioscoop-amsterdam) voor een overzicht van onze functies, sfeer en voorzieningen.

## Context, gesprek en community

Een filmhuis is meer dan een bioscoop. We organiseren specials, nagesprekken met filmmakers, themablokken rond iconen als Akira Kurosawa of hedendaagse trailblazers, en programma’s waarin we ruimte bieden aan nieuwe stemmen. Met inleidingen, randprogramma en ontmoetingen met makers verdiepen we de kijkervaring en brengen we filmliefhebbers met elkaar in contact. Voor en na de film is er tijd voor gesprek in onze foyer en het café.

## Praktisch: tickets en bezoek

Tickets zijn online verkrijgbaar via onze agenda en via de kassa op locatie (indien beschikbaar). Je vindt alle actuele tijden in de [Bioscoopagenda Amsterdam](nl/agenda). In de filmomschrijving zie je of een titel Engels gesproken is of Nederlandse/Engelse ondertiteling heeft. Heb je een vraag over toegankelijkheid, taal of randprogramma? Neem contact met ons op, we denken graag mee.

## Veelgestelde vragen

### Wat maakt dit filmhuis anders dan een gewone bioscoop?

Bij een filmhuis draait het om curatie en context. We selecteren films op artistieke waarde en maatschappelijke relevantie, bieden verdieping met specials en gesprekken, en creëren een plek waar je films ziet die je niet snel in het reguliere aanbod vindt. De beleving is kleinschalig, persoonlijk en inhoudelijk.

### Welke soorten films vertoont Rialto, het filmhuis van Amsterdam?

We vertonen arthouse films, niet-Westerse cinema, Nederlandse producties, documentaires, klassiekers en themaprogramma’s. Je vindt bij ons zowel nieuwe releases als heruitgaven en festivalhoogtepunten, regelmatig aangevuld met inleidingen of Q&A’s.

### Zijn er ook premières bij het filmhuis in Amsterdam?

Ja. Wekelijks programmeren we premières en voorpremières, vaak met extra context of randprogramma. Het actuele overzicht en ticketverkoop staan in de [Bioscoopagenda Amsterdam](nl/agenda).

## Ontdek het complete aanbod

Klaar om je volgende film te kiezen? Start bij [Films Amsterdam](nl/films) voor het volledige overzicht, bekijk wat er vandaag en morgen draait via de [Bioscoopagenda Amsterdam](nl/agenda) en plan je bezoek aan onze zalen via [Locaties Rialto de VU en de Pijp](nl/locaties/).

# JAN magazine: dit zijn de leukste kleine bioscopen van Amsterdam

"We kunnen Netflixen, films downloaden en tv-zappen tot we een ons wegen, maar een avondje bioscoop op z'n tijd is toch wel alles-overtreffend. Misschien niet in die standaard Pathé, maar wél in zo'n intiem gezellig filmhuis. Gelukkig zit Amsterdam vol met gezellige alternatieve bioscoopjes en filmhuizen. Niet alleen voor de échte filmliefhebbers, maar ook gewoon voor diegenen die zin hebben in een avondje uit. JAN zet de leukste kleine bioscopen in Amsterdam op een rij."

hits.title: 13 Film Houses in Amsterdam // Your Little Black Book
hits.snippet:
### Tuschinski

Oké, oké, we geven het toe. Tuschinski is geen echt filmhuis, maar stiekem een Pathé-bioscoop. Toch voelt het alsof je in een prachtig filmhuis terecht bent gekomen. In deze bioscoop in art–deco stijl in het centrum van Amsterdam zie je blockbusters, maar ook af en toe een PAC-film (Pathé Alternative Cinema). Als je de mogelijkheid hebt om naar zaal 1 te gaan, altijd doen! Boek dan de zogenaamde love seats voor een heuse VIP treatment. Hier zit een drankje en snack bij inbegrepen, en je hebt de beste stoelen in the house!  
Reguliersbreestraat 26, Amsterdam – pathe.nl

pathe tuschinski

© Pathe TUSCHINSKI

# 13 cinemas in Amsterdam for the perfect movie date

##### YLBB Notes

## Ga naar de movies bij deze filmhuizen in Amsterdam

We kunnen Netflixen, films downloaden en tv kijken wat we willen. Maar: een avondje bioscoop blijft toch wel het aller-allerleukste. Wat is er nu lekkerder dan achterover zitten in zo’n comfortabele bioscoopstoel, om vervolgens de nieuwste film te zien? Gelukkig voor de filmliefhebbers onder ons zit Amsterdam vol met te gekke bioscoopjes en filmhuizen. Van de commerciëlere plekken als Pathé tot aan mini-filmhuizen. Wat vinden wij nu de leukste plekken om een film te kijken in Amsterdam? Dat lees je in deze lijst met deleukste filmhuizen in Amsterdam. [...] ### De Filmhallen

De Filmhallen in Amsterdam West is een super goede toevoeging aan het filmaanbod van Amsterdam. Het is van dezelfde eigenaren als The Movies. Ga eerst even langs de Foodhallen of De Kanarieclub voor een stevige bodem. Daarna rol je direct door naar De Filmhallen. Als je zin hebt in een blockbuster, ga je gang! Maar ook als je meer in de stemming bent voor een arthouse film, ben je bij De Filmhallen Amsterdam ook aan het goede adres.  
Hannie Dankbaarpassage 12, Amsterdam – filmhallen.nl

Nét terug uit Catalonië en ik dacht: ik ga direct wat tips delen.  
  
Tijdens onze roadtrip bleven we twee nachtjes in de Ebrodelta. Een ontzettend leuke stop voor families én foodies, waar van alles te doen is.  
  
· Een boottochtje naar het Musclarium is een must! Hier eet je oesters en mosselen zo van de farm, midden op zee.  
  
 · Bewonder de flamingo’s en zwarte ibissen die hier het hele jaar door te vinden zijn.  
  
 · In de Ebrodelta wordt het hele jaar door rijst verbouwd. Voor paella, maar Kensho maakt er ook sake en miso van! Je kunt hen bezoeken tijdens een tasting.  
  
 · Fietsen langs de rijstvelden, kanoën op de Ebro-rivier en naar verschillende stranden. Het was nu nog net te koud, maar er is van alles te doen.  
  
 · De stadjes zijn heel low key en niet zo spannend, maar je kunt er wel fantastisch lekker lokaal eten. Van het door Michelin bekroonde Les Moles\* tot lokale eethuisjes als Vermuteria Bonmarisc en Lo Tinglado. We aten heel veel vis, maar ook fritters van anemoon (een first!).  
  
Nu back to work en ga ik een uitgebreide travel guide maken van deze roadtrip door Catalonië, maar deze tips wil je alvast bewaren voor een volgend avontuur.  
  
✖️✖️✖️  
🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck   
  
{ In deze post deel ik tips voor de Ebrodelta in Spanje (Delta de l’Ebre). Meer tips voor Catalunya zijn te vinden op www.yourlittleblackbook.me }

Een kookschrift van oma. Drie Michelinsterren. En een prachtige jaren ’70-villa in een rustige wijk in Bangkok, waar je wordt verwelkomd alsof je bij de chefs thuis bent.  
  
De Sühring brothers koken niet vanuit trends, ze koken vanuit herinneringen. Aan de boerderij van hun grootmoeder, aan de smaken van hun jeugd in Duitsland en aan technieken die generaties lang zijn doorgegeven.  
  
@relaischateaux nodigde mij uit om Sühring tijdens een sololunch te ervaren en dit is toch wel mijn ultieme definitie van culinaire self-care.  
  
Dit was een verrassende middag in Bangkok, waar ik het beste Duitse eten at dat ik ooit heb geproefd. En dat in een stad waar ik normaal gesproken de Europese restaurants juist mijd. Nou, voor @restaurant\_suhring wil je écht die uitzondering maken.  
  
Signature dishes waar ik nog regelmatig aan terugdenk?  
🍴 Labskaus met kaviaar  
🍴 Enleta wafeltje met Pfalz vinegar  
🍴 Wildpastete  
🍴 Dry aged Duck  
  
TIP: reserveer als je naar Sühring gaat de seats bij de keukenbar, zodat je uitzicht hebt op de chefs die aan het werk zijn!  
  
Volg @yourlbb voor meer tips waarop je kunt vertrouwen.  
  
✖️✖️✖️  
🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck

Dit is een van de leukste gratis uitjes in de lente! Wist je dat je in Nederland op verschillende plekken zelf oesters kunt rapen (of plukken, voor de kenners 😉)? Dit weekend ben ik even in Zeeland en als het lukt gaan we weer.  
  
📍In deze video laat ik zien waar je je emmer kunt vullen.   
  
Comment oester voor de uitgebreide guide met alle details over alle 8 locaties + handige tips en regels.  
  
Belangrijk om te weten:  
 – Max. 10 kilo per persoon (≈ 1 emmer)  
 – Alleen voor eigen gebruik  
 – Niet scheppen of schrapen in de bodem  
 – Ga bij laag water  
  
Handig om mee te nemen:  
Emmer, schroevendraaier, laarzen, werkhandschoenen, theedoek, schone kleren en vuilniszakken (voor de oesters én vieze kleding).  
  
En of course: neem ook een oestermes, citroen en champagne mee 🍋🥂Want je wilt toch het liefst direct iets proeven!   
  
Het oesterseizoen loopt van september tot ongeveer april / begin mei, dus nu het water niet niet zo koud is, is dit het perfecte uitje.   
  
In de full guide deel ik precies waar je ze makkelijk kunt vinden, zonder veel moeite. Comment oester en ik stuur hem je direct!  
  
Met wie wil jij binnenkort oesters rapen?   
  
✖️✖️✖️  
 🖊️ @anne\_de\_buck
